subclass.name: Sun Priest
subclassFeatures: Channel Divinity: Summon the Stars|Cleric|sns|Sun Priest|sns|9

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Druidic Training
entries: All druids are taught to commune with nature, to become a conduit for natural power, and to maintain the secrets of their druid circle. Also at 1st level, you gain proficiency with {@skill Nature}, and you learn the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip (this does not count against your total cantrips known).

You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak the language and use it to leave hidden messages. You and others who know this language automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful DC 15 {@skill Perception} check but can't decipher it without magic.

classFeature.name: Secrets of Nature
entries: During your druidic training and exploration of nature you pick up several methods of surviving in the wilderness. At 2nd level you gain two {@filter secrets of nature|optionalfeatures|feature type=sn} of your choice. The Secrets Known column of the Druid table shows when you learn more secrets of nature.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter druid spell list|spells|level=0|class=druid (sns)}. You learn additional druid cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Druid table.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Druid table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your {@filter druid spells|spells|class=druid (sns)} of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these druid spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of druid spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the druid spell list. When you do so, choose a number of druid spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your druid level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you are a 3rd-level druid, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Wisdom of 16, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell cure wounds}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
You can also change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your druid spells, since your magic draws upon your devotion and attunement to nature. You use your Wisdom whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a druidic focus as a spellcasting focus for your druid spells.

classFeature.name: Nature's Bond
entries: At 2nd level, nature provides you with the ability to summon its minor spirits. As an action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to cast the {@spell find familiar} spell, without material components.
The familiar you summon is a fey instead of a beast, and it disappears after a number of hours equal to half your druid level.

classFeature.name: Wild Shape
entries: Also at 1st level, as an action you can magically shapeshift into one of three types of the {@creature Animal Form} statblock: Land, Sea, and Sky.

As you assume a form, you determine its appearance, selecting an animal that matches the form's type. You can alternatively choose a hybrid form that incorporates visual characteristics from two or more animals. The appearance you choose has no effect on the form's capabilities.

When choosing an animal's appearance for a form, that animal needn't be the same size as the form. For example, you could choose elephant for the apprance and Small for the size - creating a form that resembles a little elephant.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Whenever you use your Wild Shapes feature to shapeshift, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to triple your proficiency bonus.

You can stay in a wild shape for a number of hours equal to your druid level. You then revert to your normal form unless you expend another use of this feature. You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a bonus action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall {@condition unconscious}, drop to 0 hit points, or die.
While you are shapeshifted, the following rules apply:

items: Your game statistics are replaced by the statistics of the form, except you retain your hit points and Hit Dice, personality, the ability to speak and understand any languages you know, your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, your non-armour defences, and your skill proficiencies.
While in a wild shape your Strength and Dexterity are equal to your Wisdom score, and your armour is equal to half your Wisdom modifier.

While in a wild shape you may only cast spells with a range of Self or Touch. You can perform the verbal and somatic components of such spells while in a wild shape and your wild shape itself acts as your spellcasting focus. Shapeshifting doesn't break your concentration on a spell you've already cast, nor does it prevent you from taking actions that are part of a spell, such as {@spell call lightning}, that you've already cast.
You still retain the benefit of any feature from your class and can use them if your wild shape is physically capable of doing so.
Any equipment you are wearing or wielding merges into your new form and retains its effects as if you were still wearing it.

classFeature.name: Natural Demeanor
entries: At 3rd level your connection to the natural world comes forth and becomes omnipresent. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: A Way with Animals
entries: Beasts understand anything you verbally communicate in Druidic, and you can comprehend them in return. The knowledge and awareness of many beasts is limited by their intelligence, but at minimum beasts can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever they have perceived within the past day. At the DM's discretion, you might be able to persuade a beast to perform a small favour for you. In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Nature} checks.

entries.name: First-Hand Naturalist
entries: You've learned the intricacies of nature through fisthand experience rather than academic study.
You gain an expertise die on {@skill Nature} checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Wisdom when making {@skill Nature} checks.

entries.name: Leyline Awareness
entries: Druidic rituals attune you to the leylines that course through the Material Plane. While you are on the Material Plane, you always know the following information:

items: Which way is north.
The direction of the nearest forest.
The direction of the nearest natural body of water.
The direction of the nearest city or other humanoid community.
The general health of the local ecosystem, and if anything unnatural or magical is upsetting the natural order.

entries.name: Scholar of Old Ways
entries: Your circle taught the old magic at the root of all things, granting you eldritch insights beyond mundane arcane studies.
You gain proficiency with Arcana, or if you already have proficiency you instead gain an expertise die on Arcana checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Wisdom when making Arcana checks.

classFeature.name: Druidic Lore
entries: At 6th level, your knowledge of the natural world grows. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Druidic Secrets
entries: You're adept at covertly performing druidic magic without attracting attention. When you cast a druid spell you may choose to do so utilising silent and subtle Druidic motions and signs in place of both the spell's somatic and verbal components, gestures that creatures which can speak Druidic are aware of. Once you cast a druid spell in this way, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Toxin Intuition
entries: You are a veteran of nature's chemical warefare. You automatically know if any flora or fauna is poisonous or venomous. In addition, you gain advantage on checks made to determine if a foodstuff is poisonous and non-armour attacks that deal poison damage or cause the {@condition poisoned} condition have disadvantage against you.

entries.name: Truthspeaker
entries: You've learned to tap into the fundamental truth that connects all things, at least briefly.
You may cast the spell {@spell legend lore} without expending a spell slot and without material components. Once you cast the spell in this way, you cannot do so again for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: With 10 minutes work you can scavenge valuable cuts from the corpse of a beast, dragon, monstrosity, or plant that has a CR of 1 or higher and has died within the last hour. If sold to a collector, naturalist, or other druid, this notable component is worth an amount of gold equal to the creature's CR.

classFeature.name: Ferocity or Serenity
entries: At 9th level you choose to embrace either the wild savagery of nature or the control you need to protect it. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Embraced Ferocity
entries: You gain a +2 bonus on armour attack rolls you make while in a Wild Shape. In addition, whenever you use Wild Shape to shapeshift, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to five times your proficiency bonus instead of three times.

entries.name: Embraced Serenity
entries: You gain +1 to non-armour defences while concentrating on a spell. In addition, as a bonus action you may sacrifice a use of Wild Shape to recover an expended spell slot of 3rd-level or lower, or two uses of your Wild Shape feature to recover an expended spell slot of 6th-level or lower.

classFeature.name: Improved Natural Bond
entries: At 10th level your bond has grown and you are able to summon forth far greater nature spirits. As an action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to cast the {@spell conjure animals} spell.

classFeature.name: Harmonise or Tyrannise
entries: At 14th level you choose to harmonise with nature or to harness it. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Harmonise
entries: During a short rest you may choose to meditate and harmonise with your surroundings. If you do, at the end of your rest you attune to the area in a 5-mile radius around you. You magically know what types of creatures are present in your attuned area, their approximate numbers, and if there are any magics disturbing the natural order in your attuned area. While in your attuned area, you have advantage on {@skill Nature}, {@skill Perception}, and {@skill Survival} checks, and blindsight out to a range of 30 feet. Once attuned to an area, you remain attuned for the next 24 hours. You can only attune to one area at a time.

entries.name: Tyrannise
entries: You draw upon nature's power to become a prime example of one of its children. Whenever you assume a wild shape you may choose to assume the apex form of that wild shape. An apex form is always Large and any attack made by an apex form wild shape deals an extra {@dice 1d8} damage (this is multiplied by the Empowered Attack feature). In addition, while in an apex form wild shape you gain an expertise die on saves and Strength and Dexterity checks.

classFeature.name: Greater Natural Bond
entries: At 18th level your bond expands to encompass the Feywild and you are able to summon any woodland beings. As an action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to cast the {@spell conjure woodland beings} spell as if you cast it using a 6th-level spell slot.

classFeature.name: Archdruid
entries: At 20th level, you become an extension of nature. Your Wild Shape has no duration. In addition, when you cast a druid spell you do not need seen, vocalised, or material components. You gain this benefit in both your normal shape and while using Wild Shape.

You also learn to tap into the intricate ebb and flow of life around you and how to divert it as you see fit. You may cast the spells {@spell power word kill} and {@spell mass heal} without expending a spell slot and without any material components. Once you cast either spell in this way, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

Finally, you can turn nature in its rawest elements into deadly weapons no matter your form. Once each turn, you can use a bonus action to choose a target point within 30 feet and one of the following element options. If the element you choose is not present at or near the target point, any harm caused by this feature is halved and creatures have advantage on any save against it.

items.name: Air
entries: Each creature within 20 feet of that point must make a save. On a failure, a target suffers bludgeoning harm equal to {@dice 3d8} + your Wisdom modifier and is flung up to 20 feet away from the target point in a random direction and knocked {@condition prone}. If a thrown target strikes an object, such as a wall or floor, the target suffers {@dice 1d8} bludgeoning harm. If the target is thrown at another creature, that creature must succeed on a save or suffer the same damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. If the save is successful, the target suffers half the bludgeoning harm and isn't flung away or knocked {@condition prone}.
In addition, all ranged armour attacks within 30 feet of the target point have disadvantage until the beginning of your next turn.

items.name: Earth
entries: Clumps of dirt and stone explode outward. Creatures within 10 feet of the target point suffer {@dice 4d8} bludgeoning harm and are knocked {@condition prone}. A creature that makes and passes a save suffers half the harm and is not knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Fire
entries: Flames roil outward in a burst of inferno. Creatures within 15 feet of the target point suffer {@dice 5d6} fire harm.

items.name: Ice
entries: You condense and flash freeze the water from a vast area above you into a massive boulder to drop down with immense force. A creature at least 30 feet below the target point suffers {@dice 4d6} bludgeoning and cold harm.
In addition, the area in a 20-foot radius around the creature becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute.

items.name: Water
entries: You create a 20-foot tall, 20-foot wide wave that travels 30 feet in one direction and then crashes down. Any Large or smaller creatures and objects in the wave's path make a save. On a failure, a creature suffers {@dice 4d8} bludgeoning harm and is carried with the wave.

items.name: Wood
entries: Splinters appear and fling themselves into flesh and armour alike. Creatures within 20 feet of the target point suffer {@dice 3d6} piercing harm.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass.

entries.name: Subclass Spells
entries: With your subclass you gain access to a list of subclass spells at the levels noted in your subclass description. Once you gain a subclass spell it is always prepared and doesn't count against the spells you can prepare each day.
If you gain a subclass spell that is not on the cleric spell list, it is considered cleric spell for you.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Defensive Blessing
entries: Starting at 1st level, the high powers which guide you bestow a modicum of protection for your journey. Choose one of the following options.

entries.name: Armour of Conviction
entries: While you are not wearing any body armour or wielding a shield, your armour is equal to half your Wisdom modifier.
Also, in addition to the spells you normally prepare, you always have {@spell shield of faith} prepared.

entries.name: Sacred Archery
entries: You gain proficiency with light armour, medium armour, and ranged martial weapons.

entries.name: Spirit Soldier
entries: You gain proficiency with light armour, medium armour, and shields.

classFeature.name: Sacred Call
entries: Also at 1st level, your clerical vocation anoints you with certain graces for spreading your message to the people. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Clerical Charisma
entries: Crowds find your public presence irresistable. You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. Whenever you use Performance to deliver a sermon or to sing holy songs in a town or city, even on a failed check other than a natural 1, you still attract a crowd of people no smaller than your standard DC.

entries.name: Ordination
entries: Your mission in the world is supported by the spiritual community or religious hierarchy who called you to religious service. As a formal leader within your religious order, you are expected to perform the ceremonies of your faith, including weddings and funerals. In exchange, members of your faith provide you and your companions with food, lodging, and a modest lifestyle. This also improves their default attitude towards you to friendly.

Additionally, if your congregation or order is able, they send you letters with regular updates about their well-being. They are willing to help connect you with other chapters of your faith through letters of introduction.

entries.name: Zeal of the Convert
entries: You enthusiastically see the hand of a greater entity everywhere. You gain advantage on {@skill Persuasion} checks when you invoke the name of your deity or movement during a conversation with a pious person. If the listener is negatively disposed to your greater entity or movement, you have disadvantage instead. This balances out to not affect your journey activity checks.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: As a conduit for divine power, you can cast cleric spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know three cantrips of your choice from the cleric spell list. You learn additional cleric cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Cleric table.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Cleric table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your {@filter cleric spells|spells|class=cleric (sns)} of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of cleric spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the cleric spell list. When you do so, choose a number of cleric spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you are a 3rd-level cleric, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Wisdom of 16, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell cure wounds}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.

You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of cleric spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your cleric spells. The power of your spells comes from your devotion to your deity. You use your Wisdom whenever a cleric spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item holy symbol} as a spellcasting focus for your cleric spells.

classFeature.name: Channel Divinity (1/rest)
entries: At 2nd level, you are able to channel the energy of your greater entity directly, and use it for a variety of magical effects. You start with one of these effects, Turn Undead, and can gain additional effects from your Cleric Subclasses. Some subclasses provide additional uses of Channel Divinity as your cleric level increases, detailed under the heading of your chosen archetypes.
You may choose which Channel Divinity effect to create each time you use this feature. You must then finish a short or long rest before you're able to Channel Divinity again.
From 6th level you can use your Channel Divinity twice between rests, and beginning at 18th level you can use it three times between rests. Finishing a short or long rest regains your expended uses.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Turn Undead
entries: As an action, you present the holy symbol of your greater entity and speak a divine word, rebuking the undead. Make a will attack against each undead creature within 30 feet of you that can sense you, with the following outcomes:. If the creature fails it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage from any source.

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is {@condition sluggish} when moving towards you for 1 round.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is turned for 1 minute, or until it takes damage from any source. If this effect ends from damage and the target spent at least one of its own turns turned, it immediately takes a turn. This does not affect initiative order.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also vulnerable to all damage while turned.

A turned creature must use its turns trying to move as far away from you as possible. It cannot willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you by moving towards you. It also cannot take reactions. On its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape any effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature can use the Dodge action.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Channel Divinity (2/rest)
entries: From 6th level, you can use your Channel Divinity twice between rests.

classFeature.name: Channel Divinity (3/rest)
entries: From 18th level, you can use your Channel Divinity thrice between rests.

classFeature.name: Principles of Devotion
entries: At 2nd level, after much contemplation and communication with your greater entity, you pledge to uphold certain standards of behaviour beyond what is necessary for adventure. In return, your greater entity grants you one of the following related boons. When you fail to uphold this pledge, you lose any benefits from it until 24 hours after you finish a long rest and rededicate yourself.

entries.name: Chaste
entries: In exchange for forgoing pleasures of the heart and flesh, you are hardened to the {@skill persuasion}s of others. Subtract your Wisdom modifier from any attack rolls made against you that can {@condition charmed|charm}. You also gain an expertise die in your choice of {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, or {@skill Persuasion} checks.

entries.name: Destitute
entries: In exchange for forgoing material luxury, vendors who are not explicitly opposed to your greater entity are compelled to give you heavy discounts of up to 20%, and inkeeps offer free lodging.

entries.name: Devotional Integrity
entries: You adhere to a strict code of morality and ethics. When an action you take is honest and non-violent, until the end of your turn you are immune to the {@condition rattled} condition and you do not suffer disadvantage or penalties on Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma checks made as part of that action.

entries.name: Image of the Divine
entries: You exhibit a uniform, makeup, tattoo, or body modification indicating your higher entity or religious order. Creatures have advantage when discerning your religious identity. Attacks that can {@condition frightened|frighten} and {@skill Intimidation} checks made against you have disadvantage. You also gain an expertise die in your choice of {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Persuasion} checks.

entries.name: Merciful
entries: In exchange for forgoing bloodshed - torture, attacking {@condition unconscious} creatures, and intentionally dealing lethal wounds to creatures you reduce to 0 hit points - you are yourself granted mercy. While you have at least two failed death saves, you have advantage on death saves.

entries.name: Rule of Ritual
entries: You make formal religious observances and perform ritual timekeeping. You can spend 1 hour during a long rest to make these observances, and if you do you only need 4 hours of sleep instead of the normal 6. You learn the {@spell guidance} cantrip if you do not already know it, and when you grant expertise dice with a levelled spell, the expertise die you grant increases by one step.

entries.name: Secrecy
entries: Your mission from your deity requires you to work undercover, concealing your true self with a secret identity. In exchange for forgoing the pleasure of true connection and friendship, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Deception} checks.

entries.name: Severity
entries: In exchange for forgoing weakness such as helping or protecting others (except when it serves you), you add your Wisdom modifier to {@skill Perception} checks made to find valuable objects, and on opposed checks made against the {@skill Persuasion} check of another creature. This benefits your {@skill Insight} journey activity checks, but no others.

entries.name: Silence
entries: In exchange for speaking only to cast spells, you are able to compel other creatures to action with only a glance. Your {@skill Persuasion} bonus is not factored into your party's Group {@skill Persuasion} bonus, but the Group {@skill Persuasion} bonus is increased by your Wisdom modifier.

classFeature.name: Signs of Faith
entries: At 3rd level, you gain a {@filter sign of faith|optionalfeatures|feature type=sf} of your choice. The Signs Known column of the Cleric table shows when you gain new signs.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Sacred Office
entries: Also at 4th level, your mission has taken root and is known through much of the land, conveying rumours both good and ill about your deeds. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Auspicious Collaborator
entries: The higher powers you are attuned to have witnessed your deeds and bestowed upon you a quality of worthiness. Your requests for aid are perceived as opportunities for partnership rather than impositions.

People of all backgrounds are more drawn to help you than they would ordinarily be. You gain an expertise die in {@skill Persuasion}, and an additional expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks related to seeking aid. The Pray journey activity counts as seeking aid.

entries.name: Divine Authority
entries: Your connection to religious hierarchy opens many doors. Sermons you give and courtesy calls you make to different religious groups put you in contact with a local representative of your faith, who is happy to strongly recommend you to another local leader. You gain an expertise die in your choice of {@skill Insight} or {@skill Persuasion}. In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Insight} and {@skill Persuasion} checks made against local leaders to whom you are recommended that would otherwise be indifferent towards you.

entries.name: Reputation
entries: Your reputation for talented ministry and good work precedes you. Common folk frequently recognise you on sight, and those who do often have a friendly disposition towards you. They often ask you for counsel, favours, and blessings. The disposition of people they introduce you to are one stage better than normal (the indifferent become friendly, the hostile become indifferent).

entries.name: Symbol of Might
entries: The strength of the powers you serve is obvious to anybody you interact with, and you are able to use it to garner a certain level of fear and compliance. You gain an expertise die in {@skill Intimidation}, and you gain an additional expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks made to convince others to comply with your demands. This counts for Rob journey activity checks. The people you intimidate are no longer friendly towards you, if they were previously.

classFeature.name: Empowered Turning (1)
entries: At 5th level your greater entity grants you improved ways to turn undead or other abhorrent beings. You gain this feature again at 8th, 11th, 14th, and 17th level. Each time you gain Empowered Turning, choose one of the following:

entries.name: Command Undead
entries: If you choose, creatures you turn instead regard you as their master for the duration. While they are under this effect, you may use your action to issue simple commands (no more than a sentence or two) which they are compelled to obey. They will not take any action that is obviously harmful to them, and so will not leap from a great height, jump onto a sword, or purposefully self-inflict any damage. They follow the command until you issue a new one. During combat, this simply counts as the target being {@condition dominated}.

entries.name: Destroy Undead
entries: Creatures turned by you of CR 1/2 or less are immediately destroyed, transformed into dust by a blinding flash of light. You can select this option multilpe times. Each time you choose it, you are able to destroy more powerful creatures, progressing to CR 1, CR 2, CR 3, and then CR 4 each subsequent time you select this option.

entries.name: Turn Ideology
entries: Choose one of the following: Chaotic, Evil, Good, or Lawful. When you use Turn Undead, you can also choose to affect creatures with the chosen alignment trait. You can select this option multiple times.

entries.name: Turn Supernatural
entries: Choose a creature type from celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend. When you use Turn Undead, you can also choose to affect creatures of that type. You can select this option multiple times.

classFeature.name: Empowered Turning (2)
entries: At 8th level, you gain another choice from your Empowered Turning options.

classFeature.name: Empowered Turning (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain another choice from your Empowered Turning options.

classFeature.name: Empowered Turning (4)
entries: At 14th level, you gain another choice from your Empowered Turning options.

classFeature.name: Empowered Turning (5)
entries: At 17th level, you gain another choice from your Empowered Turning options.

classFeature.name: Sacred Presence
entries: At 9th level, you bring the true presence of your ideals wherever you go. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Cosmic Idealist
entries: Your moral compass aligns with the transcendent forces of the multiverse. Choose Chaotic, Evil, Good, Lawful, or one of the following: Chaotic and Evil, Chaotic and Good, Lawful and Evil, or Lawful and Good. You gain the chosen alignment trait or alignment traits, though you do not need to act upon them. You can identify creatures that have the same alignment traits as you, and you have advantage when making Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma checks against them.
Additionally, you do not suffer negative effects from being on a plane with which you share an alignment trait.

entries.name: Eyes of the Heart
entries: You have a way of really seeing folk's inner character. When you succeed on a contested {@skill Insight} check, if the opposing creature's Charisma score is equal to or less than your standard DC, for the next 24 hours you do not need to make any further checks to recognise whenever that creature is lying, telling the truth, or excluding information.

entries.name: Spiritual Salve
entries: Your spiritual mindset has given you unique insights that can help you and your companions endure hardships. Your constant encouragements and proverbs allow allies within 30 feet of you to reroll a fortitude attack against them. If they do so, they must use the new roll. Once a creature has used your Spiritual Salve to make a reroll, it cannot do so again until it has finished a long rest.

classFeature.name: Providence
entries: At 10th level, the potency of your faith increases. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Divine Intervention
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can use an action to call on your deity to intervene on your behalf when your need is great. Describe the assistance you seek and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your level, your deity intervenes. The DM chooses the nature of the intervention; the effect of any spell would be appopriate. If your deity intervenes, you can't use this feature again for 7 days. Otherwise, you can use it again after you finish a long rest.
After each failed divine intervention roll, add 1 to the number required to succeed until it is a success, at which point it resets to your cleric level or below.
At 20th level, your call for intervention succeeds automatically, no roll required.

entries.name: Imminent Turning
entries: A powerful connection to the life force of your greater entity extends the range of your Turn Undead. You may now Turn Undead from up to 60 feet away.

entries.name: Master Ritualist
entries: Careful study of your holy texts has made you intimiately familiar with all of your ritual spells. You may cast any ritual spell in the cleric spell list as a ritual, whether or not you have it prepared.

entries.name: Prayer of Protection
entries: After each long rest choose one damage type from the following list: cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, psychic, radiant, or thunder. You gain 50% resistance to this damage type until the end of your next long rest.

classFeature.name: Avatar of Faith
entries: At 20th level, your secret inner divinity is finally revealed. You can use your Channel Divinity to become a conduit of the divine, flooding the world around you with the unfettered power of the Moral Planes. Once you have used either of these options, you cannot use that option again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Channel Negative Energy
entries: You can use an action to evoke baleful energy from the Lower Planes to harm creatures. You choose the creatures. Make a will attack against each creature that deals {@dice 5d12} force damage and {@dice 5d12} necrotic damage.

When you use this feature, you also choose one of the following conditions: {@condition blinded}, {@condition confused}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition poisoned}, {@condition rattled}, or {@condition slowed}. A creature hit by the attack also gains the chosen condition until it passes a passive save.
In addition, you can choose any number of creatures that have the Evil alignment trait. Each regains {@dice 5d10} hit points.

entries.name: Channel Positive Energy
entries: You can use an action to evoke energy from the Upper Planes to heal creatures you are able to see. You choose the creatures and divide 225 hit points of healing among them.

A creature that regains hit points from this feature is also healed of one level of {@condition fatigue} and one level of {@condition strife}, and if it is {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition confused}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition doomed}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition staggered}, {@condition poisoned}, {@condition rattled}, {@condition slowed}, or {@condition debilitated} those conditions end on it. This feature cannot be used on undead or constructs.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Divine Sense
entries: At 3rd level, choose one of the following sets of enemies: 'celestials and fiends', 'fey and elementals', or 'aberrations and undead'. You can always sense when a creature of your chosen types is within 30 feet of you, but cannot determine their number, type, or location. As a bonus action, you can briefly enhance your senses to learn the location, number, and type of any creatures of your chosen types within 30 feet of you. When you do so, make a will attack against creatures of those types that are within range, with disadvantage if the creature's CR is higher than your herald level. This attack has the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: You learn the target's CR.

items.name: Standard success
entries: You learn the target's identity and its CR.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and you may ask the DM 3 questions about the target's game statistics.
Within the same radius, you also detect any locations that have been consecrated or desecrated, as with the {@spell hallow} spell.

You can active this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, and regain spent uses upon finishing a long rest. Upon finishing a long rest, you may also swap your chosen set of creature types for another.

classFeature.name: Lay on Hands
entries: From 3rd level, your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a long rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of hit points equal to your herald level x 6.
As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from the pool to restore a number of hit points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in your pool.

Alternatively, you can expend 6 hit points from your pool of healing to cure the target of one disease or neutralise one poison affecting it. You can cure multiple diseases and neutralise multiple poisons with a single use of Lay on Hands, expending hit points separately for each one.
This feature has no effect on undead and constructs.

classFeature.name: Channel Divinity
entries: Also at 1st level, your oath allows you to channel the divine for more magical effects than just casting spells. Each Channel Divinity option granted by your subclass explains how to use it. Whenever you use your Channel Divinity, you choose which option to use. Once you have used this feature you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and devotion to your oath.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Herald table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your {@filter herald spells|spells|class=herald (sns)}. To cast one of your herald spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of herald spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the herald spell list. When you do so, choose a number of herald spells equal to your Charisma modifier + half your herald level, rounded down (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you are a 5th-level herald, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Charisma of 14, your list of prepared spells can include four spells of 1st or 2nd-level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell cure wounds}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or a 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.

You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of herald spells requires time spent in prayer and meditation: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your herald spells, since their power derives from the strength of your convictions. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item holy symbol} as a spellcasting focus for your herald spells.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Sanguine Knot|maneuvers|Tradition=sanguine knot}, {@filter Spirited Steed|maneuvers|Tradition=spirited steed}, or {@filter Tempered Iron|maneuvers|Tradition=tempered iron}. You learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You use your maneuvers by spending exertion points, but you do not gain an exertion pool. Instead, at the start of each of your turns you can expend spell slots of 1st-level or higher to gain exertion points that go into a temporary exertion pool which lasts until you start your next long rest. You gain 2 exertion points for a 1st-level spell slot, and 2 more for each spell slot level above 1st. Only spell slots gained from the herald class can be expended this way.

The Maneuvers Known column of the Herald table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you finish a long rest, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Divine Smite
entries: Starting at 2nd level, whenever you hit a creature wtih a weapon attack you can choose to deliver a divine smite that deals an additional {@dice 1d8} radiant damage. The amount of extra damage increases as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Smite Damage column of the Herald Table.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain spent uses upon finishing a long rest.

classFeature.name: Divine Lessons
entries: In preparing to take your oath, you have learned a number of skills and tricks that can help you during your adventures. At 3rd level, you gain a {@filter divine lesson|optionalfeatures|feature type=dl} of your choice. The Lessons Known column of the Herald table shows when you learn more divine lessons.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Empowered Smite
entries: At 4th level, you are able to manipulate magic to add extra power to your smites. Whenever you would use your Divine Smite feature, you can choose one of the following effects. Once you have used this feature you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest. Alternatively, you can empower a smite by expending a spell slot of 1st-level or higher.

entries.name: Igniting Smite
entries: Your smite instead deals fire damage, and if the triggering attack scored a major success, the target suffers {@dice 1d6} ongoing fire harm until it passes a passive save. The fire can also be put out as an action or by the environment, and exstinguishes itself after 1 minute.

entries.name: Marking Smite
entries: Your smite causes the target to shed bright light for 5 feet, and dim light for an additional 5 feet. The effect lasts for up to 1 minute, or until you target another creature with a weapon attack. As long as the effect lasts, the target gains no benefit from {@condition invisible|invisibility} and has disadvantage on checks made to hide.

entries.name: Repelling Smite
entries: Your smite instead deals force damage, and if the triggering attack scored a major success, the target is pushed back 10 feet and knocked {@condition prone}. On a standard success, the target is knocked back only 5 feet and is not knocked {@condition prone}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Heraldic Sermon
entries: Also at 5th level, your oath has so inspired you that your words seem to be imbued with magic when you talk about it. Choose one of the following features.

entries.name: Devout
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill. You have advantage when using this skill to influence others by discussing your oath or deity, and witnesses that hear your words are inspired to share any useful or important information they might have. This affects the Busk journey activity.

entries.name: Fearmonger
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Intimidation} skill. You have advantage when using this skill and your faith to intimidate others, and witnesses with opposing views make obvious efforts to avoid you. This affects the Mug journey activity.

classFeature.name: Sacred Aura
entries: At 6th level, you are able to manifest an aura that aids you and your allies. Each aura has a radius of 15 feet, is centred on you, and you must be conscious for your and your allies to benefit from it. You can have only one of the following auras active at a time, and can change which aura is active whenever you finish a long rest. At 18th level, the range of all auras increases to 30 feet.

entries.name: Aura of Courage
entries: You and creatures of your choice within range cannot be {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Aura of Resistance
entries: You and creatures of your choice within range gain a bonus to non-armour defences equal to half your Charisma modifier (minimum 0).

entries.name: Aura of Willpower
entries: You and creatures of your choice within range cannot be {@condition charmed}.

classFeature.name: Aura Improvements
entries: At 18th level, the range of all your Sacred Auras and Improved Sacred Auras increases to 30 feet.

classFeature.name: Greater Empowered Smite
entries: At 8th level you learn the following new empowerments for your smites, and can use this feature twice at no cost in between long rests. Alternatively, you can use an empowered smite by expending a spell slot of 1st-level or higher.

entries.name: Blinding Smite
entries: If the triggering attack scored a major success, the target is {@condition blinded} for 1 minute, or until it passes a passive save.

entries.name: Disorienting Smite
entries: Your smite instead deals psychic damage and if the triggering attack scored a major success, the target has disadvantage on armour attack rolls for 1 minute, or until it passes a passive save.

entries.name: Taunting Smite
entries: If the triggering attack scored a major success, the target takes an additional {@dice 1d6} radiant damage when making an armour attack against a creature other than you and cannot make opportunity attacks against anyone but you. This effect lasts up to 1 minute, or until the target passes a passive save.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Devotion
entries: At 9th level, you can draw upon your oath for extra assistance in social situations. Choose one of the following features.

entries.name: Lend Faith
entries: When an ally within 30 feet that can see and hear you would make an ability check you can inspire them with your faith, adding your Charisma modifier to the roll. Once you have used this feature you cannot do so again until you finish a short rest.

entries.name: Reveal Heresy
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight} skill, and gain an expertise die on {@skill Insight} checks. Whenever a creature is lying to you, it makes a {@skill deception} check against a DC equal to 10 + your {@skill Insight} bonus. This check is made with disadvantage and done in secret by the DM. On a failure, you get a strong sense that they are being untruthful.

entries.name: Truth of Conviction
entries: Whenever you or an ally are telling the truth and make any Charisma ability check to do so, you can use this feature to treat any roll of 10 or less as an 11 for that roll. Once you have used this feature you cannot do so again until you finish a short rest.

classFeature.name: Improved Sacred Aura
entries: At 10th level, you gain a more powerful aura that is active alongside your Sacred Aura. The range of this aura is the same, and you can only have one Improved Sacred Aura active at a time. You can swap your active improved aura upon finishing a long rest.

entries.name: Aura of Healing
entries: Whenever you or an ally cast a spell to heal yourself or another ally while both a within your aura, both the spellcaster and target gain additional hit points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Aura of Overcoming
entries: Weapon armour attacks made by allies within your aura ignore bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing resistances and immunities.

entries.name: Aura of Smiting
entries: Whenever an ally within your aura scores a critical hit with a weapon armour attack, they deal additional radiant damage equal to half your herald level.

classFeature.name: Divine Warrior
entries: Beginning at 11th level, each of your strikes is infused with divine might. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you deal an extra {@dice 1d8} radiant damage.

classFeature.name: Divine Recovery
entries: At 12th level, you can transform divine energy into more easily manipulated magics. You can use a bonus action to recite a short prayer or mantra and expend a use of Channel Divinity to regain a spell slot of 3rd-level or less.
Once you have used this feature three times, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Infectious Resolve
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can use your action to touch one willing creature (or yourself) and choose one of the following effects.

items.name: All In
entries: The target gains advantage on its next weapon armour attack, and deals an extra {@dice 1d8} radiant damage on a hit.

items.name: Iron Will
entries: For the next minute, as long as you are conscious, will spell attacks against the target have advantage.

items.name: Walk It Off
entries: One spell or condition of the target's choice affecting them ends
Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Holy Exemplar
entries: At 20th level, your deity constantly watches over you. Even death cannot touch you as you recover from mortal wounds in a flash of holy light, a sudden swelling of unnatural darkness, or another expression of potent divine power. When you die after being reduced to 0 hit points by an attack or spell, after {@dice 1d4} rounds you return to life with 112 hit points. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: No Equipment
entries: From 1st level, the sidekick has no ability to wear any equipment beyond their starting items. In exchange, they receive bonuses to their stats based on the Attack Roll/Armour, Max HP, and Damage bonus columns of the Sidekick table.

classFeature.name: No Feats
entries: At 1st level, the sidekick gains no feats. Instead, it gains 4 points to distribute between its ability scores. The sidekick cannot increase an ability score above 20 with this feature.

classFeature.name: Passive Regeneration (double prof)
entries: Also from 1st level, the sidekick heals a number of hit points equal to double their proficiency bonus at the start of each of their turns in combat.

classFeature.name: Danger Sense
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, reflex attacks made against the sidekick by effects they can see, such as traps and spells, have disadvantage.

classFeature.name: Improved Critical (+1)
entries: Starting at 3rd level, the sidekick's critical hit range increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 4th level, the sidekick has 3 points to distribute amongst their ability scores. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 6th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 14th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, the sidekick increases one ability score of their choice by 2, or the sidekick increases two ability scores of their choice by 1 each. The sidekick can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (1)
entries: Beginning at 5th level, when the sidekick uses the Attack action, their main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + their Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double their Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Battle Readiness
entries: After the sidekick reaches 7th level, it has advantage on initiative rolls.

classFeature.name: Improved Defences (+1)
entries: From 9th level, the sidekick gains a +1 bonus to fortitude, reflex, and will.

classFeature.name: Improved Armour
entries: At 10th level, the sidekick's armour increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (2)
entries: From 11th level, when the sidekick uses the Attack action, their main hand weapon's base damage is tripled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + their Strength modifier deals {@dice 3d6} + triple their Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Improved Defences (+2)
entries: From 13th level, the sidekick gains a further +1 bonus (for a total of +2) to fortitude, reflex, and will.

classFeature.name: Improved Critical (+2)
entries: Starting at 15th level, the sidekick's critical hit range increases by a further 1 (for a total of +2).

classFeature.name: Improved Defences (+3)
entries: From 17th level, the sidekick gains a further +1 bonus (for a total of +3) to fortitude, reflex, and will.

classFeature.name: Passive Regeneration (level)
entries: From 18th level, the number of hit points the sidekick heals at the start of each of their turns in combat increases to their level.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (3)
entries: At 20th level, when the sidekick uses the Attack action, their main hand weapon's base damage is quadrupled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + their Strength modifier deals {@dice 4d6} + quadruple their Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Artificial Spellcasting
entries: With ingenuity and knowhow, artificers are able to harness magic through technology.

entries.name: Spell Inventions
entries: Whenever you prepare an {@filter artificer spell|spells|class=Artificer (sns)}, you create a spell invention for that spell using your tools of artifice and whatever materials you have on hand.

A spell invention is a unique experimental device that is esoteric and useless to other creatures, but in your hands can be used to cast the spell it was prepared for. You prepare a number of spells with a spell level no higher than your maximum spell level (both as shown on the Artificer table) chosen from the artificer spell list.

You can change your list of prepared spells and replace or create new spell inventions whenever you finish a long rest by spending at least 10 minutes tinkering. Any spell inventions you've prepared previously are taken apart and integrated into the new ones, or fall apart due to a lack of maintenance. You can only have a maximum of 1 spell invention per spell you have prepared.

You do not have spell slots, and instead utilise your spell inventions to cast spells. Whenever you cast a prepared artificer spell using a spell invention, you may cast it at a spell slot level up to your maximum spell level as shown on the Artificer table.

You may freely draw a spell invention from your inventory as part of the same action used to cast a spell through it, and you may also freely stow any spell inventions you were already wielding using that same action.
Using a spell invention does not remove its spell from your list of prepared spells.

You can determine the exact form a spell invention takes but it weighs 500 grams per spell level and is no longer than 1 foot in any dimension (treat cantrips as 1st-level spells). A spell invention has an armour equal to half your Intelligence modifier, and a number of hit points equal to your artificer level x 3. You can fully repair your spell inventions whenever you finish a long rest, so long as you have access to your tools of artifice.

items.name: Fizzle Die
entries: After you cast a spell using a spell invention, you must roll a fizzle die, a {@dice d4}. If the result is less than or equal to the spell slot level used to cast the spell, your spell invention burns out, runs out of power, or otherwise malfunctions and cannot be used again until repaired. Your fizzle die improves by one step at 10th level (from {@dice 1d4} to {@dice 1d6}) and again at 20th level (from {@dice 1d6} to {@dice 1d8}), and it improves by one step when using a spell invention gained from your artificer subclass (maximum {@dice 1d10}).

items.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know to cantrips of your choice from the artificer spell list and construct a spell invention for each. Unlike your other spell inventions, using these does not require rolling a fizzle die. You learn additional artificer cantrips of your choice at higher levels and construct spell inventions for them, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Artificer table. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can replace one of the artificer cantrips you know with another cantrip from the artificer spell list.

items.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your artificer spells. You use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

items.name: Tools of Artifice
entries: You produce your spell inventions and infuse items with your tools which act as your spellcasting focus. After you gain the ability to infuse items at 2nd level, you can also use any item bearing one of your infusions as tools of artifice.

classFeature.name: Fizzle Die Improvement (d6)
entries: Your fizzle die increases from a {@dice d4} to a {@dice d6}.

classFeature.name: Fizzle Die Improvement (d8)
entries: Your fizzle die increases from a {@dice d6} to a {@dice d8}.

classFeature.name: Craftsman
entries: Artificers often start out in creative or artistic trades. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Magiscience} checks.

classFeature.name: Tactical Chemistry
entries: At 1st level, you know how to quickly turn inert chemicals into something violently reactive or useful in the moment. As a bonus action, you can create one of the following items:

items: acid (vial) (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
alchemist's fire (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
black powder charge (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
ether (flask) (https://a5e.tools/rules/common-poisons)
flash bomb (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
oil (1 pint flask) (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
paint pot (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
poison (basic) (https://a5e.tools/rules/common-poisons)
smoke bomb (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
tanglefoot bag (https://a5e.tools/rules/miscellaneous-adventuring-gear)
torch (https://a5e.tools/rules/survival-gear)
torch (alchemical) (https://a5e.tools/rules/survival-gear)

entries: Items you make in this way are extremely unstable and must be used immediately. If an item you create in this way is not used (activated, lit, thrown, or applied as appropriate to the item) by the end of your next turn it dissolves into useless sludge.

You can use this feature to create a number of items equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. You cannot regain expended uses in this way if you don't have access to tools of artifice.

classFeature.name: Schematics
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can build all manner of things with the right tools and, most importantly, the schematics of what you're trying to make.

entries.name: Schematic Book
entries: You gain schematics for three common rarity magic items (https://a5e.tools/magic-items?field_mi_rarity_target_id%5B%5D=315&field_mi_cost_value%5Bmin%5D=&field_mi_cost_value%5Bmax%5D=&combine=) of your choice. A schematic is a collection of notes and diagrams that explains in detail how to create a specific magic item. These form your schematic book, and any future schematics you gain are added to it.

entries.name: Crafting with Schematics
entries: When you have the schematics for a magic item, you can craft it as part of a long rest by spending half of the item's default gold cost. You can only craft a single magic item per long rest without disrupting the rest. Crafting a magic item using a schematic does not use up the schematic in any way, and the schematic remains usable.

entries.name: Creating New Schematics
entries: You can attempt to create and add a new schematic for any traditional magic item (https://a5e.tools/magic-items) to your schematic book by spending 1 hour studying and 10% of the item's default gold cost in experiments and material tests. At the end of the hour make a DC 15 {@skill Magiscience} check. On a success you create a new schematic of that magic item and add it to your schematic book, and on a failure the time and materials are wasted.

entries.name: Infusion
entries: Once you have sufficiently studied a magic item you can reproduce and temporarily infuse its magical properties into items through magitechnical ingenuity.
If you have the schematic for a magic item, you can use an action to create it.
You can infuse a number of items as shown on the Infusion Limit column of the Artificer table. You regain all expended infusion uses whenever you finish a long rest.

Items you infuse do not conflict with regular loot items for item slots, but do conflict with each other (for example, you cannot wear two helmets that you have infused, but you can wear an infused helmet and a helmet you looted). Additionally, a creature can only wear up to three infused items that require attunement at a time, and cannot wear multiple copies of the same item that requires attunement.

Any infused items you create vanish after you finish a long rest, but they can remain imbued indefinitely if you commit to regular upkeep. Whenever you finish a long rest you can maintain any infused items you have access to by expending one infusion use for each. Any infusions you fail to maintain vanish.

classFeature.name: Field Discoveries
entries: Experimentation is the key to scientific progress, and through your experiences and findings in the field you've discovered new skills and data. At 2nd level you gain one field discovery of your choice. The Field Discoveries column of the Artificer table shows when you learn more field discoveries. Unless otherwise noted, you can gain each field discovery only once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Battlefield Smithing
entries: At 6th level, as a veteran combat-tinkerer you specialise in certain aspects of warfare. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Armour Smithing
entries: During a short rest, you can spend 25 gold in materials to permanently add one of the following modifications to a suit of armour or change an additional modification:

items.name: Camouflaged
entries: A terrain type for this modification is chosen when it is crafted. The wearer gains an expertise die on {@skill Stealth} checks while in that terrain.

items.name: Spiked
entries: A creature that begins its turn grappling the wearer takes 1 thorns damage.

items.name: Stealthy
entries: The armour has been disguised to look like a piece of clothing. A creature observing the item only realises that it is armour with a DC 15 {@skill Investigation} check (made with disadvantage if the armour is being worn at the time).

items.name: Storage
entries: The armour contains a hidden compartment the size of a small vial that requires a DC 20 {@skill Investigation} check to be found without knowledge of it.
A suit of armour can only have one additional modification.

entries.name: Weapon Smithing
entries: During a short rest, you can spend 25 gold in materials to permanently add one of the following modifications to a weapon or change an additional modification:

items.name: Flamboyant
entries: Creatures have disadvantage on any saves made to resist being distracted by the weapon, and the wielder has advantage on {@skill Intimidation} checks made with the use of it.

items.name: Quickdraw
entries: The weapon can be swapped in or out as a free action once per turn.

items.name: Stealthy
entries: The weapon has been disguised to look like another normal item. A creature observing the item only realises that it is a weapon with a DC 15 {@skill Investigation} check (made with disadvantage if the weapon is sheathed).

items.name: Storage
entries: The weapon contains a hidden compartment the size of a small vial that requires a DC 20 {@skill Investigation} check to be found without knowledge of it. The compartment has a release that can be used as a bonus action to allow the liquid within the compartment to flow out and be applied to the weapon.
A weapon can only have one additional modification.

classFeature.name: Intellectual Calibre
entries: At 7th level, your attitudes and academic manners have cemented how you and your intellect are perceived by your peers. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Kooky Eccentric
entries: Your excitement for your work is infectious and most find your quirky methodology inescapably endearing. You gain proficiency in {@skill Persuasion}, or if you are already proficient in {@skill Persuasion} you gain an expertise die instead. In addition, you can always choose to use Intelligence when making a {@skill Persuasion} check.

entries.name: Sporadic Genius
entries: You're known for sudden bursts of insight and people listen to your fervorous insights. When you or another creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an ability check or save, you can use your reaction to add your Intelligence modifier to the roll.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Stern Proctor
