entries: Your demand for perfection extends past your own work, and with only a discerning glare and a few choice words you can make the confident feel foolish. You gain proficiency in {@skill Intimidation}, or if you are already proficient in {@skill Intimidation} you gain an expertise die instead. In addition, you can always choose to use Intelligence when making an {@skill Intimidation} check.

classFeature.name: Advanced Tactical Chemistry
entries: At 9th level you've mastered and branched out from basic chemistry, discovering a new science to integrate into your tactics. You may add your Intelligence modifier to the attack rolls of any item you create using Tactical Chemistry. In addition, choose one of the following:

entries.name: Humanoid Biochemistry
entries: You've learned a great deal of biochemistry and what chemicals you can apply to alleviate many ailments. You can create the following additional items using your Tactical Chemistry feature:

items: antitoxin (https://a5e.tools/rules/medicinals)
healing potion (greater) (https://a5e.tools/node/1090)
laudanum (https://a5e.tools/rules/medicinals)

entries.name: Toxin Synthesis
entries: You've learned the subtleties of toxins and can now rapidly produce foul and deadly substances. You can create the following additional items using your Tactical Chemistry feature:

items: curare (vial) (https://a5e.tools/rules/common-poisons)
poison, potent (vial) (https://a5e.tools/rules/common-poisons)

classFeature.name: Trinket Master
entries: At 10th level you've mastered the simpler enchantments and technological principles. What once was careful trial and error has been replaced with factory-like efficiency and speed. Whenever you craft a magic item from a schematic that has a default gold cost of 50 or less, it can be done so in a short rest instead of a long rest.

classFeature.name: Reliable Spell Inventions
entries: At 11th level your practice has finally smoothed out the early faults of your spell inventions, so long as you keep the arcane power within acceptable parameters. Whenever you roll a 1 on the fizzle die, you may reroll it, if you do you must use the new result.

In addition, your spell inventions have become streamlined enough that other creatures can make sense of them. A creature holding one of your spell inventions can use an action to make a {@skill Magiscience} check (DC equal to your standard DC). On a success the spell is cast at its lowest possible spell level, or on a failure the spell fizzles out and fails to cast. Whenever a creature attempt to cast a spell using one of your spell inventions in this way, roll the fizzle die as normal.

classFeature.name: Marvel of Innovation
entries: At 13th level you've managed to truly improve on something or create something new altogether, taking a solid step forward in the technological development of the world. When you first gain this feature, choose a newly invented foodstuff, object, or trade good that could conceivably be crafted. For example, you could invent carbonated sodas, glowing iridium glassware, or a fine mechanism for better performing pocket watches.
As part of a long rest, you can create your new invention for 5 gold worth of materials.
Any other uses your invention may have are at the DM's discretion, but its utility shouldn't exceed that of other mundane adventuring gear.
In addition, your notoriety as a great inventor spreads along with your invention. While you are in a region you've sold your invention in, you have advantage on {@skill Persuasion} checks.

classFeature.name: Technological Attunement
entries: At 14th level you've managed to bypass many of the arcane roadblocks regarding magic item usage through the clever use of technology.

items: You can attune to up to 5 magic items created from schematics at once.
You ignore all race, class, level, and alignment requirements when attuning to or using magic items creature from schematics.
You gain an additional magical equipment slot for looted items. This can hold any looted items your body armour slot would usually be able to hold.

classFeature.name: Hotfixer
entries: At 17th level you've mastered the art of rerouting power, quick welds, and improvising on the spot to keep your spell inventions functional. When rolling the fizzle die would burn out one of your spell inventions, you can use your reaction to fix it and restore it to full working order. Once you repair a spell invention in this way you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Infusion Recharge
entries: At 18th level you've learned what makes magic items tick and how to charge them up on your own. While you are attuned to a magic item that uses charges, you can use a bonus action to expend one infusion use and restore up to {@dice 1d6} of that item's spend charges.

classFeature.name: Magical Automaton
entries: At 20th level you've managed to achieve what most inventions are truly designed for, making machines that do your work for you. Whenever you create a spell invention, you may spend 25 gold in material components and expend two uses of your infusion feature to transform that spell invention into a spell automaton that uses the {@creature spell automaton} stat block and it is destroyed if it reaches 0 hit points.
The exact design of your spell automaton is up to you, and it may resemble a humanoid creature, a boxy contraption, or be a magical construct flowing with arcane energies.

Your spell automaton is a minion, and requires your concentration to maintain. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Block action and uses its move to avoid danger.

You can maintain this spell automaton as if it was an infused item, requiring both the prepared spell invention and two uses of your infusion feature. You can repair your spell automaton to full hit points during a long rest so long as your have access to your tools of artifice.

classFeature.name: Commanding Presence
entries: Starting at 1st level, you have a Commanding Presence which extends from you in a 10-foot radius.

Your allies can attack at your command. When you take the Attack action, you can forgo either the entire attack or one instance of the Empowered Attack feature to allow a friendly creature within range of your Commanding Presence to act instead. If the target can hear or see you, it can use its reaction to either cast a cantrip or make one weapon attack.
The range of your presence increases as shown in the Commanding Presence column of the Marshal table.

classFeature.name: Rallying Surge
entries: Also at 1st level, you can use a bonus action to choose an ally within 30 feet of you. If the target can sense you, it regains hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} + your marshal level + your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
You can simultaneously target 2 allies with this feature at 3rd level, and starting at 7th level you regain use of this feature after finishing a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Rallying Surge Improvement (2 targets)
entries: From 3rd level, you can target up to 2 allies with your Rallying Surge within the same action.

classFeature.name: Rallying Surge Improvement (short rest)
entries: From 7th level, you regain the use of your Rallying Surge after completing a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Biting Zephyr|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr}, {@filter Gallant Heart|maneuvers|Tradition=gallant heart}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|maneuvers|Tradition=mirror's glint}, {@filter Mist and Shade|maneuvers|Tradition=mist and shade}, {@filter Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=rapid current}, {@filter Razor's Edge|maneuvers|Tradition=razor's edge}, {@filter Sanguine Knot|maneuvers|Tradition=sanguine knot}, {@filter Spirited Steed|maneuvers|Tradition=spirited steed}, and {@filter Unending Wheel|maneuvers|Tradition=unending wheel}. You learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Maneuvers Known column of the Marshal table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you learn a new maneuver, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Lessons of War
entries: Also at 2nd level you learn a {@filter lesson of war|optionalfeatures|feature type=lw} of your choice. The Lessons Known column of the Marshal table shows when you learn more lessons of war.

classFeature.name: Mark Foe
entries: At 3rd level, your martial direction improves the efficiency of your allies' armour attacks against an enemy you focus their attention on. You can use a bonus action to choose a creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the start of your next turn, creatures able to hear or see you gain an expertise die on armour attacks made against that creature.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Combat Directives
entries: Starting at 5th level, you can direct your companions so effectively that they can use the techniques you've mastered. You learn one additional combat maneuvers from the {@filter Sanguine Knot tradition|maneuvers|Tradition=sanguine knot}. This combat maneuver does not count against the number of combat maneuvers you know.

In addition, when a creature uses your Commanding Presence to make an attack, it can simultaneously use one Sanguine Knot combat maneuver that you know. If the creature does not have any exertion points to spend on combat maneuvers, it does not require exertion points for that use but it cannot benefit from Combat Directives again until it has finished a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (1)
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (2)
entries: From 11th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is tripled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 3d6} + triple your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Followers
entries: At 5th level, you gain one inexperienced follower from the below options. At 10th, 15th, and 20th level, you gain an additional follower or one of your followers increases in experience tier by 1.
When one of your followers dies, after 1 month you receive word that their replacement is ready to join you.

entries.name: Apothecary
entries: Known for assorted tonics and serums, apothecaries are sometimes mistaken for alchemists but their servives are purely medicinal.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once between the apothecary's long rests, a creature treated by the apothecary can make a save against a poison or disease that can be ended or reduced with a save.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Once per long rest, the apothecary can use an antidote to automatically end the {@condition poisoned} condition on a creature.

items.name: Expert
entries: Once per week, the apothecary provides one medicinal of your choosing (https://a5e.tools/rules/medicinals) worth no more than 100 gold. The medicinal must be used within 24 hours or it loses potency.

entries.name: Bodyguard
entries: A bodyguard is tasked with keeping you alive.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once only, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, the bodyguard takes the damage instead. The bodyguard is killed in the process.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Once per long rest, when you would take damage from an armour attack the bodyguard takes the damage instead.

items.name: Expert
entries: Once per long rest, the bodyguard leaps in front of you to take all damage that would be dealt to you for 1 round.

entries.name: Cook
entries: Preparing meals over a campfire is an entirely different skill set than cooking in a kitchen. Warm meals are a luxury many adventurers forgo, but consuming only preserved rations can lead to ill health.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, when you take a short rest, you heal 1 additional Hit Die without expending it.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Once per long rest, when you and up to 4 allies take a short rest, you each heal 1 additional Hit Die without expending it.

entries.name: Diviner
entries: Whether drawn to adventurers by fate and prophecy, unusual circumstance, or coin for promoting their faith a diviner brings a touch of the beyond with them.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, the diviner cast a {@filter cantrip or 1st-level spell from the cleric spell list|spells|level=0,1|class=Cleric (sns)} on you or an ally of your choosing.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Once per long rest, the diviner receives a helpful vision and you gain an expertise die on your next ability check.

items.name: Expert
entries: Once per week, the diviner channels a supernatural voice that reveals an event in the near future that grants you advantage on one ability check, attack roll, or save.

entries.name: Footpad
entries: A footpad is a low-level thief.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, when in a crowded area, you are able to gain 5 gold per footpad as they mingle with the crowd.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Your footpad can search a town or other settlement in order to gain valuable information or to locate people or objects.

items.name: Expert
entries: Once per week, you can direct your footpad to shadow a person. They report back to you after 7 days with detailed notes on every location the person visited, the people the person interacted with, and any purchases the person made.

entries.name: Healer
entries: A healer is able to tend wounds.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, the healer heals a creature for {@damage 1d8 + 3} hit points.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: As inexperienced. In addition, once per long rest, the healer heals a creature for {@damage 2d8 + 6} hit points.

items.name: Expert
entries: As seasoned. In addition, once per long rest, the healer heals a creature for {@damage 4d8 + 11} hit points.

entries.name: Interpreter
entries: Occasionally called translators, interpreters are polyglots who are not only able to speak multiple languages but also quick to learn new tongues.

items.name: All
entries: Once per long rest, when encountering an unknown language, you have advantage on {@skill History} checks to comprehend that language.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Choose two languages. Your interpreter is proficient in these languages.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Your interpreter can translate all languages generally spoken on the Material Plane.

items.name: Expert
entries: Your interpreter can translate all languages whether alien, current, or dead.

entries.name: Minstrel
entries: Equally adept at performing songs and telling tales, minstrels entertain during long travels and recount adventurers' heroic deeds.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, you gain advantage on a Charisma check.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: Once per long rest, you gain an expertise die on an attack roll using a weapon you are proficient with or ability check using a skill you are proficient with.

items.name: Expert
entries: The ballads, poems, songs, and tales that your minstrel has composed about your deeds spread far and wide. You gain a bonus to your {@skill Persuasion} equal to half your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Sage
entries: Sages are specialists frequently consulted by those in search of obscure information. They rarely leave civilisation, but sometimes accompany adventurers who need their expertise to examine ruins or immovable relics. A sage is very knowledgeable about a single skill. For each sage, choose a skill.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: Once per long rest, the sage may make their skill check for you as though you were proficient in that skill. In addition, you gain an expertise die on the check.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: As inexperienced. In addition, once per day you have advantage on an Intelligence check made to learn or recall a piece of knowledge.

items.name: Expert
entries: As seasoned, except that expert sages only grant the following ability when they are proficient in {@skill Magiscience}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill History}: once per week when the sage is able to see a creature they can learn information about from their skill, you gain advantage on your first armour attack roll against it.

entries.name: Smith
entries: A smith keeps your weapons and armour in top condition.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: While you have a smith in your employ, any weapon you wield does +1 damage after your smith has fine-tuned it over the course of a short rest.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: As inexperienced, and your smith takes particularly good care of some of your equipment. At the end of each week choose a number of items equal to your proficiency bonus. The chosen items always look polished and brand-new as your smith makes sure to keep them in excellent condition.

items.name: Expert
entries: As seasoned, and any body armour you wear gains a +1 Armour bonus after your smith has fine-tuned it over the course of a long rest. In addition, any body armour you wear grants you +1 resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and any weapons you wield have their base damage die increased by 1 size.

entries.name: Squire
entries: Trained to assist warriors with their gear, squires are typically youths of noble blood apprenticing to be knights. However, lowborn adventurers occasionally feign nobility and employ squires of their own. Squires remain close to their master, holding extra weapons and equipment, picking up dropped gear, or carrying their liege's banner.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: A squire can assist in equipping a shield or other weapon, allowing you to swap held items once per round as a free action.

items.name: Seasoned
entries: As inexperienced. In addition, your squire can identify other warriors and their squires, as well as where they are from and if they are knighted.

items.name: Expert
entries: As seasoned. In addition, your squire can announce you in royal courts. You gain advantage and a +5 bonus to your first Charisma check made in a royal court after your squire has introduced you.

entries.name: Torchbearer
entries: Also known as a lantern bearer or linkboy, torchbearers provide light during expeditions to dark places, freeing their employers' hands for other purposes.

items.name: Inexperienced
entries: You shed bright light in a 40-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

classFeature.name: Call to Arms
entries: Beginning at 6th level, when you roll initiative, you and each creature friendly to you that are within range of your Commanding Presence gain an expertise die to their initiative check. Additionally, when you roll for initiative, you can switch your result with that of any ally you can see.

classFeature.name: Martial Renown
entries: At 6th level, tales of your deeds take root in the hearts of the people. As a leader, you are more famous than most adventurers. You add half your proficiency bonus to your {@skill Persuasion}.

In addition, your reputation strongly affects how you are perceived, and when it becomes known that you are in a settlement people treat you accordingly. At the DM's discretion, there may be settlements (such as an enemy's war camp) where you cannot utilise this feature. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Famous
entries: When you arrive in a settlement, after you reveal who you are local authority figures seek you out to make introductions and invite you to share a meal or drink.
In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks.

entries.name: Infamous
entries: When you arrive in a settlement, after you reveal who you are common folk hurry to get out of your way, and when you corner a commoner to ask about something local they rapidly tell you whatever details they think you might want to know.
In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks.

entries.name: Maverick
entries: When you arrive in a settlement, after you reveal who you are the local watch starts keeping an eye on you. Heads of illegal organisations may introduce themselves to you if they have goals well-suited to your talents. Additionally, guards expect you're up to no good so are quick to leave their posts to follow you.

classFeature.name: Versatile Exploration
entries: Also at 6th level, whenever you learn a new lesson of war, you can instead choose from {@filter fighter soldiering knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=lw}.

classFeature.name: Spur Ally
entries: Beginning at 9th level, when an ally within your line of sight who can sense you fails an ability check or save, you can use your reaction to make them reroll. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
Starting at 13th level, you regain use of this feature after finishing a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Spur Ally Improvement (short rest)
entries: At 13th level, you regain the use of your Spur Ally after completing a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Expanded Directives (1)
entries: Starting at 10th level, your capacity to direct your companions broadens. Choose one tradition that you know combat maneuvers from. You are able to use Combat Directives to grant uses of combat maneuvers from the chosen tradition.
At 15th level, choose a second tradition that you know combat maneuvers from.

classFeature.name: Expanded Directives (2)
entries: At 15th level, choose a second tradition that you know combat maneuvers from that you can use your Combat Directives with.

classFeature.name: Rouse the Troops
entries: Also at 10th level, you can spend 1 minute speaking words of encouragement and support to reinvigorate your companions. Each friendly creature than can hear or understand you can spend any number of Hit Dice to regain hit points without having to finish a short rest. In addition, each creature that does spend at least one Hit Die in this way can remove one level of {@condition fatigue} or {@condition strife} it is suffering from. Once a creature has removed a level of {@condition fatigue} or {@condition strife} from your use of this class feature, the creature must finish a long rest before it can do so again.

classFeature.name: Commanding Demeanor
entries: Starting at 12th level, the lessons you've learned on the battlefield become useful tools beyond the realm of combat. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Calm
entries: Knowing how and when to embrace quietude allows your silence to speak far lounder than any words, and you easily pick up on the subtleties revealed by body language and demeanor. When you first meet a creature, you can choose to remain silent and speak through nonvisual cues - scrunching your brows, pursing your lips, narrowing your eyes, slowly leaning ever so slightly forward, hunching your shoulders, and so on. As long as you remain silent for 1 minute, the creature has a CR lower than your level, and it remains within your line of sight for the duration, it acts as if you succeeded on an {@skill Intimidation} check.
In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Insight} checks.

entries.name: Resolute
entries: Winning a battle is as much about knowing when to quit as it is knowing when not to give up. Whenever you make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check against a creature and fail, you gain an expertise die on your next Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check made against that creature.

entries.name: Responsive
entries: Whether between duelists or armies, combat is all about timing and you are impeccably punctual. When you overhear an interesting conversation between other creatures, you can interject so seamlessly and casually that the speakers think they already know you, answering one question you ask before realising that they don't.
In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks.

classFeature.name: Dauntless
entries: Beginning at 13th level, attacks that can cause the {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} conditions have disadvantage against you and allies within your Commanding Presence.

classFeature.name: Stronghold
entries: Also at 13th level, you gain a stronghold in a location of your choice. While your stronghold is not destroyed, you gain a +3 bonus to {@skill Persuasion} checks and further bonuses based on the type of stronghold. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Castle
entries: You gain a coat of arms, which can be displayed on your shield or on the flag of a squire bearing your standard (if you have one). Attacks that can cause the {@condition frightened} condition have disadvantage against allies who can see your coat of arms.
Additionally, your Strength is increased by 1.

entries.name: Encampment
entries: You gain an expertise die on ability checks made to hide in natural surroundings. In addition, whenever you lose {@condition fatigue}, lose 1 additional level. Whenever you take a long rest, you always count as doing so in a Haven.
Finally, your hit point maximum is increased by 2.

entries.name: Farm
entries: You gain a number of additional Hit Dice equal to your proficiency bonus. In addition, you gain 1 gold whenever you complete a long rest while in the same hex as your stronghold. Your time working the farm has also made it easy for you to recover quickly. When you expend Hit Dice to heal during a short rest, you can treat a Hit Die roll of 3 or lower as a 4.
Finally, your hit point maximum is increased by 2.

entries.name: Guildhouse
entries: Whenever you complete a long rest within a city's borders, gain 1 gold. In addition, you gain an expertise die on ability checks made to deal with representatives of commercial or criminal organisations.
Finally, your choice of either your Wisdom or Charisma is increased by 1.

entries.name: House
entries: The urge to return to your home can help embolden the hearts of everyone who travels with you. The length of journeys your party makes towards the house are reduced by 1 day (minimum 1). In addition, once per week, you and your party may gain the benefit of a long rest by taking a short rest. Once per month, you are able to throw a lavish and impressive event in the house that draws the attention of people from all around. You gain a {@dice d6} expertise die on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma checks made while hosting such an event.
Finally, your Charisma is increased by 1.

entries.name: Laboratory
entries: You immediately learn one new cantrip or spell at the highest spell level which you have access to, chosen from a spell list you have access to. In addition, once between long rests you can use a bonus action to regain a spell slot of a level equal to half your proficiency bonus. You also gain one 1st-level spell slot, access to your choice of either the {@filter sorcerer|spells|level=1|class=Sorcerer (sns)}, {@filter warlock|spells|level=1|class=Warlock (sns)}, or {@filter wizard|spells|level=1|class=Wizard (sns)} spell list, and learn one new 1st-level spell from a spell list you have access to. The spell is governed by your Charisma, regardless of which list it came from.
Finally, your Intelligence is increased by 1.

entries.name: Library
entries: You gain an expertise die whenever you make an Intelligence check using a skill you are proficient with. In addition, you gain proficiency with your choice of {@skill History}, {@skill Magiscience}, or {@skill Nature}. You can change this decision whenever you complete a long rest.
Finally, your Intelligence is increased by 1.

entries.name: Menagerie
entries: Once per short or long rest, you can use an action to heal a minion of yours that you touch, which regains {@dice 4d6} hit points. In addition, you gain an expertise die whenever you make an ability check related to animals. Once per long rest, you can also spend 1 minute tending to a minion of yours to restore it to its hit point maximum.
Finally, your Wisdom is increased by 1.

entries.name: Sacred Grove
entries: Choose up to two skills you are proficient with. If you are in natural, outdoor surroundings you gain an expertise die whenever you make an ability check using the chosen skills. In addition, you learn the {@spell commune with nature} spell if you do not already know it, and you may cast it once between long rests without expending a spell slot.
Finally, your Wisdom is increased by 1.

entries.name: Shop
entries: The cost of all loot results is reduced by 5 gold for you. In addition, whenever you gain gold, you gain 2 additional gold.
Finally, your choice of either your Wisdom or Charisma is increased by 1.

entries.name: Tavern
entries: You have advantage on ability checks and armour attack rolls made against creatures {@condition poisoned} by alcohol. In addition, you gain an expertise die on saves made against poison, and cannot be {@condition poisoned} by consuming alcohol.
Finally, your Charisma is increased by 1.

entries.name: Temple
entries: Whenever you heal a creature with a limited power through the rolling of dice, you restore two additional hit points per die rolled. In addition, you gain advantage on Wisdom and Charisma checks made against followers devoted to the same higher power as your temple.
Finally, your Wisdom is increased by 1.

entries.name: Workshop
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Magiscience} checks made in the workshop.

Additionally, any body armour worn by members of your party grants +2 resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage, and the base damage die size of any weapons wielded by members of your party is increased by 2.
Finally, your Strength is increased by 1.

classFeature.name: Advantageous Action
entries: Starting at 14th level, your experience in battle lets you help your companions more easily. You can use the Help action as a bonus action. In addition, when you take the Help action choose one of the following effects:

items: You end the {@condition frightened} condition on one creature within 30 feet of you that can sense you.
You grant one creature adjacent to you temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus.
You touch a living creature that has 0 hit points. The creature regains 2 hit points.

classFeature.name: Greater Stronghold
entries: At 16th level, your stronghold improves based on its type.

entries.name: Castle
entries: Your coat of arms can inspire your allies. When an ally able to see your coat of arms is targeted by an attack that can cause the {@condition frightened} condition that results in a minor or lower success, they gain Inspiration.

entries.name: Encampment
entries: Your familiarity with temporary structures is such that you can spend 1 hour to create a Haven able to shelter a number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus. You can't use this feature again until after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Farm
entries: You regain 2 additional hit points for every Hit Die expended during a short rest.

entries.name: Guildhouse
entries: The expertise die on ability checks made to deal with representatives of commercial or criminal organisations increases to a {@dice d6}.

entries.name: House
entries: The expertise die on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma checks made while hosting events increases to a {@dice d8}.

entries.name: Laboratory
entries: You gain a 2nd-level spell slot and a 2nd-level spell from the spell list you chose.

entries.name: Library
entries: You can change your proficiency choice whenever you complete a short or long rest.

entries.name: Menagerie
entries: You gain advantage on checks made to learn about creatures you can see.

entries.name: Sacred Grove
entries: You gain an expertise die whenever you make an ability check if you are in natural, outdoor surroundings.

entries.name: Shop
entries: Whenever you gain gold, you gain 1 additional gold.

entries.name: Tavern
entries: If you have consumed alcohol within the past minute, you gain an expertise die on ability checks.

entries.name: Temple
entries: Whenever you are healed by a spell through the rolling of dice, the dice are rolled with advantage.

entries.name: Workshop
entries: You gain advantage on {@skill Magiscience} checks made in the workshop, and the expertise die on {@skill Magiscience} checks made in the workshop now applies anywhere.

classFeature.name: Critical Weakness
entries: Beginning at 17th level, you can focus your mind to identify any enemy's weaknesses. As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 60 feet, exposing a flaw in its defences. Until the end of your next turn, any creature able to sense you has advantage on armour attack rolls against that creature, and their attacks deal an extra 6 damage to it. Once you have used this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Impressive Reputation
entries: Starting at 18th level, your very name can inspire courage in the hearts of soldiers, fear among the ranks of the enemy, or uncertainty in the minds of the commanders set against you. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Hero
entries: Inspiring tales of your victories have won you friends in high places. Nobles and royalty treat you as an equal, granting you free lodging, food, and a place upon their court for a number of days up to your marshal level. Whenever you complete a long rest within these conditions, gain 1 gold.

entries.name: Iconoclast
entries: You are known for doing the unexpected and people love to hear about it. Whenever you arriv in a settlement you are visited by {@damage 1d4 + 1} bards, scholars, and sages asking for recountings of your recent exploits. In exchange they each either share a piece of information they think might be relevant to your current quest, or make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check (bonus of +5) on your behalf to answer a specific question.

entries.name: Slaughterer
entries: Tales of your merciless bloodshed have won you friends in unlikely places. Bandit and pirate captains, crimelords, intelligent monsters of ill intent, and even fiends know who you are and that you are a valuable ally, doing their best to persuade you to take up the sword against their enemies. Whenever you encounter such a creature, it spends its first action (even in combat) declaring that it knows of your prowess and offering an alliance.

classFeature.name: Legendary Commander
entries: At 20th level, friendly creatures within range of your Commanding Presence add half your Charisma modifier (minimum 0) to their non-Armour defences. Additionally, choose one of the following:

entries.name: Commander's Expertise
entries: Whenever a creature uses your Commanding presence to act, it gains an expertise die on any associated {@dice d20} rolls it makes.

entries.name: Feedback Loop
entries: Whenever a creature uses your Command Presence to make an attack that hits, you gain a reaction. You must use this reaction before the start of your next turn or it is lost.

entries.name: Rapid Deployment
entries: After initiative is rolled and until combat ends, your Speed increases by 20 feet, and allies that are able to sense you increase their Speed by 20 feet.

classFeature.name: Supreme Stronghold
entries: If your stronghold initially granted you +1 to an ability score, you gain a further +1 to the same ability score. If it instead granted you +2 to your hit point maximum, you gain a further +3 to your hit point maximum.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Battle Defence
entries: Starting at 1st level, you decide on how best to protect yourself on the battlefield - relying on barbaric might or encased in weighty armaments. Choose one of the following options.

entries.name: Juggernaut
entries: You gain proficiency with heavy armour.

entries.name: Rugged Defence
entries: While you are not wearing any armour, your base armour is equal to half your fortitude. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.
In addition, your Speed increases by 5 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armour. At 5th level, your speed increases by an additional 5 feet.

classFeature.name: Developed Talents
entries: Whether in the ruins of an ancient civilisation, the barren terrain of the hinterlands, or the back alleys of a prosperous city, berserkers are adept at surviving and interacting with their environments.
At 1st level you gain a {@filter developed talent|optionalfeatures|feature type=dt} of your choice. The Talents Known column of the Berserker table shows when you learn more developed talents.

classFeature.name: Rage
entries: In battle, you give yourself over to the fury within. Starting at 1st level, while you are in combat, you are raging while bloodied or if you have been targeted by a damaging attack since your last turn.
While raging, you gain the following effects:

items: You have advantage on Strength checks, and fortitude attacks against you have disadvantage.

entries: At the start of your turn while above 0 hit points and raging, you gain a number of rage hit points that increases as you gain levels as a berserker, as shown in the Rage Hit Points column of the Berserker table. Whenever you take damage, you reduce your rage hit points first, then your regular hit points. Rage hit points stack each round until combat ends, at which point they dissipate.
The maximum number of rage hit points you can have at one time is equal to 6 x your berserker level.

items: When you make a melee weapon attack governed by Strength or Dexterity, you gain a bonus to its damage that increases as you gain levels as a berserker, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Berserker table.
You have 50% resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.
You cannot cast spells or concentrate while raging.
You gain no benefit from temporary hit points.

classFeature.name: Feed the Anger
entries: From 1st level, you gain the ability to beat your chest or your head, or otherwise harm yourself in order to viscerally force more fury from yourself. As a bonus action while healthy, reduce your hit points until you are bloodied. Then, gain rage hit points as if you had just started your turn while raging.

classFeature.name: Brutal Capability
entries: Also at 1st level, you gain an expertise die in all Strength checks.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Adamant Mountain|maneuvers|Tradition=adamant mountain}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|maneuvers|Tradition=mirror's glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=rapid current}, {@filter Tempered Iron|maneuvers|Tradition=tempered iron}, {@filter Tooth and Claw|maneuvers|Tradition=tooth and claw}. You can learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Manuevers Known column of the Berserker table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you take a long rest, you can replace any of your maneuvers with new ones of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Danger Sense
entries: At 2nd level, you gain an uncanny sense of when things nearby aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge away from danger. Reflex attacks from effects that you can see, such as traps and spells, have disadvantage against you. To gain this benefit, you cannot be {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition dazed}.

classFeature.name: Furious Critical
entries: Also at 2nd level you learn to maximise the effects of your mightiest blows, empowering your weapon armour critical hits. Choose one Furious Critical. At 4th level and every even berserker level after, you learn an additional Furious Critical.

Simple damage isn't all that a berserker can deal in combat. With just the right application of force, a berserker can inflict a number of conditions on their opponent. The attack must use a melee weapon that has the two-handed trait. Each Furious Critical can only be taken once and only one Furious Critical can be used per critical hit, unless otherwise stated.

entries.name: Blinding Maneuver
entries: Your attack flings debris into the target's eyes, causes blood to run into their eyes, or otherwise impedes their vision. The target is {@condition blinded} for 1 round.

entries.name: Dazzling Prowess
entries: Prerequisite: 6th level.
Your show of martial skill is so stunning that the target can only gape. The target makes a save, becoming {@condition debilitated} until the end of your next turn on a failure.

entries.name: Deafening Blow
entries: The force of the blow leaves the target with a ringing sensation in their ears, muffling the world around them. The target is {@condition deafened} for a number of rounds equal to half your proficiency bonus. If the target attempts to cast a spell while {@condition deafened} in this way, it must pass a save in order to do so successfully. If it fails, the spell has no effect, consuming its resources as usual. The caster may then use the same action it used to cast another spell with the same casting time as the failed spell, consuming resources as required. It cannot cast the failed spell again for the next minute.

entries.name: Disorienting Strike
entries: The force of your attack leaves your target seeing double. Your target treats all creatures as though they have half cover for a number of rounds equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Elemental Wrath
entries: Your fury is so great that it incites the very elements to join you. After dealing damage the weapon that dealt the blow is charged with acid, cold, fire, or lightning, chosen when you take this Furious Critical.
The weapon now deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage of the chosen type. This effect lasts for the remainder of combat.

This Furious Critical can be taken multiple times as you gain berserker levels, increasing the damage each time, but needing to meet a level prerequisite to be taken again; 2d6 at 10th level, 3d6 at 16th level, and 4d6 at 20th level.
Whenever you finish a long rest, you may change the damage type.

entries.name: Furious Momentum
entries: Seizing on the fervor of a well-struck blow, you use your fury to fight all the harder. The next melee weapon attack you make before the end of your next turn has +3 critical hit range. Once you have used this feature while not raging, you cannot do so again until you have finished a short rest. The effects of this Furious Critical can be combined with any other.

entries.name: Inspiring Flair
entries: Your sheer competence inspires your companions. Choose another creature that can sense you. That creature gains an expertise die on its next armour attack roll, which is added to the base d20 roll instead of the final result.

entries.name: Jockeying Blow
entries: You can move up to 30 feet. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from the target of your attack. In addition, you gain advantage on any ability checks made to jump as part of this movement. The effects of this Furious Critical can be combined with any other.

entries.name: Knockdown Strike
entries: With the right angle, your attack knocks the target sprawling. The target is knocked {@condition prone} and pushed 30 feet away from you in a straight line.

entries.name: Mighty Blow
entries: Prerequisite: 6th level

You know how to hit so it hurts the most. The attack deals additional damage equal to the die component of your weapon's damage. This Furious Critical can be taken again at 14th level and 18th level, adding more dice of damage.

entries.name: Pinning Strike
entries: The profound force of your blow breaks the environment, crumbling the stone beneath your target's feet or knocking shattered timber over their legs. The target is {@condition grappled}, and {@condition restrained} while grappled in this way.

entries.name: Relentless Attack
entries: Your blows fall down mercilessly. You may immediately make a save. On a success, add your weapon's damage to the attack's damage. On a critical success, in addition to adding that damage, you may also repeat this save. This save benefits from any improvements to critical hit range the attack had. The save can be repeated (after the first attempt) a maximum number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus. No other Furious Critical can be activated alongside this one.

entries.name: Terrifying Force
entries: You focus your rage, unleashing a fear-inspiring strike. Each creature you choose within 60 feet that can sense you becomes {@condition frightened} of you for a number of rounds equal to half your proficiency bonus. The effects of this Furious Critical can be combined with any other.

entries.name: Vitalising Force
entries: The thrill of your attack drives you to greater feats. Your Speed increases by 20 feet for 1 minute or until you use this Furious Critical again.

classFeature.name: Versatile Exploration
entries: Beginning at 3rd level, whenever you learn a new developed talent, you can instead choose from fighter {@filter soldiering knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=sk}.

classFeature.name: Warrior Born
entries: Also at 3rd level, the ways of combat that have come naturally to you bring new and unexpected lessons. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Agitate
entries: Many battles have taught you how to spot aggressive body language like a pursed eyebrow, tensed temple, or portruding vein, and you've mastered using these cues to unsettle others outside of combat. As an action, make an {@skill Intimidation} check opposed by the {@skill Insight} check of a creature you can see and hear within 20 feet. On a success, you subtly press the creature to act out. What exactly the creature does - back away quickly, blurt out something compromising, draw a weapon, look towards a concealed or disguised companion, protectively touch something it is carrying - is at the DM's discretion.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Fearsome Reputation
entries: Word spreads of your prodigious strength or how terrifying you can be in battle. Whenever you are in a settlement, at least one commoner approaches you with a modest gift or bribe and beseeches you to help settle a feud, move a large obstacle, or otherwise make use of your impressive might. In addition to their offering, they are eager to tell you about their home and neighbours.

entries.name: Imposing Prowess
entries: When an ally you can sense makes a {@skill Deception} or {@skill Persuasion} check, you can use your reaction to support them with an intimidating display of strength or unsettling glower. Your ally rerolls the check, gaining an expertise die on the roll.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Crushing Blows
entries: By 5th level your martial prowess is such that you are able to land attacks to the best effect, dealing devastating amounts of damage. Your melee weapon armour attacks have +1 critical hit range.
At 17th level, your melee weapon armour attacks have a further +1 critical hit range.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Battle Defence Improvement (Rugged Defence)
entries: At 5th level, if you chose the Rugged Defence option in the Battle Defence feature, your Speed increases by an additional 5 feet.

classFeature.name: Martial Presence
entries: At 6th level your methodology to battle steeps further into the way you interact with others - and how they interact with you. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Forceful
entries: People respond favorably to your vigor. You can always choose to use your fortitude in place of your Charisma modifier when making {@skill Persuasion} checks.

In addition, in every settlement you visit you inspire a number of followers equal to your proficiency bonus. Your followers perform minor tasks to help you while you are there, feed you and your allies, carry your belongings to nearby destination, share their mount animals, reveal some of their secrets to try and gain your favour, and ask you for help before turning to the authorities with a problem.

entries.name: Mighty
entries: You turn your toughness into a tool when imposing upon others. You can always choose to use your fortitude in place of your Charisma modifier when making {@skill Intimidation} checks.

In addition, when you enter a tavern or other social gathering, you can make a DC 15 {@skill Intimidation} check while loudly declaring that you are looking for something or someone. On a success, most of the people there look at and otherwise point out whoever they believe is the person you are looking for or the person most likely to know about what you are seeking.

entries.name: Scary
entries: It's commonly thought that it's best not to ask you for too much clarification or detail. You can always choose to use your fortitude in place of your Charisma modifier when making {@skill Deception} checks.

In addition, when you fail a {@skill Deception} check against a creature, it makes an {@skill Insight} check against a DC equal to 10 + your {@skill Deception} bonus. On a failure, it thinks twice about your dishonesty and your {@skill Deception} check becomes a success. When performing the Scam journey activity, this causes your failures of any degree to have a 50% chance to be converted into a success.

classFeature.name: Advanced Battle Defence
entries: By 7th level you have doubled down on your battle defence style. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Enhanced Vitality
entries: Whenever you are targeted by a damaging will attack, you can choose to convert it into a fortitude attack instead.

entries.name: Preternatural Agility
entries: You can evade danger to an unbelievable degree. Whenever you are targeted by a damaging reflex attack, you reduce its success tier by 1.

classFeature.name: School of the Open Road
entries: At 11th level your journeys have given you a wealth of practical experience and helpful bits of information. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Don't Get Fooled Again
entries: You've heard and seen (and perhaps told) enough lies that you spot them as easily as tells in battle. You can always choose to use your fortitude in place of your Wisdom modifier for {@skill Insight} checks and gain an expertise die when rolling to detect a falsehood. This affects the Gossip journey activity.
In addition, you are seen as an arbiter of truth by those who have heard of your reputation and you may be called in to mediate a trail or question a suspected wrong-doer, even in larger settlements.

entries.name: Storyteller
entries: You have an ear for interesting events, places, and people of the past. You can always use your fortitude in place of your Intelligence modifier when making {@skill History} checks and gain an expertise die when the subject has something to do with war, battle, or martial prowess. This expertise die does not benefit the Chronicle journey activity.

In addition, your reputation means that people assume that you want to know the history (or perhaps gossip) of the settlement you're visiting. When you are at a tarvern or social gathering they are likely to approach you with tales of the baroness' last stand 50 years ago, or mention 'history in the making' in the form of the duke's child being rumoured to be a werewolf.

entries.name: Trial and Error
entries: Between learning from both your own mistakes and those of others, you've collected considerable knowledge about the natural world. You can always choose to use your fortitude in place of your Intelligence modifier when making {@skill Nature} checks and gain an expertise die on checks made to identify a plant or beast. This affects the Befriend Animal journey activity.
In addition, people see you as someone who loves a good survival story. When you are at a tavern or social gathering they are likely to approach you with information about local hazards or wildlife.

classFeature.name: Notable Reputation
entries: At 13th level your name has spread far and wide, gaining you the respect of even important figures you've yet to meet. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Armed Assistance
entries: Mercenary groups make a point of making your acquaintance, especially in larger towns. As part of their hospitality they feed you and your companions, and provide a safe place to sleep, as well as offer to trade information and word of any jobs your group may be interested in. When these meetings go particularly well they might offer more tangible help in the form of scouting the area around a monster's den or distracting a group of bandits to give you an edge.

entries.name: Noble Curiosity
entries: You reputation and ferocity have sparked the fascination of the nobility. A liveried servant is likely to approach you when you enter the holdings of such a noble and invite you to take part in their employer's hospitality. This includes lavish feasting, introductions to the rich and powerful, and offers of assistance such as mounts, the repair of your armour and weapons, and miscellaneous items worth less than 5 gold.

entries.name: Token of Gratitude
entries: Word of your deeds is not just advantageous to you: many merchants are eager to capitalise on having done business with someone of your reputation. Upon recognising you, they offer up to 15% off of the listed price of a particular item in exchange for some sign of your presence such as a small token or autograph.

classFeature.name: Prickly Mind
entries: Starting at 15th level, your reactions are so honed that you react even to magic trying to affect your mind. Whenever a creature would target you with an attack that could charm you, you can use your reaction to cause an amount of psychic harm equal to your proficiency bonus to the attacker. This backlash does not reveal the creature that tried to charm you.
In addition, your will increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Reliable Might
entries: At 17th level, if your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use your Strength score in place of the total.

classFeature.name: Paragon of Rage
entries: At 20th level, you have reached the peak of brawn and stamina. Your Strength score increases by 4, and your maximum hit points increase by 8.
In addition, while raging you gain the following benefits:

items: Your size increases by one category (from Small to Medium, or Medium to Large), to a maximum size of Large. Your size doubles in all dimensions and your weight increases eightfold.
Whenever you use a basic maneuver alongside a melee weapon attack, you do not give the attack disadvantage.
You deal double damage to objects.
You are unaffected by the {@condition Weakened} condition.
Your Speed increases by 15 feet.
Your horizontal jump distance increases by 40 feet and your vertical jump distance increases by 20 feet.
You gain a {@dice d8} expertise die on Strength checks, and your fortitude increases by 2.

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (2)
entries: At 4th level, you gain a second Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (3)
entries: At 6th level, you gain a third Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (4)
entries: At 8th level, you gain a fourth Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (5)
entries: At 10th level, you gain a fifth Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (6)
entries: At 12th level, you gain a sixth Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (7)
entries: At 14th level, you gain a seventh Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (8)
entries: At 16th level, you gain an eighth Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Furious Critical (9)
entries: At 18th level, you gain a ninth Furious Critical (see the 2nd-level Furious Critical feature).

classFeature.name: Crushing Blows (2)
entries: At 17th level, your melee weapon armour attacks have a further +1 critical hit range (for a total of +2).

classFeature.name: Exploration Knacks
entries: For a ranger exploration isn't just something that happens between destinations - it's a way of life. You start with two ranger {@filter exploration knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=ek} of your choice. The Knacks Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more exploration knacks.

classFeature.name: Familiar Terrain
entries: When making an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to a natural terrain, you gain an expertise die if using a skill you're proficient in. This affects the Chronicle, Gather Components, Harvest, Scout, and Seek Shelter journey activities, but no others. While traveling in a natural terrain, you gain the following benefits:

items: Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
You gain an expertise die on {@skill Perception} checks made to avoid ambushes.
While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, their sizes, and how long ago they passed through the area.

classFeature.name: Expose Weakness
entries: At 1st level, by analysing patterns and drawing from your extensive experience, you gain the ability to expose a creature's weaknesses to you and your allies. Choose one of the following options to determine how you do so.

items.name: Analyse and Study
entries: Whenever you roll for initiative, choose a creature within 60 feet of you and make one of the following ability checks based on its creature type. The DC is equal to 10 + the creature's CR. On a success, the creature is Exposed.
You can use a reaction at the end of each of your turns to repeat the above ability check against a creature within 60 feet of you.

items.name: Observe Defences
entries: Whenever you score a major hit against a creature, you can use your reaction to Expose that creature.

items.name: Observe Offence
entries: Whenever a creature hits you, you can use your reaction to Expose that creature.

Whenever you Expose a creature, you reveal a random weakness it has based on its creature type by rolling a {@dice d8} on one of the following tables. This weakness remains until the creature dies. If you attempt to Expose a creature while a creature is already Exposed by you, the existing Exposed weakness disappears.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Biting Zephyr|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr}, {@filter Mirror's Glint|maneuvers|Tradition=mirror's glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=rapid current}, {@filter Razor's Edge|maneuvers|Tradition=razor's edge}, {@filter Spirited Steed|maneuvers|Tradition=spirited steed}, and {@filter Unending Wheel|maneuvers|Tradition=unending wheel}. You can learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Manuevers Known column of the Ranger table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.

Additionally, whenever you take a long rest, you can choose any number of the maneuvers you know and replace them with the same number of other maneuvers of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek
entries: Your wilderness survival instincts guide your physical and mental training. You gain one of the following features at 2nd level. You gain the other feature when you reach 5th level in this class.

entries.name: Hunter's Target
entries: You can use your bonus action to focus your attention on a specific enemy. Choose a creature you can see within 90 feet to mark as your quarry. For 1 hour, you are focused on this creature. You gain a +1 bonus to weapon armour attack rolls against the creature, and your weapon armour attacks against it deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage. In addition, you have advantage on {@skill Perception} and {@skill Survival} checks you make to find it. If the creature drops to 0 hit points before the hour ends, you can use a bonus action to mark a new quarry.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Swift Feet
entries: You can use your bonus action to move with greater agility. For 1 minute, your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks. You can choose to end this effect early to either increase your Speed by 30 feet until the end of your turn or gain advantage on a weapon armour attack.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek (2)
entries: You gain the other feature from your Stride and Seek feature.

classFeature.name: Trained Accuracy
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to maintain a level of discipline that spills over into everything you do. This perpetual state of focus allows you to perform incredible feats. You gain both of the following features.

entries.name: Accuracy Bonus
entries: When you make a weapon armour attack, you can use your reaction to gain an attack bonus or damage bonus as shown on the Accuracy Bonus table. Between long rests, you have a number of uses equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

entries.name: Accuracy Reserve
entries: If you have not moved since the start of your last turn, you gain a +1 bonus to weapon armour attack rolls during your turn.

classFeature.name: Exploitative
entries: From 3rd level, you become adept at identifying specific weaknesses in your enemies that you and your allies can exploit. Whenever you Expose a creature, you roll two {@dice d8}s instead of one and can choose one of the results rolled for the weakness that is revealed.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increases two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Wilderness Mystique
entries: At 4th level your experience as an expert survivalist manifests at the subconscious level. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Answering Silence
entries: When others seek your advice on wilderness matters, they often arrive at the correct conclusion despite (or perhaps because of) the silence with which you respond. When a friendly creature makes a {@skill Nature} or {@skill Survival} check while adjacent to you, it gains an expertise die. This affects their journey activities.

entries.name: Fearsome Mysticism
entries: Your uncanny abilities and single-minded focus can be unnerving to the uninitiated. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks against any creature that is neither proficient in Arcana nor casts spells. This affects the Rob journey activity.

entries.name: Kindred Spirit
entries: You intuitively sense when another wilderness expert is nearby. Your {@skill Perception} for the purposes of detecting hidden or invisible creatures within 30 feet of you increases by 5, and you instantly detect whether any creature you can see is proficient with {@skill Stealth} or {@skill Survival}.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Student of the Hunt
entries: At 6th level, you gain an expertise die on checks made to track non-humanoid creatures, as well as on ability checks to recall information about them. Additionally, you learn one exotic language of your choice.

classFeature.name: Flexible Exposure
entries: At 7th level, you can choose one additional exposure application option from your {@classFeature Expose Weakness|Ranger|sns|1} feature.

classFeature.name: Explorer's Instinct
entries: At 8th level, once between long rests, you can take two reactions in a round instead of one.

In addition, whenever you learn a new ranger exploration knack or replace an existing one, you can choose from druid {@filter secrets of nature|optionalfeatures|feature type=sn} or fighter {@filter soldiering knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=sk}.

classFeature.name: Flash of Steel
entries: At 9th level you gain the {@maneuver Missile Volley} or {@maneuver Whirlwind Strike} combat maneuver. It does not count against your number of maneuvers known and you do not have to spend exertion points to activate it.

classFeature.name: Stride and Seek Upgrade
entries: At 9th level, choose one of the following improvements to a {@classFeature Stride and Seek|Ranger|sns|2} feature you have.

entries.name: Hunter's Target
entries: You can remain focused on your Hunter's Target for up to 8 hours.

entries.name: Swift Feet
entries: Your Swift Feet now lasts for up to 1 hour.

classFeature.name: Trackless Hunting
entries: At 10th level, you can't be tracked except by magical means, unless you choose to leave a trail.
In addition, whenever you learn a new ranger exploration knack or replace an existing one, you can choose to learn a {@filter rogue skill trick|optionalfeatures|feature type=st} instead.

classFeature.name: Wilderness Lore
entries: At 12th level, your knowledge of wilderness lore deepens. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Big Game Traps
entries: Leveraging your skill with snaring small game, you take 10 minutes to set a trap or snare for a creature of Large size or smaller.
The Trap's DC is 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature can spot the trap by making a {@skill Perception} check against the trap's DC.
Describe the mechanics of your trap to the DM, including what triggers it and what happens when triggered. You may use the following examples or work with the DM to invent your own.
When a creature enters the trapped area or interacts with the trapped object it makes a save or triggers the trap.

items.name: Pendulum Trap
entries: Creatures adjacent to the trap are struck by a swinging object that deals bludeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (determined by the object) equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier.

items.name: Pit Trap
entries: The creature falls into a pre-existing pit or hazard that you have concealed.

items.name: Rope Trap
entries: The creature is either knocked {@condition prone} or {@condition restrained} by a net or rope.

entries.name: Waste Not
entries: With 10 minutes work you can scavenge valuable cuts from the corpse of a beast, dragon, monstrosity, or plant that has a CR of 1 or higher and has died within the last hour. If sold to a collector, druid, or other naturalist, this notable component is worth an amount of gold equal to 5 x the creature's CR (consult your DM to confirm this gold value).

classFeature.name: Guarded Post
entries: Experience has taught you that defending others is often the key to survival. At 13th level choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Defensive Marksman
entries: When a hostile creature moves towards you or an ally, you can use your reaction to make a ranged weapon attack against it.

entries.name: Hunter's Blind
entries: You can spend 10 minutes constructing a natural shelter able to accommodate you. The shelter blends in with its surroundings, and as long as you don't move into or out of it, the shelter is indistinguishable from a natural object. It is designed to allow you to see out while inside, but prevent anyone outside from seeing in.
The shelter is comfortable and dry, regardless of the weather outside, and counts as a Haven for you. Any other creature that tries to use your Hunter's Blind inadvertantly ruins it after 2d4 hours.

classFeature.name: Tactical Advantage
entries: At 14th level, you can choose one of the following ranger tactics:

entries.name: Covered Shot
entries: When you are protected by half cover, you gain the benefits of three-quarters cover.

entries.name: Nature's Shadow
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Stealth} checks.

classFeature.name: Survivalcraft
entries: At 16th level, basic survival skills have become second nature to you. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Under the Stars
entries: You gain the benefits of a long rest in half the normal amount of time.

entries.name: Woodlore
entries: Using your action, you can produce a flame at a moment's notice even in wet or cold environments. This allows you to ignore the penalties of wet or cold weather on your journey activities. You can hold the flame in your hand without harming yourself or your equipment. Without additional fuel, the flame lasts for 10 minutes, shedding bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

classFeature.name: Hunter's Intent
entries: At 17th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you inflict with a weapon armour attack.

classFeature.name: Flora and Fauna
entries: At 18th level choose one of the following benefits:

entries.name: Beastly Attack
entries: Once on each of your turns, a beast that is friendly to you and able to sense you can use its reaction to make a single basic weapon attack.

entries.name: High Ground
entries: You instantly analyse the natural foliage and features of a given terrain as well as how to leverage them to your advantage. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your walking and climbing speeds increase by 5 feet.
When you are protected by three-quarters cover, you gain the benefits of total cover.
If you make a weapon armour attack against a creature, your movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from it until the beginning of your next turn.

When you move through an area, you can cause it to become difficult terrain until the beginning of your next turn, and any creature that moves through the area during that time has disadvantage on its next attack roll.

classFeature.name: Extreme Exploration
entries: Your travels and training have taken you to new heights and amazing depths. At 19th level, you gain two of the following benefits:

items: You gain 50% resistance to fire and cold damage.
You gain immunity to poison.
You gain a swimming speed and climbing speed equal to your Speed.

classFeature.name: Nature's Scion
entries: At 20th level, you are a master of yourself and your natural surroundings. You become extremely familiar with all types of terrain and can use the {@optfeature Master Tracker} exploration knack at will.
