In addition, you learn Primordial if you don't already know it and gain one of the following:

entries.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: By focusing on a specific natural element within 100 feet (air, fire, earth, water, or wood) you can attract similar essences to you. After 10 minutes of meditation, a creature of that element is drawn to your presence and obeys your commands. This creature is based on a CR 7 generic minion (https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS), but with the following modifications:

items: The guardian speaks Primordial.
If the guardian is a fire guardian, its Melee attack deals fire damage. Otherwise, it deals bludgeoning damage.
If the guardian's Melee attack hits a Medium or smaller target, the target is {@condition grappled}, and the guardian uses its Engulf action on it.

The guardian gains the Engulf action: The guardian engulfs a Medium or smaller creature {@condition grappled} by it. The engulfed creature is {@condition blinded}, {@condition restrained}, and unable to breathe, and at the start of each of the guardian's turns it makes a save or suffers {@damage 2d8 + 4} bludgeoning harm (or fire for a fire guardian). If the guardian moves, the engulfed creature moves with it. The guardian can have only one creature engulfed at a time.

The guardian gains the Guard Life action: The guardian uses its Engulf on a willing, {@condition unconscious}, or {@condition incapacitated} ally without dealing damage. While engulfed, the ally is {@condition grappled} but not {@condition restrained}, can see and breathe normally, and has total cover. As soon as the ally takes an action, reaction, or bonus action, they are no longer engulfed. The guardian can release the ally at will (no action required).

entries: The creature disappears after 1 hour, when it is reduced to 0 hit point, or you use a bonus action to dismiss it. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Summon Stampede
entries: You can spend an action speaking directly to the spirit of nature itself to request assistance. All beasts within a 1-mile radius of you are friendly to you for 1 minute, and for the duration on initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), you can choose one of the following effects. You can't use the same effect two rounds in a row. Once you have used this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

items.name: Enraged Fauna
entries: Up to 3 Huge native creatures of up to CR 8 charge forward (use CR 8 generic minions - https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS), bowling into your enemies. Choose any number of creatures you can see within 100 feet to suffer {@dice 9d6} bludgeoning harm. In addition, you can choose one of the Huge creatures to stay and fight. You can have only one such creature assist in this way at a time, and it retreats when bloodied or when you command it to leave.

items.name: Rushing Stampede
entries: A Huge-sized horde of Small, Medium, and Large native creatures hurry through the area, traveling in a 30'-wide line from a direction you choose. Until the start of your next turn, the area is difficult terrain. In addition, each creature of your choice in the area suffers {@dice 5d8} bludgeoning harm. Each target must also make a save, becoming {@condition prone} and {@condition debilitated} until the start of your next turn on a fail. On a successful save, a target is {@condition rattled} until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Swarming Pests
entries: Several Medium-sized swarms of Tiny native creatures enter the spaces of up to 5 Small, Medium, or Large creatures you choose, or the space of a Huge or Gargantuan creature. Each creature in the swarm's area becomes {@condition blinded} and {@condition slowed} until the start of your next turn, and loses concentration on any effect it is concentrating on.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Adroit Defence
entries: At 1st level, you learn special defensive techniques. Choose one of the following options.

entries.name: Agile Defence
entries: While you are unarmoured and are not wielding a shield, your base armour is equal to half your Wisdom modifier.

entries.name: Brutal Defence
entries: You are proficient with light armour. While you are wearing light armour, you replace its base armour with half your Strength modifier if it is higher. If you know the Adept Speed practiced technique or any other that has it has a prerequisite, you can use them while wearing light armour.

classFeature.name: Martial Arts
entries: At 1st level, your trail to perfection gives you mastery of combat styles that use unarmed strikes and adept weapons, which are quarterstaves, punching daggers, shortswords, and any simple melee weapons that don't have the Two-Handed trait.
You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only adept weapons and you aren't wielding a shield:

items: You can choose to govern the attacks of your main hand unarmed or adept weapons with either Strength or Dexterity.

You can roll a {@dice d4} in place of the normal base damage die of your main hand unarmed or adept weapons. This die changes as you gain levels, as shown in the Martial Arts column of the Adept table.
You can use a bonus action to add your weapon's base damage to the next unarmed or adept weapon attack you make before the end of your turn.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Mirror's Glint|maneuvers|Tradition=mirror's glint}, {@filter Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=rapid current}, {@filter Razor's Edge|maneuvers|Tradition=razor's edge}, {@filter Unending Wheel|maneuvers|Tradition=unending wheel}. You can learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Manuevers Known column of the Adept table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you take a long rest, you can replace any of your maneuvers with new ones of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.
As an adept, you gain a +1 bonus to non-armour attack rolls made by your maneuvers.

classFeature.name: Exertion Focus
entries: At 2nd level, your training allows you to harness your mental energy into a supernatural state of focus. Some cultures refer to this energy as prana, pneuma, or ki. Your access to this energy is represented by your exertion pool.

You can spend exertion points to fuel various focus features. You start knowing three such features: {@optfeature Flurry of Blows}, {@optfeature Long Step}, and {@optfeature Patient Defence}. You learn more focus features as you gain levels in this class.

classFeature.name: Practiced Techniques
entries: You have picked up a number of tricks in your path towards perfection. At 2nd level you gain a {@filter practiced technique|optionalfeatures|feature type=pt} of your choice. The Techniques Known column of the Adept table shows when you learn more practiced techniques. Unless otherwise noted, you can gain each technique only once.

classFeature.name: Focus Feature
entries: Focus features stem from the mastery of your internal energies. At 3rd level, and again every level after that, choose one option from the list of {@filter focused features|optionalfeatures|feature type=ff}. Some features have prerequisites, such as minimum level or another focus feature. You must meet those prerequisites before you choose that focus feature.

classFeature.name: Battlefield Etiquette
entries: At 18th level, you learn the unwritten rules of the battlefield. After you have fought beside (or against) a humanoid whose highest ability score is either Strength or Dexterity, you have advantage on Charisma checks against it.

classFeature.name: Bonus Exertion
entries: At 4th level, your self-awareness and discipline allow you to draw upon deeper reserves of willpower than other warriors. Your exertion pool increases by the amount listed in the Bonus Exertion column of Table: Adept. For example, at 4th level your exertion pool increases to 5, at 5th level when your proficiency bonus increases your exertion pool becomes 7, at 6th level your exertion pool becomes 8, and so on.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Empty Mind
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can empty your mind and easily disguise your emotions. As an action, you can spend 1 exertion to enter a meditative empty mind state. This lasts for 1 hour. While in this state, {@skill Insight} checks against you have disadvantage.
In addition, enchantment spells have disadvantage on their non-armour attack rolls against you and you gain 50% resistance to psychic damage.

classFeature.name: Acquired Knowledge
entries: Starting at 10th level, your study of different martial arts traditions and foreign cultures pays off in unexpected ways. Choose two languages you are not proficient with. You become proficient with both.

classFeature.name: Great Reputation
entries: Starting at 14th level, your reputation spreads and you become famous (or infamous) among the adept community. When you interact with any humanoid whose highest ability score is either Strength or Dexterity, their initial attitude towards you is friendly.

classFeature.name: Grandmaster
entries: At 20th level, you finally achieve true mastery and become more than adept - you are a grandmaster. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Death Blow
entries: You learn how to strike with incredible force at your target's very life force. When you hit with a melee weapon armour attack, you can choose to make your attack a critical hit that deals maximum damage. In addition, if the target is a creature it makes a save, falling to 0 hit points on a fail. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Perfection Achieved
entries: You finally achieve true mastery. Any time you roll your martial arts die, you can spend 1 exertion to roll a {@dice d12} instead. If you roll a 12 on this die, you can roll another {@dice d12} and add it to the result.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Pact Magic
entries: With care, practice, and dedication you have learned how to best siphon and channel the otherworldly powers you've gained from your patron.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the warlock spell list. You learn additional warlock cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Warlock table.

entries.name: Spell Points
entries: The Warlock table shows how many spell points you have. To cast one of your warlock spells of 1st-level or higher, you must spend a number of spell points dependent on the spell's level, shown on the table below. You can also cast a spell you know at a higher spell level by spending the appropriate number of spell points. The maximum spell level you can cast is shown in the Spell Level column of the Warlock table. You regain all expended spell points when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
entries: At 1st level, you know two 1st-level spells of your choice from the warlock spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Warlock table shows when you learn more warlock spells of your choice of 1st level and higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level. When you reach 6th level, for example, you learn a new warlock spell, which can be 1st, 2nd, or 3rd level.

Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the warlock spells you know and replace it with another spell from the warlock spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: The manner in which you pursue ever greater power from your patron is defined by the nature of that pursuit: through wit, devotion, or fervor. Choose Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma to be your spellcasting ability. Once you make this choice, you cannot change it. You use the chosen ability whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item arcane focus} as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Blast
entries: At 1st level you learn to use your patron's power as a weapon, blasting enemies with an eldritch force that defies even the laws of magic. Choose one of the following features.

entries.name: Eldritch Disturbance
entries: You can use an action to strike a creature within 60 feet with a reality-distorting curse, blending pain both mental and physical. Make a ranged spell will attack against the creature that deals {@dice 1d12} force damage.

entries.name: Eldritch Ray
entries: You can use an action to create a deadly beam that strikes a creature within 120 feet. Make a ranged spell armour attack against the creature that deals {@dice 1d10} force damage.

entries.name: Eldritch Scythe
entries: Once per turn when you would make a melee weapon attack as part of the Attack action or an opportunity attack, you can instead make a melee spell armour attack, lashing out with an ephemeral and unearthly blade. It deals {@dice 1d8} force damage. In addition, on a hit, you can choose a creature that you can see and that is within your reach. It suffers half as much damage as force harm.

entries.name: Eldritch Whip
entries: You can use an action to thrash a creature with a scintillating tendril of arcane force. Make a melee spell armour attack against a creature within 15 feet that deals {@dice 1d6} force damage.

At the end of your turn you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to half the damage you dealt with Eldritch Whip this turn (minimum 1), which last until the end of your next turn. When you damage a creature with your Eldritch Whip and you already have temporary hit points, you can end the temporary hit points early to gain a bonus to the Eldritch Whip's damage equal to half the number of temporary hit points you ended early.

classFeature.name: Expanded Spell List (First)
entries: At 1st level, if your first subclass does not provide you with an expanded spell list, you choose an expanded spell list from below that your patron grants you access to. Choose one patron expanded spell list. These spells count as warlock spells for you.

entries.caption: Darkness Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Detect Poison and Disease}, {@spell Inflict Wounds}

entries.caption: Fire Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Burning Hands}, {@spell Fog Cloud} (appears as smoke)

entries.caption: Genius Loci Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Meld Into Stone}, {@spell Speak with Dead}

classFeature.name: Expanded Spell List (Second)
entries: At 11th level, if your second subclass does not provide you with an expanded spell list, you choose an expanded spell list from below that your patron grants you access to. Choose one patron expanded spell list. These spells count as warlock spells for you.
You cannot choose the same expanded spell list that you may have chosen for your first expanded spell list.

entries.caption: Darkness Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Detect Poison and Disease}, {@spell Inflict Wounds}

entries.caption: Fire Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Burning Hands}, {@spell Fog Cloud} (appears as smoke)

entries.caption: Genius Loci Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Meld Into Stone}, {@spell Speak with Dead}

classFeature.name: Eldritch Invocations
entries: The gifts of your patron are manyfold — not only can you channel their magic to work spells, but you can also siphon away talents, tricks, and reality-defying essence to perform other impressive magical feats.

At 2nd level, you gain an eldritch invocation of your choice. Your invocation options can be found on the {@filter Optional Features|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=EI} page. When you gain certain warlock levels, you gain additional invocations of your choice, as shown in the Invocations Known column of the Warlock table. Additionally, when you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the invocations you know and replace it with another invocation that you could learn at your level. If you should gain a bonus invocation, they do not count against your Invocations known.
When you are able to learn a new invocation in this way, you may instead choose to learn a {@filter secret of arcana|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=SA}.

classFeature.name: Secrets of Arcana
entries: Also at 2nd level, you realize that there is power to be taken that your patron may never have meant for you to have. You gain a secret of arcana of your choice. Your secret options can be found on the {@filter Optional Features|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=SA} page. The Secrets Known column of the Warlock table shows when you learn more secrets of arcana.

classFeature.name: Pact Boon
entries: At 3rd level, the entity to which you are beholden provides a more durable and lasting blessing. Choose one of the following features.

entries.name: Pact of the Blade
entries: You can use your action to conjure in your empty hand an armament bestowed with power: a pact weapon. Choose any simple or martial weapon when you conjure your pact weapon. You are proficient with it while you wield your pact weapon, and you can use your spellcasting ability as its governing ability. This weapon ignores resistance and immunity to its base damage type. Your pact weapon disappears if it is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more. It also disappears if you use this feature again, if you dismiss the weapon (no action required), or if you die.

By performing a 1 hour ritual while holding a weapon, you can transform it into your pact weapon. Any affixes on this weapon that empower the weapon are also applied to your Eldritch Blast (ranged weapons apply these effects to Eldritch Ray, and melee weapons to Eldritch Scythe; it has no effect on Eldritch Disturbance or Eldritch Whip). You can then dismiss the weapon, shunting it into an extradimensional space, and it appears whenever you create your pact weapon thereafter. You can't affect a sentient weapon in this way. The weapon ceases being your pact weapon if you die, if you perform the ritual on a different weapon, or if you use a 1 hour ritual to break your bond to it. The weapon is collected by your patron if the bond breaks while it is in the extradimensional space.

entries.name: Pact of the Chain
entries: You learn the {@spell find familiar} spell and can cast it as a ritual. The spell doesn't count against your number of spells known. When you cast the spell, you can choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature imp}, {@creature pseudodragon}, {@creature quasit}, or {@creature sprite}. You can also opt for the spirit you call forth to be an aberration, elemental, or undead.

When you take the Attack action, you can forgo making an attack to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own with its reaction. Additionally, when you use Eldritch Scythe, you can choose to deliver the attack through your familiar as though you had cast a spell with a range of touch.

entries.name: Pact of the Tome
entries: Aware of your drive for knowledge, you awaken to find a grimoire left in your pack by your patron: a Book of Shadows. Choose three cantrips. While the book is in your possession, you know these cantrips and they are treated as warlock spells. They don't count against your number of cantrips known. If you lose your Book of Shadows, you can perform a 1 hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony destroys the previous book. The book is collected by your patron when you die.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Invocation of the Courts (1)
entries: Also at 4th level, you gain a bonus invocation — one to help you traffic in courts both otherworldly and mortal. Choose one invocation from the following list: {@optfeature Beguiling Influence}, {@optfeature Courts of the Outer Realms}, {@optfeature Eyes of the Runekeeper}, {@optfeature Gaze of Two Minds}, {@optfeature Mask of Many Faces}, {@optfeature Ominous Tormentor}, {@optfeature Psionic Secrecy}, {@optfeature Spellbinding Contract}, {@optfeature Voice of the Chain Master}, {@optfeature Whispers of the Grave}.

You gain another bonus invocation in this way at 10th level and again at 16th level. Whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the bonus invocations you know and replace it with another bonus invocation from this list that you could learn at that level.

classFeature.name: Empowered Blast
entries: Beginning at 5th level, your Eldritch Blast becomes more vicious.
Your Eldritch Blast gains an additional die of damage at 5th level, and again at 11th level and 17th level (to a maximum of 4 damage dice).
This feature otherwise counts as Empowered Attack (for the purpose of qualifying for feats, spell effects, and the like).

classFeature.name: Eldritch Evolution (1)
entries: At 6th level, you gain a bonus invocation — one which represents your growth in the lethal lore behind Eldritch Blast. Choose one invocation from the following list: {@optfeature Agonizing Blast}, {@optfeature Eldritch Bind}, {@optfeature Eldritch Elementalism}, {@optfeature Eldritch Grasp}, {@optfeature Eldritch Prism}, {@optfeature Eldritch Riposte}, {@optfeature Eldritch Severance}, {@optfeature Eldritch Spear}, {@optfeature Eldritch Squall}, {@optfeature Eldritch Tentacle}, {@optfeature Repelling Blast}, {@optfeature Versatile Blast}.

You gain another bonus invocation in this way at 14th level and again at 18th level. Whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the bonus invocations you know and replace it with another bonus invocation from this list that you could learn at that level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Invocation of the Courts (2)
entries: At 10th level, you gain another bonus invocation from your Invocation of the Courts feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Evolution (2)
entries: At 14th level, you gain another bonus invocation from your Eldritch Evolution feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Invocation of the Courts (3)
entries: At 16th level, you gain another bonus invocation from your Invocation of the Courts feature.

classFeature.name: Eldritch Evolution (3)
entries: At 18th level, you gain another bonus invocation from your Eldritch Evolution feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Dread Thaumaturge
entries: At 20th level, you have learned all you can from your patron. You gain the following abilities.

entries.name: Aura of Anathema
entries: Hostile creatures within 120 feet gain vulnerability to necrotic damage. A creature with resistance to necrotic damage instead loses its resistance, and a creature immune to necrotic damage instead replaces that immunity with 50% resistance.
Whenever you finish a long rest, you can choose a different damage type for your aura.

entries.name: Highest Arcanum
entries: You learn an ultimate exploit in the laws of magic. Choose one of the following:

items: You can cast {@spell plane shift} without material components once between short rests.
You are under the constant effects of the {@spell foresight} spell. This effect cannot be detected or dispelled by any means short of a {@spell wish} spell.
Once per long rest, you can spend 1 minute in arcane contemplation to regain your spent spell points.

classFeature.name: Adroit Defence
entries: From 1st level, you constantly analyse combat situations to improve your defensive posture, proactively interfering to guide attacks away from yourself. While you are wearing unarmoured body armour, your armour is increased by half your Intelligence modifier. You cannot use a shield and still gain this benefit.
The savant is not proficient in armour by default, but might gain those proficiencies through other means. When you are wearing armoured body armour, your armour is increased by 1.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Clever Schemes
entries: At 1st level, you learn one {@filter clever scheme|optionalfeatures|feature type=cs} of your choice. The Schemes Known column of the Savant table shows when you learn more clever schemes.

classFeature.name: Savant Tricks
entries: At 1st level, you have developed a small number of clever gambits, deft maneuvers, and canny guards which help you prevail in battle by using your wits. The number of tricks you know is listed on the Savant table, and you can choose from the list below and from the list of tricks your subclass may make available.

To use a trick, you must first prepare it by spending a bonus action. You cannot prepare a trick outside of an encounter, but once a trick is prepared, it remains prepared until you use it, until the encounter ends, or until you spend a bonus action to replace it with a different trick. You can only have on trick prepared at a time.
Different tricks can be used at different times. Some require your action, bonus action, or reaction. Others might be used when making attacks.
Whenever you finish a long rest, you can choose to replace any number of tricks you know with different tricks.

entries.name: Types of Tricks
entries: Some tricks are called Aegises, which let you use your reaction to defend against some sort of attack. Other tricks are called Flourishes, which you can use when you make attacks.

If a trick offers a save, after you use it against a particular creature, that creature has advantage on saves against other uses of that same trick until the end of combat. If the trick doesn't permit a save, after you use it against a particular creature, you cannot use the same trick against that creature for the rest of the combat.

items.name: Attention Diverting Aegis
entries: When a creature within 30 feet that can sense you makes an armour attack that isn't targeting you, you can use this trick to try to distract the attacker. If it fails a save, until your next turn it has disadvantage on all armour attack rolls it makes that aren't against you.

items.name: Canny Dodge Aegis
entries: When an armour or reflex attack would hit you, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's success tier by 1.

items.name: Committee Defence Aegis
entries: When a creature makes an armour attack against you, if it is not the first creature to attack you since the start of your last turn, you find an avenue of opportunity amid the massed assault. You can use this trick to impose disadvantage on that creature's attack. Then, you gain advantage on the next attack roll you make before the end of your next turn that targets a creature that attacked you this round.

items.name: Improvised Bastion Aegis
entries: When a creature would damage you, you can use this trick to devise a momentary defence (using a chair as a shield, predicting a safe spot in an explosion, diluting a spray of acid with a solvent, etc.). You gain 50% resistance to one type of damage you would take, which protects you only against the triggering damage.

items.name: Mindful Reason Aegis
entries: When you would be hit by a reflex or will attack, you can use this trick to reduce the attack's success tier by 1.

items.name: Serpentine Rush Aegis
entries: When you are targeted by a ranged armour attack, you can use this trick to move your speed. Until the end of your next turn, ranged weapon attacks against you have disadvantage. Additionally, your movement might get you to a location where cover makes you hard or impossible to hit. Reduce your speed on your next turn by the distance that you move when using this trick.

items.name: Tangled Dance Aegis
entries: When you would be hit with an armour attack and a creature other than the attacker is adjacent to you, you can use this trick to try to dodge so the attack hits that creature. If the attacker fails a save, change the attack's target to another creature within 5 feet, and the attacker uses the same result of its attack roll.

items.name: Undermining Taunt Aegis
entries: When a creature fails to hit with an attack, you can use this trick to capitalise on their failure, warning the creature why another possible course of action will also turn out badly. If that creature can understand you, choose an action, such as Attack or Cast a Spell. The trget must make a save. If it fails, until the end of its next turn it is {@condition debilitated} while attempting to take that action.

items.name: Antagonising Flourish
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can try to draw its ire. If it fails a save, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Disarming Flourish
entries: WHen you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can attempt to disarm it. If it fails a save, its grip is loosened, and the creature cannot make use of the item until it spends a bonus action on its turn to regain a solid hold. If the creature has disadvantage on the save and fails both rolls, it drops the item.

items.name: Experimental Flourish
entries: Whenever you fail to hit with an armour attack, you improvise a follow-up that doesn't directly attack a foe, such as slicing a rope to pin an enemy with a chandelier or smashing a pipe to spray blinding steam on an enemy. Circumstances will dictate what the effect is, but some examples include shoving or imposing the {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition grappled}, or {@condition prone} condition, and may also deal environmental damage equal to your Intelligence modifier. Any conditions imposed should seldom last more than one round. Creatures affected can make a save to anticipate your trick and avoid the effect.

items.name: Guiding Flourish
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can try to trick it into moving. If it fails a save, it shifts up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice. If this movement would cause it to take damage (such as by falling or entering fire), the creature has advantage on this save.

items.name: Menacing Flourish
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend a bonus action to activate this trick and deliver a terrifying threat. The target makes a save. If it fails, for the next minute it is {@condition frightened} of you.

items.name: Surgical Flourish
entries: When you have advantage on a melee weapon attack and both die results are high enough to hit, choose one of the creature's limbs or eyes to disable. If the creature succeeds a save, you disable that body part only until the end of your next turn. If it fails the save, the body part is disabled until it can take a short rest.

A creature is {@condition debilitated} when attempting to use a disabled limb to attack, and gains no armour benefit from a shield held by a disabled limb. A creature with a disabled leg has its speed reduced by half, it falls {@condition prone} after using the Dash action, and it has disadvantage on Dexterity checks to balance. A creature with a disabled eye has disadvantage on {@skill Perception} checks that rely on sight and on ranged weapon attack rolls.

items.name: Assess Vulnerability
entries: When you make an armour attack against a creature, you can use your Intelligence modifier in place of another ability score modifier for the attack's damage. This applies to all armour attacks you make against it before the start of your next turn, but not to armour attacks you make against other creatures.

items.name: Choreographed Disappearance
entries: On your turn, you say something to turn a foe's attention away from you or an ally. You or an ally of your choice who can understand you can move up to their speed. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from one creature of your choice that can understand you. If they end up having obscurement or cover relative to the distracted foe, they can immediately Hide as a free action.

items.name: Defending Advice
entries: Spend a bonus action to advise an ally who can understand you. Choose a non-armour defence. One time before the end of this encounter, that ally can reduce the success tier of an attack of that defence type by 1.

items.name: Direct Ally
entries: You identify an opening an ally can take advantage of. You can spend an action and choose an ally that can understand you, then choose a target. If that ally hits that target with an armour attack before the start of your next turn, their attack deals an extra {@dice 1d8} damage. Thr bonus dice increase to {@dice 2d8} at 5th level, {@dice 3d8} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d8} at 17th level. After you use this trick, you can choose the same ally again, but cannot choose the same target for the rest of the encounter.

items.name: Frightful Suppression
entries: When you make an armour attack that causes loud noises - a firearm or trap, or also any power that deals thunder damage - you can use this trick to force the attack's target (or creatures in the attack's area) to make a save to gauge when it is safe to move, and thus avoid being pinned down. A creature that fails is {@condition sluggish} and can't move while adjacent to you until the start of your next turn unless it spends an action to break out of the suppression.

items.name: Rallying Word
entries: You know just what to say to inspire an ally's flagging stamina. You can spend a bonus action to let an ally within 30 feet who can understand you spend a Hit Die. If it does, it rolls that Hit Die plus {@dice 1d8} and heals hit points equal to the total. The bonus dice increase to {@dice 2d8} at 5th level, {@dice 3d8} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d8} at 17th level. After you use this ability, the same ally cannot benefit from it again until you complete a short rest.

items.name: Sweeping Stride
entries: When you move at least 10 feet and enter a space adjacent to a creature no more than one size larger than you, you can try to trip it. If the creature fails a save, it falls {@condition prone}. If it succeeds, instead your movement ends in the space you entered to use this trick.

items.name: Timely Tool
entries: Spend a bonus action to use an item that normally can be used as an action. This cannot cause damage or make an attack. Examples include administering an antitoxin, potion, or other easy-to-swallow item to a willing creature within reach; lighting a torch; tossing out caltrops, or barring a door.

items.name: Unbalancing Intervention
entries: When a creature within your reach makes a Strength or Dexterity ability check or receives a fortitude or reflex attack, you can use your reaction to perform a series of pulls, shoves, and strikes that put a creature off-balance. The creature has disadvantage on its ability check or the attack roll has advantage.

classFeature.name: Combat Poise
entries: At 2nd level, you choose one of the following poises.

entries.name: A Step Ahead
entries: You have a deft ability to predict your opponents' responses and interfere with them. Whenever a creature within your reach that you are aware of attempts to take a reaction, you can use your reaction to try to disrupt them. That creature must make a save, wasting their reaction on a failure.

entries.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: You gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain an exertion point pool equal to your proficiency bonus.
Choose two martial traditions. Whenever you gain a savant trick, you can instead choose a maneuver from any of your chosen martial traditions.

You can initially learn maneuvers of the 1st degree. At 7th level you gain access to 2nd degree maneuvers, then at 13th level you can access 3rd degree maneuvers, and finally at 19th level you can access 4th degree maneuvers.
Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Analysed Need
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you complete a short or long rest you can choose a skill. Until you complete another rest, whenever you make an ability check using that skill, you use Intelligence instead of the ability score it normally uses.

classFeature.name: Skill Focus
entries: At 3rd level, and again at 7th, 11th, 15th, and 19th, choose a skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks with that skill.

classFeature.name: Skill Focus
entries: At 7th level, choose a skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks with that skill.

classFeature.name: Skill Focus
entries: At 11th level, choose a skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks with that skill.

classFeature.name: Skill Focus
entries: At 15th level, choose a skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks with that skill.

classFeature.name: Skill Focus
entries: At 19th level, choose a skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks with that skill.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Signature Move
entries: At 6th level, choose one savant trick you know. You are considered to always have that trick prepared, and it does not count against the limit of tricks you can have prepared. When you use that trick, it does not stop being prepared. Whenever you complete a long rest, you can change your signature move.

classFeature.name: Developed Poise
entries: At 5th level, you refine your combat poise. Choose one of the following, or one of the combat poise options available at 2nd level.

entries.name: Empowered Attack
entries: When you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

entries.name: Rational Maneuvers
entries: Non-armour attack rolls made through your maneuvers gain a +1 bonus.

classFeature.name: Intelligent Caution
entries: At 7th level, whenever you complete a long rest, you may choose one non-armour defence to gain a +1 bonus to until you use this feature again.

classFeature.name: Quick Wits
entries: Also at 7th level, on your turn you can prepare a trick without spending a bonus action. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. Thereafter, preparing tricks requires the usual bonus action until you complete a short rest.

classFeature.name: Focused Defence
entries: At 9th level, you can use the confusion of a large battle to protect you. If there are at least two enemies within 30 feet, you can use a bonus action to choose one of them. Until the end of your next turn, that creature has disadvantage on armour attack rolls against you.

classFeature.name: More Tricks
entries: At 10th level, you can have two tricks prepared at once, in addition to your signature move. You still require a bonus action to prepare a trick.

classFeature.name: Exceptional Poise
entries: At 14th level, your combat poise can achieve remarkable things. Choose one of the following, or one of the combat poise options available at 2nd or 5th level.

entries.name: Confounding Defence
entries: The first time each round that you use an aegis, you can immediately prepare another aegis trick.

entries.name: The Opportune Moment
entries: On your turn, you can take one additional action. The additional action that you take cannot make any attacks. You cannot use this ability on the first round of combat. After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Clockwork Mind
entries: At 15th level, your mental capabilities transcend the normal limitations mortal minds face. You gain an expertise die on all Intelligence checks and a +1 bonus to will.

classFeature.name: Nothing That Can't Be Solved
entries: At 18th level, you can overcome obstacles with ease. When a creature that can understand you starts its turn you can grant it the ability to ignore all sources of disadvantage until the start of its next turn. After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Ultimate Schema
entries: At 20th level, you're clever enough to accomplish anything. Whenever you complete a long rest, choose an effect that could be accomplished by a spell of level 8 or lower. You can have that effect occur immediately, or keep it in reserve for the day, and cause it to occur at any point by spending an action. This can be because your acquired a magic item or spellcaster willing to perform the magic, or have a device or hirelings capable of matching the effect.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have awoken a latent magic within yourself caused by something you were exposed to, a quirk of an ancestor that has now manifested in you, supernatural experimentation, or something else entirely. This conduit, which you may not yet fully understand, fuels your spellcasting.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know four cantrips of your choice from the {@filter sorcerer spell list|spells|level=0|class=sorcerer (sns)}. You learn additional sorcerer cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Sorcerer table.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Sorcerer table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your sorcerer spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of sorcerer spells that are available for you to cast. To do so, choose a number of sorcerer spells from the {@filter sorcerer spell list|spells|class=sorcerer (sns)} equal to the value in the Spells Prepared column of the Sorcerer table. The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you're a 3rd-level sorcerer, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. Your list of prepared spells can include four spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination, chosen from the sorcerer spell list. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell magic missile}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or a 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your sorcerer spells, as your magic is fueled by an attunement to your very soul. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item arcane focus} as a spellcasting focus for your sorcerer spells.

classFeature.name: Font of Magic
entries: At 2nd level, you've begun to understand the well of power within you that you draw from to cast your magics. Up until now, it's just been something abstract, maybe hardly understood, but now you can use it in a precise manner to fuel your magics. It grants you a new resource in the form of Sorcery Points, as well as a way to use them.

entries.name: Sorcery Points
entries: You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer table. You can never have more Sorcery Points than shown on the table for your level. You regain all spent Sorcery Points when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Flexible Casting
entries: You can use your Sorcery Points to gain additional spell slots, or sacrifice spell slots to gain additional Sorcery Points.

items.name: Conversion
entries: You can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot, or vice versa, as a bonus action. The Creating Spell Slots table shows the cost of creating a spell slot of a given level, or the value of sacrificing a spell slot for Sorcery Points. You cannot convert spell slots of 6th-level or higher.

classFeature.name: Manifestation
entries: At 2nd level, you also choose one of the following manifestations, which represents a kind of energy you are especially effective at producing. When you cast a spell that deals acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, or thunder damage, you can change the damage type to your manifestation's damage type.

entries.name: Blizzard (Cold)
entries: When you cast a spell that deals cold damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point and choose one creature you can see. If that creature takes cold damage from the spell, it is also chilled, reducing its movement speeds by 10 feet for a number of rounds equal to the level of the spell (minimum 1 round).

entries.name: Corrosion (Acid)
entries: When you cast a spell that deals acid damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point and choose one creature you can see. If that creature takes acid damage from the spell, its armour is reduced by 2 until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Hurricane (Lightning or Thunder)
entries: You can treat both lightning and thunder damage as your manifestation's damage types. When you cast a spell that deals lightning or thunder damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point to produce a thunderous blast around you. Large or smaller creatures adjacent to you must make a save, being pushed back 10 feet and knocked {@condition prone} on a fail.

entries.name: Inferno (Fire)
entries: When you cast a spell that deals fire damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point and choose one creature you can see. If that creature takes fire damage from the spell, it is also set ablaze, suffering {@dice 2d4} ongoing fire harm for a number of rounds equal to the spell's level, or until it uses an action to put itself out.

entries.name: Venom (Poison)
entries: When you cast a spell that deals poison damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point, causing creatures immune to poison damage to take half damage, and creatures resistant to poison damage to take full damage.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Latent Power
entries: By 3rd level, your power starts to manifest as something people can subconsciously sense, even if they know nothing beyond the mundane, and it affects how they see you. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: All Eyes
entries: You can an expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks. In addition, you can use your magic to make your presence known. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 sorcery point to put out a subtle pulse that causes all creatures within 60 feet to take notice of you, allowing you to speak and be heard even in the midst of chaos. During combat, this notice lasts only a split second - not long enough to distract, but allowing you to make a Charisma check as part of the bonus action used to activate this featutre.

entries.name: Daunting Energy
entries: You can an expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks. If you fail an ability check against a creature trying to intimidate you, or an attack would cause you to become {@condition frightened}, you can spend 1 sorcery point to immediately reroll the check or attack.

entries.name: Unsuspecting
entries: You can an expertise die on {@skill Deception} checks. After you make a {@skill Deception} check against a humanoid, you can use a combination of fast-talk and your magic to make them forget your misstep, spending 1 sorcery point to reroll the check. On a success, their memory skips a beat and fills in the gap with your new story. On a failure from the reroll, not only do they not believe you, but anyone who can cast spells and observed the exchange (including your target, if applicable) knows that arcane subterfuge was attempted.

classFeature.name: Metamagics
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two minor Metamagic options, and one moderate Metamagic option. You gain another minor Metamagic option at 5th level, 10th level, 14th level, and 18th level. You gain an additional moderate Metamagic option at 10th and 18th level. Whenever you complete a long rest, you can replace any Metamagic options with new ones.
You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted.

items.name: Delayed Spell
entries: When you ready a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point. The spell can be held for up to 1 minute, instead of 1 round.

items.name: Distant Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that has a range of 5 feet or greater, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double the range of the spell. Alternatively, if you cast a spell hat has a range of touch, you can spend 1 sorcery point to make the range of the spell 20 feet.

items.name: Enduring Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 sorcery point to double the duration, to a maximum of 24 hours.

items.name: Reliable Spell
entries: When you roll a 4 or lower on a spell attack, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll the attack. You can use Reliable Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

items.name: Subdual Spell
entries: When damage or harm from one of your spells reduces one or more creature to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction and spend 1 sorcery point to make that spell non-lethal, even if it usually wouldn't be possible.

items.name: Subtle Spell
entries: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any vocalised or seen components.

items.name: Treacherous Spell
entries: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to alter its components in subtle ways. A creature attempting to identify the spell perceives it as a different spell of your choice, of the same or lower level. Attempts to {@spell counterspell} this spell are made at disadvantage.

items.name: Careful Spell
entries: When you cast a spell with an instantaneous duration that affects an area, you can spend 1 sorcery point, and choose a number of creatures up to your Charisma modifier in the area (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature is unaffected by the spell.

items.name: Empowered Spell
entries: When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls. You can use Empowered Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

items.name: Heightened Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that makes a non-armour attack, you can spend 3 Sorcery Points to give that attack advantage against one target of the spell.

items.name: Quickened Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting. As part of this, any attacks the spell may make are converted to requiring the target to make a save, where a success counts as a critical failure on the original attack, and a failure counts as a standard success. Additionally, any damage the spell deals is caused as harm instead.

items.name: Reflexive Spell
entries: Immediately before rolling initiative, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to cast a spell of up to 3rd-level that has a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action. The spell activates before any other actions in initiative, but doing so uses your action for your first turn in the combat.

items.name: Refracting Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that involves a damaging spell armour attack and targets only a single creature, you can spend an additional 2 Sorcery Points. If the attack hits, a creature adjacent to the original target suffers half the attack's damage as harm of the attack's base type.

items.name: Twinned Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of Self, you can spend a number of Sorcery Points equal to the spell's level (or 1 for a cantrip) to target a second creature in range with the same spell.

To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell's current level. For example, {@spell magic missile} and {@spell scorching ray} are ineligible, but {@spell ray of frost} can be modified by Twinned Spell.

classFeature.name: Additional Metamagics
entries: At 5th level, you gain one additional minor Metamagic option.

classFeature.name: Additional Metamagics
entries: At 10th level, you gain one additional minor Metamagic option and one additional major Metamagic option.

classFeature.name: Additional Metamagics
entries: At 14th level, you gain one additional minor Metamagic option.

classFeature.name: Additional Metamagics
entries: At 18th level, you gain one additional minor Metamagic option and one additional major Metamagic option.

classFeature.name: Arcane Innovations
entries: At 4th level, you acquire insights in arcana that others would never realise. You gain an {@filter arcane innovation|optionalfeatures|feature type=ai} of your choice. Your arcane innovations are detailed at the end of the class description. The Innovations Known column of the Sorcerer table shows when you gain additional innovations.

classFeature.name: Arcane Refinement
entries: At 6th level, your command over your magic becomes more precise, and you are able to assert more control over it. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Deep Font
entries: You can reach beyond your limits to draw more power from within. When you finish a short rest, you may expend a number of Hit Dice up to your Charisma modifier to regain some of your magic essence. For each hit die expended in this way, you regain 2 Sorcery Points. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Patient Aptitude
entries: You may pick a number of spells with the ritual tag equal to your Charisma modifier. These spells need not be from the sorcerer spell list, but they must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You can cast these spells as rituals, though you don't gain the ability to cast them normally with spell slots, nor do they count toward your spells prepared. If you increase your Charisma modifier, the number of ritual spells you know also increases. Whenever you finish a long rest, you may replace any of these ritual spells with new ones.

entries.name: Prodigious Innovator
entries: Choose three cantrips from any spell list. These cantrips don't count towards your cantrips known.

classFeature.name: Rapturous Presence
entries: Beginning at 9th level, your magic becomes a presence that noticeably bears down on creatures. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Larger Than Life
entries: Your power tends to distort people's perception of you, causing you to seem more dangerous than you are. You have advantage on {@skill Intimidation} checks made to threaten someone with your magic. This benefits your Mug journey activity checks. On a failure, your target is unconvinced and unless you make good on your threat with an unambiguous show of force to prove your mettle, this feature no longer functions until you prove your reputation again. Additionally, features and traits that attempt to evaluate how tough you are, such as the fighter's Size Up, always give the impression that you are 3 levels higher than you actually are.

entries.name: Nobody's Fool
entries: People find it difficult to lie to you or to conceal their motives in your presence. You gain a bonus to Insight checks equal to your Charisma modifier. In addition, you can spend 1 sorcery point as an action to project an aura of honesty. For the next 10 minutes, creatures within 10 feet of you have disadvantage on {@skill Deception} checks made to lie or conceal the truth. When a creature in this aura fails a {@skill Deception} check, it accidentally blurts out something it didn't mean to while attempting to spin its lie.

entries.name: Otherworldly
entries: You have a way with creatures wildly unlike yourself. Select three of the following creature types: aberration, celestial, dragon, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead. You have advantage on Charisma checks to make a first impression on a creature of one of your chosen types, and if you do not share a common language, you can still communicate some simple concepts to them such as 'friendly', 'help', and 'run'. In addition, by spending 2 Sorcery Points and 1 minute in meditation, you can change your creature type to one of the chosen creature types. This transformation lasts for 1 hour, and cannot be reversed early without repeating the meditation and spending the Sorcery Points again.

classFeature.name: Evolving Manifestation
entries: At 12th level, you gain a deeper understanding of your magics. You may either take the improved from of the manifestation you selected at 2nd level, or you may choose an additional feature from the Manifestation list.

entries.name: Evolving Blizzard
entries: When you cast a levelled spell that deals cold damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point to gain {@dice 1d6} temporary hit points per level of the spell cast. While you have these temporary hit points and a creature hits you with a melee weapon armour attack, the attacker suffers {@dice 1d10} cold harm. These temporary hit points last for 1 hour.

entries.name: Evolving Corrosion
entries: When you cast a levelled spell that deals acid damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point to create a 10-foot radius pool of acid centred on either the target of the spell or the spell's point of origin. The area is difficult terrain, and when a creature ends its turn in the area it suffers {@dice 2d8} acid harm. The pool remains for 1 minute of until you use a bonus action to dismiss it.

entries.name: Evolving Hurricane
entries: When you cast a levelled spell that deals thunder or lightning damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point to create a 15-foot radius storm centred on you, which moves with you. The area of the storm is heavily obscured and counts as difficult terrain, but you can see and move through it normally. At the end of each subsequent turn you can spend an additional sorcery point to maintain it, otherwise it dissipates.

entries.name: Evolving Inferno
entries: When you cast a levelled spell that deals fire damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point to store some of the energy of the spell as a flame you hold in one hand. You can use an action to throw this flame, making a ranged spell armour attack against a target you can see within 30 feet, dealing {@dice 1d8} fire damage per level of the spell cast to trigger this effect. The flame lasts for up to 1 minute or until it is thrown.

entries.name: Evolving Venom
entries: When you cast a spell that deals poison damage, you can spend 1 sorcery point and choose one creature you can see. If the target creature takes poison damage from the spell, it suffers {@dice 2d6} ongoing poison harm for 1 minute or until a creature uses an action to treat the victim (requiring a {@skill Medicine} check against your standard DC).

classFeature.name: Major Metamagic
entries: At 15th level, you gain one major metamagic option. These are more powerful expressions of your magic, though they come at significantly higher costs. You gain an additional major metamagic option at 20th level.

items.name: Consumptive Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that deals damage, you can spend 4 Sorcery Points. You, or a creature within 10 feet of you, regain hit points equal to half the damage roll for the spell. If you roll damage multiple times for a spell, you only restore hit points from the first roll.

items.name: Contingent Spell
entries: When you cast a spell, rather than have it go off immediately, you can spend 6 Sorcery Points and describe an external condition. When that condition occurs before you finish a long rest, you can use your reaction to cast the spell. You can only have one Contingent Spell at a time.

items.name: Echo Spell
entries: When you cast a spell of 5th-level or lower, you can spend Sorcery Points equal to the sorcery point conversion cost for a spell slot of that level (see Table: Creating Spell Slots). The spell repeats on the next turn, originating from the same point and aimed at the same target as the first instance of the spell. Any attacks the repeated spell may make are converted to requiring the target to make a save, where a success counts as a critical failure on the original attack, and a failure counts as a standard success. Additionally, any damage the repeated spell deals is caused as harm instead.

items.name: Intense Spell
entries: Before rolling damage for a spell or seeing the result of any attack rolls for the spell, you can spend Sorcery Points equal to the spell's level. The spell deals its maximum possible damage to one of its targets.

classFeature.name: Additional Major Metamagic
entries: At 20th level, you gain one additional major Metamagic option.

classFeature.name: Arcane Clutch
entries: At 20th level, you can reclaim some fraction of the energies you expend as you cast. Whenever you cast a spell of 3rd-level or higher, you regain Sorcery Points equal to a third of the expended spell slot's level.

classFeature.name: Explosive Magics
entries: Also at 20th level, the magic you bring to bear can be made to build upon itself. When you cast a spell of 4th-level or lower that deals damage, you can spend a number of Sorcery Points equal to half the spell's level (minimum 1) to reroll any dice that have the maximum result and add them to the total. For example, if you cast an exploding fireball and roll three 6s amongst your damage dice, you roll an additional {@dice 3d6}, and continue to reroll any 6s, adding them to the total damage dealt.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain two {@filter Arcane Utilities|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (5)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fifth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: As a student of arcane magic, you have a spellbook containing spells that show the first glimmerings of your true power.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know three cantrips of your choice from the {@filter wizard spell list|spells|level=0|class=wizard (sns)}. You learn additional wizard cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Wizard table.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Wizard table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your wizard spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of wizard spells that are available for you to cast. To do so, choose a number of wizard spells from the {@filter wizard spell list|spells|class=wizard (sns)} equal to your Intelligence modifier + your wizard level (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you're a 3rd-level wizard, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With an Intelligence of 16, your list of prepared spells can include six spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination, chosen from the wizard spell list. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell magic missile}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or a 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.
You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your wizard spells, since you learn your wizard spells through dedicated study and memorization. You use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item arcane focus} as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

classFeature.name: Arcane Recovery
entries: From 1st level, once per day when you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be 6th-level or higher.

classFeature.name: Elective Studies
entries: Your study of magic has uncovered unique ways to explore the world. At 2nd level you gain one {@filter elective study|optionalfeatures|feature type=es} of your choice. The Studies Known column of the Wizard table shows when you learn more elective studies. Unless otherwise noted, you can gain each study only once.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or your can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Signature Spell (1st-level)
entries: At 5th level, you have developed your spellcasting skill and honed it into spells that you consider to be your Signature Spells.

Choose one {@filter 1st-level wizard spell|spells|level=1|class=Wizard (sns)}.
