You always have this spell prepared, it doesn't count toward your maximum prepared spells, and you can cast this spell at its lowest level without expending a spell slot. Once you have cast a signature spell in this way, you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.

At higher levels, you gain additional Signature Spells, each of which you can cast once between rests. At 10th level, you can choose one 2nd-level Signature spell. At 17th level, you can choose one 3rd-level Signature Spell. Each time you complete a long rest, you can replace one of your Signature Spells with another of the same level.

classFeature.name: Signature Spell (2nd-level)
entries: At 10th level, you learn another Signature Spell, which must be a {@filter 2nd-level wizard spell|spells|level=2|class=Wizard (sns)}.

classFeature.name: Signature Spell (3rd-level)
entries: At 17th level, you learn another Signature Spell, which must be a {@filter 3rd-level wizard spell|spells|level=3|class=Wizard (sns)}.

classFeature.name: Spell Study
entries: At 6th level, you learn to hone your magic and spells to new power levels thanks to your dedicated studies. Choose one Spell Study that reflects what you have dedicated your arcane studies to.

entries.name: Arcane Objects
entries: When you touch a magic item, either willingly or when hit by it, you are immediately aware that it is magical. If you hold such an item in your hand, you can cast {@spell identify} on it as an action without spending a spell slot, even if you don't have the spell prepared.

In addition, when you cast {@spell identify} using a spell slot or as a ritual, you also learn if the item is cursed, and the DM will give you a hint as to the curse's effects (which may be cryptic and vague, but must not be a lie).

entries.name: Detective Spell Study
entries: You are adept at sensing magic and its effects. If there are active magical effects near you, such as a spell effect on an object or a spell effect in an area, you automatically sense its presence. You don't know what spell effects there are or where they are, but you can automatically sense the effect of magic in a 20-foot radius around you.

In addition, you have honed your detection spells. Add {@spell detect magic} and {@spell detect thoughts} to your spellbook if they weren't already there. Whenever you cast {@spell detect magic}, the range that you can sense magic is increased to 60 feet. Whenever you cast {@spell detect thoughts}, the range of creatures that you can read thoughts from is increased to 60 feet.

entries.name: Fauna and Flora
entries: Your research into plants, wildlife, and the natural workings of magic grants you superior insights into the magic of living creatures and flora. Whenever you make a non-armour attack against a plant or beast with a spell that you cast, the attack has advantage.

classFeature.name: Wizard's Flair
entries: At 9th level, you have created a flair that you use when casting spells. Choose a flair from the options below. When you finish a long rest, you may replace your flair with another.
Whenever you cast a spell of 1st-level or higher, you can choose to cast it with your flair. You can do so again when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Awe and Wonder
entries: Choose one creature affected by the spell. It must succeed on a save or be {@condition charmed} by you until the end of its next turn. While charmed in this way, it is {@condition unconscious}, has a movement speed of 0, and does nothing but stare blankly at the dazzling effects of your magic. This unconscious effect ends if the target is attacked. Additionally, if the unconscious effect ends, so does the charm.

entries.name: Terror
entries: Choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you when you cast the spell. It must succeed on a save or become {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn. While frightened in this way, it starts its turn by using a free action to move its full speed (without consuming movement) to get away from you in the most direct manner possible, avoiding obvious hazards along the way.

entries.name: Brilliance
entries: Choose one creature within 30 feet that can see you when you cast the spell. It must succeed on a save or be {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Warding
entries: After casting the spell, until the start of your next turn, a creature who attempts to make an armour attack against you must make a save. On a failure, it must choose a new target. The creature can choose to move up to their speed before choosing the attack's new target.

classFeature.name: Arcane Defences
entries: At 14th level, your training has granted you certain protections against magic. Choose one of the following:

entries.name: Mental Discipline
entries: Your mind is sheltered from intrusion. When a will attack is made against you, you can choose to reduce its success tier by 1. You can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Mystic Mantle
entries: Magic layers upon you into a defensive ward that you can extend to even protect your allies. You and creatures within 10 feet of you have 50% resistance to spell damage.

entries.name: Superior Countermagic
entries: When you make an ability check as part of casting {@spell counterspell} or {@spell dispel magic}, you add your proficiency bonus to the roll.

In addition, when you cast one of these spells, treat the spell slot level of the spell as one level higher than the actual slot you spent. The slot you use to cast the spell must still be at least 3rd-level.

classFeature.name: Spell Intensity
entries: At 18th level, you gain new ways to intensify the power of your spells, sometimes at cost to yourself. Choose one of the following features, which can be used to augment your wizard spells of 5th-level or lower. You must choose whether or not to augment the spell when you declare you're casting it, before any rolls are made.

The first time you use this feature to augment a spell, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before finishing a long rest, your current and maximum hit points are both reduced by {@dice 2d6} per level of the spell augmented (cantrips count as 1st-level spells for this purpose). This reduction lasts until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Binding
entries: After you have affected a creature with a spell that has a duration and it is able to end the spell's effect on itself with a passive save, it must succeed on that passive save twice to end the effect.

entries.name: Burn
entries: Any creature that takes damage from the spell takes that damage again at the start of your next turn, unless a {@spell dispel magic} spell of any level is cast on it before then or it's in an {@spell antimagic field} at the time.

entries.name: Chain
entries: When you cast a spell that can normally only target a single creature, it can also target up to 2 more creatures within 15 feet of the initial target.

entries.name: Duality
entries: When you cast a spell that requires concentration while already concentrating on another spell, you can maintain concentration on both spells simultaneously. If you lose concentration on one spell, you lose concentration on both.

entries.name: Overchannel
entries: When you would roll damage for a spell, you instead deal maximum damage with that spell.

classFeature.name: Swift Signature
entries: At 19th level, you're especially efficient with your Signature Spells. When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action using your Signature Spell feature, you can cast it as a bonus action instead.

classFeature.name: Archmage
entries: At 20th level, you have achieved a rare level of achievement and prestige, gaining the following features.

entries.name: Magesight
entries: Your eyes become attuned to the supernatural. You cannot be {@condition blinded}, and you can see normally in darkness, both magical and non-magical, to a distance of 120 feet.
When you can see a creature cast a spell you instinctively know what spell is being cast before the casting is complete. This does not tell you what level spell slot is being used to cast the spell.

entries.name: True Magician
entries: When you have a 1st-level wizard spell prepared, you are able to cast it as a 1st-level spell without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 1st level you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

entries.name: Archery
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

entries.name: Battle Ready
entries: You may activate up to 2 combat stances you know without expending any exertion points using the same bonus action. Once you activate a set of combat stances in this way you can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Defence
entries: While you are wearing body armour, you gain a +1 bonus to your armour.

entries.name: Dueling
entries: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

entries.name: Great Weapon Fighting
entries: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must have the two-handed trait for you to gain this benefit.

entries.name: Mounted Combatant
entries: While mounted, you gain an expertise die on armour attack rolls against creatures that are Medium-sized or smaller and not mounted.

entries.name: Protection
entries: When a creature you can see makes an armour attack against a target other than you that is adjacent to you, if you are wielding a shield you can use your reaction to hinder the attack, forcing the creature to roll with disadvantage.

entries.name: Technical Fighting
entries: Whenever you use a combat maneuver any attacks it triggers have a +1 bonus to their attack rolls and deal 2 additional damage.

entries.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
entries: Whenever you would make an attack using a weapon with the Thrown trait you may draw the weapon as part of the attack. In addition, your ranged weapon attacks with Thrown weapons deal an additional 2 damage.

entries.name: Tireless Fighting
entries: Whenever you expend hit dice to recover exertion points, you recover 3 exertion points per hit die expended (instead of 1d4 exertion points as normal).

entries.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
entries: You add your choice of either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage of any weapon you are wielding in your offhand.

entries.name: Unarmed Fighting
entries: Your damage with brawling weapons is increased by 2. If you start your turn grappling a creature, you may cause bludgeoning harm equal to your Strength modifier to any creature you are grappling.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 1st level, you gain the ability to use {@filter combat maneuvers|maneuvers|degree=1;2;3;4;5}. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions of your choice. You learn three maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Maneuvers Known column of the Fighter table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Soldiering Knacks
entries: Over the course of your training and adventures you have picked up a variety of skills and tricks to help you make your way. At 1st level you gain a {@filter soldiering knack|optionalfeatures|feature type=sk} of your choice. The Knacks Known column of the Fighter table shows when you learn more fighter soldering knacks.

classFeature.name: Steely Mien
entries: At 2nd level your combat experience becomes obvious on some subtle level, influencing how people perceive you, or perhaps how you see them. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Closed Helm
entries: You have great control over your emotions; your face is an unmoving mask, revealing nothing of what you're thinking. Creatures have disadvantage on {@skill Insight} checks made against you. This grants you advantage with the Gossip journey activity when using {@skill Deception}, but disadvantage with the Pray journey activity when using {@skill Persuasion}. In addition, you subtract {@dice 1d4} from the attack rolls of attacks that can {@condition charmed|charm} or {@condition frightened|frighten} you.

entries.name: Heroic Flair
entries: Your victories have emboldened you and you radiate with heroic confidence. You have advantage on {@skill Persuasion} checks made to influence friendly creatures with a CR lower than your level. This does not affect the Pray journey activity.

entries.name: Watchful Eye
entries: You are enternally alert and wary, ready for trouble. You have advantage on {@skill Insight} checks made to determine hostile intent or predict violence (which does not affect the Gossip journey activity), and gain an expertise die in {@skill Perception}.

classFeature.name: Maneuver Specialisation (1)
entries: As you experience combat and perfect your techniques they get easier for you to perform and more difficult for your foes to overcome. At 3rd level, and again at 7th, 11th, and 15th level you may choose to master a combat maneuver you know. You cannot master the same maneuver more than once in this way.
The exertion point cost of mastered maneuvers is reduced by 1 (minimum 1).
You gain a +1 bonus to the attack rolls of mastered maneuvers.
Any attack you make as part of a mastered maneuver (including triggering attacks) deals 2 additional damage.

classFeature.name: Maneuver Specialisation (2)
entries: At 7th level you may choose to master another combat maneuver you know, as per your Maneuver Specialisation feature.

classFeature.name: Maneuver Specialisation (3)
entries: At 11th level you may choose to master another combat maneuver you know, as per your Maneuver Specialisation feature.

classFeature.name: Maneuver Specialisation (4)
entries: At 15th level you may choose to master another combat maneuver you know, as per your Maneuver Specialisation feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Reserves (1)
entries: At 4th level your stamina increases, allowing you to engage foes longer without tiring. Your exertion pool increases by 1. At 8th, 12th, and 16th level your exertion pool increases by an additional point.

classFeature.name: Reserves (2)
entries: At 8th level your exertion pool further increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Reserves (3)
entries: At 12th level your exertion pool further increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Reserves (4)
entries: At 16th level your exertion pool further increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (1)
entries: From 5th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Empowered Attack (2)
entries: From 11th level, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is tripled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 3d6} + triple your Strength modifier instead.

classFeature.name: Martial Lore
entries: At 6th level, your knowledge of martial lore deepens. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Military Traditions
entries: You've familiarised yourself with wars long past and military protocols of the present. You gain an expertise die on Intelligence checks made to recall lore or gather information regarding warfare or military action. In addition, you gain an expertise die on Charisma checks made to influence soldiers, guards, or other organised martial forces. This does not affect any journey activities.

entries.name: Size Up
entries: Your combat experience against a wide variety of foes has given you a good eye for evaluating threats. You can use a bonus action to determine the CR of a creature you can see within 200 feet, and that creature's standard DC.

entries.name: Weapon Lore
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intelligence checks made to recall lore or gather information about weapons, armour, and other martial equipment. This does not affect any journey activities. In addition, you can use an action to investigate the magical properties of such an item by handling it, such as swinging a sword or hefting a shield. You learn its properties, how to use them, whether it requires attunement, and how many charges it has. You also learn if the item has any sentience or bears a curse, though you only learn if it is sentient or cursed (not the nature of that sentience or curse).

classFeature.name: Indomitable (1)
entries: At 9th level, you can draw upon your indomitable will to overcome pain, resist overwhelming forces, or retaliate when most warriors would be shirking back. You can use this feature and your reaction to perform any of the following:

items: Force a non-armour attack against you to be rerolled. If you do so, you must use the new roll.
Reduce the damage you would take from a single instance by {@dice 2d8} + your level.
Make a single weapon attack against a creature that has just completed an attack against you.

entries: You gain an additional use of this feature at 13th level and a third use starting at 17th level. You regain all spent uses of this feature whenever you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Indomitable (2)
entries: At 13th level, you gain an additional use of your Indomitable feature.

classFeature.name: Indomitable (3)
entries: At 17th level, you gain an additional use of your Indomitable feature.

classFeature.name: Reputation
entries: At 10th level, your reputation has begun to precede you. Choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Inspiring
entries: You're a reputed leader, a person to be trusted whose words are to be heeded. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Persuasion} checks. In addition, you may spend 1 exertion point as a bonus action to inspire a creature that can hear you. The creature makes an {@skill Insight} check opposed by your {@skill Persuasion} check, becoming inspired by you on a failure. Indifferent creatures you inspire this way become friendly towards you and your allies, and friendly creatures you inspire in this way will consider you an ally and may provide you with local information that would otherwise remain secret. Creatures will remain inspired by you indefinitely unless you do something that would make them hostile or lose confidence in you, such as lying, stealing, or causing them or their allies harm, or being proven cowardly or untrustworthy in any way.

entries.name: Intimidating
entries: You're reputed to be a terror to your foes and a fearsome ally. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks. In addition, you can use a bonus action and spend 1 expertion point to intimidate one creature you can see. Make a will attack against the creature with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is {@condition sluggish} when moving towards you for 1 round.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition frightened} for 1 minute, or until it succeeds on a passive save.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also {@condition debilitated}.

entries.name: Legendary
entries: Your deeds have become epic stories, growing bigger and more outlandish with each retelling. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Deception} checks. In addition, you can spend 1 exertion point to weave your real legendary deeds into a {@skill Persuasion} check as boasting, or include fabricated legendary deeds into a {@skill Deception} check. When you do so, you have advantage on the check. This affects your journey activity checks.

classFeature.name: War's Toll
entries: At 14th level, your experiences have left their impressions on you. All the lives ended - both friends and foes - inevitably weighs down on a person, and each must find their own way to cope and pay the toll of war. Choose one of the following.

entries.name: Battle Scarred
entries: You've embraced every victory and loss, and you wear each battle scar like a badge of honour. Whenever you are reduced to 0 hit points you gain a new permanent scar or mark, and you gain Inspiration. Once you gain Inspiration in this way you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Carouser
entries: You cope through drink and revelry, and have mastered both. You can gain the benefits of a long rest by spending 4 hours drinking, carousing, and taking the occasional blackout nap. You can carouse in this way so long as you consume at least 4 pints worth of ale (or equivalent drink), and you are alert and aware of your surroundings while doing so. If you carouse with another creature in this way, you can attempt to learn secrets from it by drinking it under the table. Each hour you spend carousing with a willing creature, make an opposed {@dice d20} roll, adding your fortitudes. On a success, that creature answers a question truthfully (even questions it would not normally answer).
In addition, you can enjoy alcohol as normal but can't gain the {@condition poisoned} condition from it.
This feature has no effect on creatures that are immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition.

entries.name: One Eye Open
entries: Your life has been so regularly in danger that you've learned to literally sleep with one eye open. You are alert and aware of your surroundings while sleeping during long rests. You can also never be surprised and you gain an expertise die on initiative rolls.

classFeature.name: Martial Legacy
entries: At 18th level, your mastered maneuvers have become as natural as breathing, and you have the opportunity to pass your techniques down to the next generation. By spending 8 hours sparring, you can teach another willing creature a maneuver you have mastered using Maneuver Specialisation. A creature must be proficient in at least one combat tradition to learn a maneuver this way, it can only learn maneuvers of a degree from which it already knows at least one maneuver, and this counts against its number of maneuvers known.

classFeature.name: Warmaster
entries: At 20th level, you have become an undisputed master of battle. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Master of Combat
entries: You master every maneuver you know and any future maneuvers you learn as per Maneuver Specialisation. Additionally, choose one manveuver of 3rd degree or less that you know. You are able to use the chosen maneuver without expending any exertion points.

entries.name: Finishing Blow
entries: You can make spectacular, devastating attacks which inspire awe in those nearby. When you fail to hit with a weapon attack while taking the Attack action, you can choose to score a standard success instead. Alternatively, you can choose to change a standard or major success with a weapon attack into a critical success, or when you score a critical success you can choose to double your critical damage bonus.
If the Finishing Blow's original outcome was a major success, all hostile creatures who see the Finishing Blow become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Skill Tricks
entries: Your focused training allows you to use your skills in ways that others can't. At 1st level you gain a skill trick of your choice. Your tricks are detailed in the {@filter Optional Features|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=ST} page. The Tricks Known column of the Rogue table shows when you learn more skill tricks. Unless otherwise noted, you can gain each trick only once.

classFeature.name: Sneak Attack
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you fight with precision, taking advantage of momentary lapses in an opponent's concentration. Your attacks with Dexterity-governed or ranged weapons can deal additional base damage. The amount of extra damage is shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue table.

In order to apply this damage, you must not have disadvantage on the attack and your target must be adjacent to another enemy of the target who is not {@condition incapacitated}. Alternatively, you can apply sneak attack damage if you have advantage on the attack.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Biting Zephyr, Mist and Shade, or Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr;mist and shade;rapid current}. You learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Maneuvers Known column of the Rogue table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Combat Tactic
entries: At 2nd level, you adopt a set of moves and tactics which complement your preferred combat strategies. Choose one of the following options. Whenever you complete a long rest, you may change this choice.

entries.name: Ambusher
entries: When you begin your turn hidden from a creature, you gain advantage on melee attacks against it until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Carver
entries: When you deal sneak attack damage with a knife weapon, your attack deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage. At 11th level, this additional damage becomes {@dice 2d6} damage.

entries.name: Sniper
entries: Once per turn, when you make an attack with a ranged weapon you can choose to add your proficiency bonus to the damage roll.

classFeature.name: Cunning Action
entries: Also at 2nd level, you use your mobility to weave through the battlefield. Each turn in combat, you can use a bonus action to take the Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Innocent Facade
entries: Beginning at 4th level, you are skilled at hiding your motives. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill to gain an expertise die from Innocent Facade.

entries.name: Cipher
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. You can invent ciphers, and are able to teach a cipher to others. Anyone who knows a cipher can encode and read hidden messages made with it; the apparent text must be at least four times longer than the hidden message. Other creatures can spend a minute to make an Investigation check (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) to detect the presence of a cipher, and are able to read the hidden message if they substantially succeed.
Additionally, you have advantage on Investigation checks made to detect and decode ciphers and codes.

entries.name: Distraction
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. You can use an action to make a Persuasion check, opposed by the Insight check of creatures of your choice that can sense you. On a failure, until the end of your next turn a creature has disadvantage on Perception checks until it can no longer sense you.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with either Finesse or Insight.

entries.name: Veiled Threat
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. When you succeed on a verbal Intimidation check, creatures of your choice other than the target of your check don't recognize your hostile intent.

classFeature.name: Aim
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you learn to briefly focus and make the most out of your next strike. If you haven't yet moved on your turn, you can use your Cunning Action to Aim, gaining advantage on the next armour attack roll you make before the start of your next turn. If you do so, your Speed is reduced to 0 until the end of your turn.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation
entries: At 6th level, your honed reflexes and mental toughness allow you to avoid danger. Choose two of the following options. You may choose an additional option at 12th level and two more at 18th level.

entries.name: Blindsense
entries: You can use Cunning Action to reach out with your senses. Until the end of your next turn, you gain 10 feet of blindsight, so long as you are able to hear.

entries.name: Evasion
entries: Reflex attacks made against you are reduced by 1 success tier.

entries.name: Slippery Mind
entries: You gain +2 will.

entries.name: Street Tough
entries: You gain +2 fortitude.

entries.name: Tumbling Retreat
entries: When a damaging melee armour attack fails to hit you, you can use your reaction to shift up to half your speed.

entries.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: When an attacker that you can see makes an armour attack against you, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's success tier by 1.

classFeature.name: Improved Critical
entries: At 7th level, your weapon armour attacks have +1 critical success range.

classFeature.name: Shrewd Judgment
entries: Beginning at 8th level, your ability to read others makes you as dangerous in conversation as you are in a dark alley. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Shrewd Judgment.

entries.name: Quick Frisk
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. You can touch a target to learn what they are carrying; make a Finesse check opposed by the target's Perception. On a success, you learn what types of objects they are carrying. If they are carrying coins, you know the approximate number. For the next 24 hours, any Finesse checks you make against the target have advantage. On a failure, you can't use Quick Frisk against that target for 24 hours.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with either Intimidation or Persuasion.

entries.name: Spot Tell
entries: You gain an expertise die on Insight checks. Whenever a creature makes a Deception check opposed by your Insight check and you win the opposed check, you also learn to recognize signs that the target is bluffing. For the next 24 hours, you have advantage on Insight checks made against the target.

entries.name: Tricky Interrogation
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. When you ask a yes or no question to a creature you can see, you can make a Deception check opposed by the target's Deception check. On a success, you learn whether the target believes the answer to be yes or no, even if they didn't reply. On a failure, you can't use Tricky Interrogation against that target for 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Low Profile
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can avoid notice when you wish. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Low Profile.

entries.name: Costumer
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks made when using a disguise kit. When you use a disguise kit on yourself or another person, creatures have disadvantage on checks made to see through the disguise.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Finesse, Insight, or Intimidation.

entries.name: True Lie
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. Your lies are so convincing that you half believe them yourself. You can use Persuasion instead of Deception when you are telling a lie.

entries.name: Walk it Back
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. After your party makes a group Deception check to tell a lie, you can use your reaction to add a supporting detail. Make an individual Deception check and replace the party's check with your own.

classFeature.name: Cunning Celerity
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you can take two different bonus actions on your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all uses of this feature when you complete a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Expert Talent
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you almost never make mistakes when performing routine tasks. Whenever you make an ability check on which you roll expertise dice, you treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation (2)
entries: Also at 12th level, you can choose another option from your Self-Preservation feature.

classFeature.name: Guarded Aspect
entries: Beginning at 14th level, you are adept at uncovering and preserving secrets. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Guarded Aspect.

entries.name: Hidden Signs
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. You can use body language and hidden hand signals to communicate a short message to some observers while hiding it from others. Make a Finesse check opposed by the Insight checks of observers from whom you want to hide your communication. On a success, a creature is not aware that you sent a message. On a failure, the creature is aware that you signaled but doesn't know the content of your message. If you substantially fail, the creature learns your message.

entries.name: Master of Disguise
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks made when using a disguise kit. As long as you are wearing a disguise, your true identity can't be determined even if your disguise fails. Additionally, you can don or doff a disguise as an action.

entries.name: Seen it All
entries: You gain an expertise die on Insight checks.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion.

classFeature.name: Elusive
entries: At 15th level, you know how to avoid vulnerable positions. As long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, no armour attack roll has advantage against you.

classFeature.name: Overpowering Personality
entries: Beginning at 18th level, your poise and charm allow you to impose your vision on the world. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Overpowering Personality.

entries.name: Dress Rehearsal
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. You can spend 10 minutes practicing a skill of your choice, at the end of which you make a Persuasion check. You can use this result to replace the die roll of one ability check you make using that skill in the next hour. You may decide to use this feature after seeing the die roll. Once you have used this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Psych Out
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. As an action, you can make an Intimidation check opposed by the Charisma check of a creature that can see you. On a success, the next armour attack roll or opposed ability check you make against that creature within the next 10 minutes is made with advantage. Once you've failed an opposed Intimidation check, you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Roguish Charm
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. After you spend a minute in conversation with a creature that is not hostile, you may make a Persuasion check opposed by its Insight. On a success, the creature is nonmagically {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour. The charm ends early if you or your allies hurt the creature. Once you've used this feature successfully, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Deception, Finesse, or Insight.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation (4)
entries: Also at 18th level, you can choose another two options from your Self-Preservation feature.

classFeature.name: Grand Larcenist
entries: At 20th level, you reach the epitome of roguish talent and expertise. You gain the following abilities.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: You master the art of distraction. When a creature within your line of sight looks in your direction, you can use your reaction to hide even if you have nothing to hide behind as long as you remain in the same space without moving or taking actions. Until you move or take an action or a reaction, you are invisible to that creature. If you are targeted by a reflex attack, you may choose to increase its success tier by 1 to remain invisible to the creature, otherwise it reveals you.

entries.name: Skill Mastery
entries: You are unparalleled in your chosen fields. For each skill in which you have an expertise die, you gain another expertise die in that skill.

entries.name: Unstoppable
entries: No portal - be it door, window, or planar device - is closed to you. You no longer need to make ability checks to pick locks, and you succeed automatically whenever you attempt to unlock any lock of any kind.

classFeature.name: Art Speciality
entries: No bard is complete without their instrument. Be it a flute, a lute, or even your own voice, starting at 1st level you learn how to make art your greatest weapon for influencing the battlefield. For the purposes of spellcasting, any musical instrument that you have proficiency with counts as a spell focus. Although spells can be cast without these instruments, only targets that are able to perceive your performance can benefit from this feature. Whenever you finish a short or long rest, choose one of the following to be your Art Specialty.

items: Percussion (Casabas, Castanets, Drums, Maracas): Any instrument played by being struck or scraped (either by other instruments or against each other) qualifies as a percussion instrument. When using this musical art as a spell focus, you double the ranges of {@filter bard spells from the sound school|maneuvers|Tradition=sound|class=Bard (sns)}. A sound spell that has a range of Touch increases its range to 30 feet.

String Instruments (Dulcimer, Harp, Lute, Lyre, Violin): String instruments are played by plucking and twanging strings. When using this musical art as a spell focus, whenever you cast a bard spell from the {@filter movement or teleportation|maneuvers|Tradition=movement;teleportation|class=Bard (sns)} school you can target an additional creature within 15 feet of you.

Visual (Calligraphy, Dance, Light Manipulation, Shapes): There are many ways to undertake a visual performance whether through illustration, illusion magic, or legerdemain. When using this art as a spell focus, whenever you cast a bard spell you may choose to make an ally able to see you the point of origin of that spell. You must be able to see any targets of the spell to do this.

Voice (Humming, Insults, Oration, Singing): Whenever you would lose concentration on a bard spell while using this musical art as a spell focus, including when reduced to 0 hit points, make a save. On a success, you do not lose concentration on that spell.

Wind Instruments (Bagpipes, Flute, Horn, Ocarina, Trombone): Any instrument played by passing air through it qualifies as a wind instrument. When using this musical art as a spell focus, whenever you cast a bard spell you can make a {@skill Deception} or {@skill Persuasion} check. Any observers with a passive {@skill Insight} score equal to or less than the result of your check do not see or hear you cast the spell. Once you have used this feature three times, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration
entries: You have a preternatural ability to inspire those around you. You can use a bonus action to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear or see you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a {@dice d6}.

Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or save it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the {@dice d20} before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.
At 5th level your Bardic Inspiration die increases to {@dice d8}, at 10th level it increases to {@dice d10}, and at 15th level it increases to {@dice d12}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities
entries: Also at 1st level, you learn certain capabilities based on your magical knowledge. You gain one {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au} now, and gain another at 5th, 11th, and 17th levels. You can only gain each utility once.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (2)
entries: At 5th level, you gain a second {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (3)
entries: At 11th level, you gain a third {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Utilities (4)
entries: At 17th level, you gain a fourth {@filter Arcane Utility|optionalfeatures|feature type=au}.

classFeature.name: Battle Hymn
entries: Like music, battle can be wild and chaotic, and through the sensible use of formula and talent order can be brought with a dedicated change of tune. At 1st level, you learn a battle hymn. It can be expressed in any way you like — a special leitmotif in your music, a change in pitch, or even a more advanced version of your favorite art.

Once at the start of your turn, you can activate a battle hymn by expending a use of Bardic Inspiration (no action is required). Performing a battle hymn requires your concentration, as though you were casting a spell. Once activated, a battle hymn continues until you lose concentration or the start of your next turn.
The number of battle hymns you know are listed in the Battle Hymns Known column of the Bard table. Whenever you gain a bard level, you may swap one battle hymn you know for a new battle hymn.

entries.name: Audience
entries: Unless stated otherwise a battle hymn does not have a target until you choose one, and each targets a single creature within 30 feet. You may choose to target a creature at any point, but after targeting a battle hymn its target cannot be changed.

entries.name: Sustained Song
entries: When you have an activated battle hymn but have not chosen a creature to benefit from it yet, at the start of your turn when it would end you can instead choose to sustain the battle hymn until the start of your next turn without expending a use of Bardic Inspiration.

items.name: Harmony of Pain
entries: The creature gains a number of temporary hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration plus your proficiency bonus.

items.name: Overbearing Rhythm
entries: An attacker targeting the creature with an armour attack has disadvantage on its attack roll.

items.name: Song of Clarity
entries: At the start of the creature's turn, if it is not at 0 hit points the creature may expend and roll one of its hit dice to regain hit points equal to the roll plus your proficiency bonus.

items.name: Bastions of Justice
entries: The creature gains a bonus to saves equal to half your Charisma modifier (minimum 0).

items.name: Call to Arms
entries: The creature can roll one additional weapon damage die on its weapon attacks.

items.name: Placid Tones
entries: The creature gains an expertise die on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma checks.

items.name: Heaven's Blessing
entries: When the creature benefits from your Bardic Inspiration, it rolls the Bardic Inspiration die twice and uses the higher result.

items.name: Ode of Blood
entries: If the creature is dying, it continues making death saving throws but stops being {@condition incapacitated} for up to 3 rounds. The creature becomes {@condition incapacitated} if it takes any damage, and it dies if it fails its last death saving throw. Once a creature has benefited from this battle hymn, it can't benefit from it again until it finishes a short or long rest.

items.name: Willful Serenade
entries: Until the end of the combat, the creature ignores up to 4 levels of {@condition strife} it is suffering from (how much to ignore is determined by the creature).

items.name: Doomsayer
entries: The creature takes a -{@dice 1d8} penalty on armour attack rolls.

items.name: Onward to Battle
entries: The creature gains a 40 foot increase to Speed.

items.name: Rise and Fight
entries: A dying creature regains hit points equal to your Bardic Inspiration die plus your proficiency bonus. Once a creature has benefited from this battle hymn, it can't benefit from it again until it finishes a long rest.

items.name: Bring Low the Foe
entries: The creature takes a -{@dice 1d4} penalty on saves. In addition, it is {@condition rattled} for {@dice 1d4} rounds after the battle hymn ends.

items.name: Carry the Spirit
entries: The creature gains advantage on saves.

items.name: Inspiring Crescendo
entries: The creature gains a {@dice d6} expertise die on ability checks.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have developed a talent for creating magic through artistic expression, whether that be a melody made enchanted or a dance that unlocks power from beyond the mortal realms.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter bard spell list|spells|level=0|class=bard (sns)}. You learn additional bard cantrips of your choice at higher levels, learning a 3rd cantrip at 4th level and a 4th at 10th level.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Bard table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your {@filter bard spells|spells|class=bard (sns)} of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.
For example, if you know the 1st-level spell {@spell cure wounds} and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast {@spell cure wounds} using either slot.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher
entries: You know four 1st-level spells of your choice from the bard spell list.

You learn an additional bard spell of your choice at each level. Each of these spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For instance, when you reach 3rd level in this class, you can learn one new spell of 1st or 2nd level.

Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose any of the bard spells you know and replace them with the same number of spells from the bard spell list, which also must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your bard spells. Your magic comes from the heart and soul you pour into the performance of your music or oration. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item musical instrument} as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

classFeature.name: Adventuring Tricks
entries: Even when abroad you are most at home when surrounded by those who love you — or at least tolerate you. At 2nd level, you learn one adventuring trick of your choice. Adventuring tricks are detailed in the {@filter Optional Features|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=AT} page. The Tricks Known column of the Bard table shows when you learn more adventuring tricks.

classFeature.name: Jack-of-All-Trades
entries: Starting at 2nd Level, whenever you make an ability check with a skill or tool you are not proficient with, you add half your proficiency bonus.

classFeature.name: Varied Expertise
entries: Also at 2nd level, choose one skill or tool you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill or tool. At 6th level, and again at 10th, 14th, and 18th level, choose an additional skill or tool.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Additionally, you gain a feat.

classFeature.name: Bardic Legend (1st)
entries: No one is a true artist until others appreciate their work. Through the course of your career as a bard your talent can attract to your doorstep both the most curious of fans and also the vilest of enemies.

At 4th level you write a bardic tale of your adventures. Your bardic legend takes whatever form you like. When you enter a settlement and spend a day playing or recounting the tale, the reputation of you and your allies starts to grow. In addition to receiving local quests suited to the exploits detailed in your bardic legend, when you rest in a settlement that knows your bardic legend you and your allies treat that location as a super-haven. In addition, you gain an expertise die on {@skill History} checks made in the settlement.

classFeature.name: Battle Hymn Focus
entries: Also at 4th level, as soon as battle commences your mind focuses on inspiring your allies. At the end of another creature's turn, you can use your reaction to activate a battle hymn. This battle hymn replaces any currently activated battle hymns. Replacing an activated battle hymn does not require expending a use of Bardic Inspiration if it has no target yet.
In addition, choose one of the following.

items: Your battle hymns no longer require concentration.
Your battle hymns can affect a number of additional creatures equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

You learn an additional battle hymn that does not count against the number of battle hymns you can know. When choosing this battle hymn, you are treated as having 4 more bard levels for the purpose of meeting its prerequisites. If you change this Battle Hymn Focus, you forget the additional battle hymn.

entries: Whenever you complete a long rest, you may change your Battle Hymn Focus.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
entries: At 5th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a {@dice d8}.

classFeature.name: Prestigious
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you cannot help but draw attention and spread your reputation. Your prestige in all factions familiar with you increases such that they generally consider you friendly at a base level. When you reach 11th level, these settlements instead generally revere you at a base level.

classFeature.name: Varied Expertise
entries: At 6th level, choose an additional skill or tool.

classFeature.name: Bardic Legend (2nd)
entries: Starting at 7th level, you craft a second bardic legend. Commoners are either more amenable or afraid of you depending on the type of bardic legend you create. Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Persuasion}. You gain advantage on checks made using the chosen skill against any commoner that has heard your bardic legend.

classFeature.name: Maestro
entries: At 7th level you become so perfect in your craft that your style begins to bleed over into all your other workings. Choose one type of Art Speciality. You always gain the benefits of that Art Speciality no matter what instrument or art you are using.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Battle Hymn Specialization
entries: At 8th level, the energy and focus you bring to battle make your presence in combat a crucial factor for victory. The range of your battle hymns increases to 60 feet.
In addition, choose one of the following.

items: You can expend 2 uses of Bardic Inspiration to activate two Battle Hymns at the same time. Losing concentration or changing battle hymns deactivates both.

Pick three spells of 4th-level or lower that have only vocalized components. These spells may be from any class spell list. You may spend 2 uses of Bardic Inspiration to cast one of the chosen spells at the start of your turn with no action required (instead of activating a battle hymn).
When you expend a use of Bardic Inspiration to activate a battle hymn, you may also grant a Bardic Inspiration die to a creature within 60 feet.

entries: Whenever you complete a long rest, you may change your Battle Hymn Specialization.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
entries: At 10th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a {@dice d10}.

classFeature.name: Varied Expertise
entries: At 10th level, choose an additional skill or tool.

classFeature.name: Art Mastery
entries: At 11th level, the use of your art specialty has transcended into something beyond majestic. You gain one of the following art masteries.

items: Percussion (Rolling Rhythm): You can throw a spell's magic, rebounding it from one place to another. When using this musical art as a spell focus, if you cast a spell that targets only one creature, you can target an additional creature within range of the first (as if the first was casting the spell). Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, you can't do so again until you finish a short rest.

String Instruments (Melody for All): The melodies you pluck spread like gusts of wind. You gain a number of melody charges equal to your Charisma modifier, which you regain whenever you finish a long rest. When using this musical art as a spell focus, if you cast a spell that targets only one creature you can spend a melody charge to target an additional creature within the spell's range.

Visual (Dual Masterpiece): Your magic resonates through the air. When using this musical art as a spell focus, after casting a bard spell that used an ally as its point of origin on your last turn, if you cast the same spell normally its spell level increases by 2. Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Voice (Everlasting Song): The spells you cast resound upon themselves. When concentrating on a bard spell, you automatically succeed on saves made to maintain concentration while you are using this musical art as a spell focus.
Wind Instruments (Perfect Pitch): While using this musical art as a spell focus, you gain advantage on {@skill Deception} and {@skill Finesse} checks to conceal the casting of bard spells.

classFeature.name: Bardic Legend (3rd)
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you craft a third bardic legend that is able to spawn other tales. Whenever you enter a settlement, you can name a new bardic legend of your choice. Allies that are part of the tale gain Inspiration when they finish a long rest in that settlement. Any other creatures that are part of that tale have their reputations ruined — either they confront you directly to stop the rumor, or they are unable to hide or deal in that town for the length of time you are in it. Once you have used this feature in a settlement, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Universal Trick
entries: Whether from watching your fellow adventurers work, from tales of your predecessors, or simply because you are just that talented, you have taken to adopting the techniques of your peers.

At 13th level, and again at 16th and 19th level, you learn a {@filter developed talent|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=DT} from the berserker class, a {@filter sign of faith|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=SF} from the cleric class, a {@filter soldiering knack|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=SK} from the fighter class, or an {@filter elective study|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=ES} from the wizard class. You may only learn one Universal Trick from any individual class.

classFeature.name: Varied Expertise
entries: At 14th level, choose an additional skill or tool.

classFeature.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
entries: At 15th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a {@dice d12}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Universal Trick
entries: At 16th level, gain an additional universal trick.

classFeature.name: Varied Expertise
entries: At 18th level, choose an additional skill or tool.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Universal Trick
entries: At 19th level, gain an additional universal trick.

classFeature.name: Virtuoso
entries: At 20th level, your capacity for drawing a crowd reaches dizzying new heights. As an action, you can expend a use of Bardic Inspiration to start an epic performance that lasts for {@dice 1d4} rounds. During that time, you can choose up to 50 creatures that are within 120 feet of you. Each creature able to hear or see your Epic Performance makes a save. If a creature fails its save, it is {@condition charmed} by you for the duration plus 6 days, or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. A {@condition charmed} creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance.

The performance ends early if you become {@condition incapacitated} or silenced, or if you voluntarily end it (no action required). At the end of the duration, you can expend an additional use of Bardic Inspiration to extend the duration by {@dice 1d4} rounds.

When your Epic Performance lasts at least 1 minute, at the end of the duration you can target each {@condition charmed} creature as if using the {@spell mass suggestion} spell cast at 9th-level. Instead of making an attack roll as part of this casting, always use the standard success outcome. The commands you give a {@condition charmed} creature are not obvious, and other creatures must make an {@skill Insight} check opposed by your {@skill Deception} check to understand that you are doing anything more than giving a performance. You can give each creature its own set of commands, but can only give out 6 different sets of commands.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Crack Shot
entries: At tier 1, your training with ranged weapons grants the following benefits:

items: You do not have disadvantage on ranged weapon attack rolls due to being adjacent to a hostile creature or attacking at long range.
Your ranged weapon attack rolls ignore half cover and three-quarters covers.

entries.name: Sharpshooting Traditions
entries: Also at tier 1, you gain proficiency in one of the following combat traditions of your choice: {@filter Biting Zephyr|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr}, {@filter Razor's Edge|maneuvers|Tradition=razor's edge}, {@filter Unending Wheel|maneuvers|Tradition=unending wheel}.

entries.name: Sharpshooter Major Effect #1
entries: For 1 round, attacks against the target have +2 critical hit range. If the target dies from this attack, your attacks have +2 critical hit range for the rest of the combat, or until you land a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Focus Shot
entries: At tier 3, you've become precise enough that when given enough time to focus you can not only strike your target, but hit specific parts of your target to great effect. Once per turn, whenever you take the Attack action and make a ranged attack, you may forgo one instance of your Empowered Attack feature to make the attack a Focus Shot. When you make a Focus Shot choose one of the following options. You gain an expertise die on the attack, and on a hit it does your chosen effect in addition to the attack's damage.

Creatures wounded by any of these effects can end the effect upon passing a passive save. Alternatively, the wounded creature (or a creature adjacent to it) can use an action to make a {@skill Medicine} check against your standard DC, ending the effect on a success. Receiving any healing also ends the effect. You cannot choose effects that specifically target features a creature does not have.

items: Aim for the Eyes: You aim for the creature's weak and delicate eyes. If the Focus Shot scores a major success, the target is {@condition blinded} until it recovers.
Bring it Down: You aim for weak points on the creature's legs. If the Focus Shot scores a major success, its Speed is reduced by half until it recovers.
Center of Mass: You aim for the heart or its horrid equivalent. Your Focus Shot's critical success range increases by 1.

Vein Slice: You aim for a vital vein or artery to bleed your target out. If the Focus Shot scores a major success, the target suffers {@dice 1d6} ongoing harm of your weapon's base type until it recovers.

Wing Clip: You aim for your target's wings. If the Focus Shot scores a major success, the target's flying speed is reduced by half until it recovers. Creatures who are currently flying when this effect begins immediately plummet up to 60 feet down and take falling damage as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Sharpshooter Major Effect #2
entries: For 1 round, if you make a ranged weapon attack as part of a maneuver you perform (e.g. Missile Volley, Horizon Shot), or use a bonus action to perform a maneuver that impacts your next ranged weapon attack (e.g. Doubleshot, Blindshot), it has no Exertion cost.

subclassFeature.name: Hawkeye
entries: At tier 2, you have become incredibly observant and alert. You gain an expertise die on {@skill Investigation} and {@skill Perception} checks, and on initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Sniper
entries: Also from tier 2, your threshold for landing a major success with ranged weapon attacks is reduced by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Reflexive Shot
entries: At tier 4, your accurate reflexes allow you to threaten shots at a moment's notice. You may make opportunity attacks using ranged weapons, acting as if your reach were 5 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Critical Shot
entries: At tier 5, you can hit miniscule targets at ludicrous ranges with seemingly impossible accuracy. Whenever you take the Attack action, you may forgo all instances of your Empowered Attack feature to turn your attack into a Critical Shot, making the attack with advantage. If your Critical Shot hits it becomes a critical success.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Prey
entries: At tier 1, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Colossus Slayer
entries: Your tenacity can wear down the most potent foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra 1d8 damage if it’s below its hit point maximum. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

items.name: Giant Killer
entries: When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you hits or misses you with an attack, you can use your reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you can see the creature.

items.name: Horde Breaker
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter Major Effect #1
entries: All mark damage you deal this combat is increase by 1 die size.

subclassFeature.name: Defensive Tactics
entries: At tier 2, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Escape the Horde
entries: Opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

items.name: Multiattack Defense
entries: When a creature hits you with an attack, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.

items.name: Steel Will
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Prey
entries: At tier 3, you gain another feature from the Hunter's Prey options from tier 1.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter Major Effect #2
entries: You restore 2 charges of your Trained Accuracy feature.

subclassFeature.name: Multiattack
entries: At tier 4, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Volley
entries: You can use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see within your weapon’s range. You must have ammunition for each target, as normal, and you make a separate attack roll for each target.

items.name: Whirlwind Attack
entries: You can use your action to make melee attacks against any number of creatures within your reach, with a separate attack roll for each target.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Hunter's Defence
entries: At tier 5, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

items.name: Evasion
entries: When you are subjected to an effect, such as a red dragon’s fiery breath or a lightning bolt spell, that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on a saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

items.name: Stand Against the Tide
entries: When a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to repeat the same attack against another creature (other than itself) of your choice.

items.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you.

subclassFeature.name: Creeping Doom
entries: From tier 1, you gain 60 feet of darkvision and add your Wisdom modifier to your initiative rolls.
Additionally, whenever you Expose a creature, you may cause it to project a 5-foot-radius sphere of darkness around itself until the Exposure ends.

subclassFeature.name: Gloom Stalker Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Gloom Stalker Rangers have access to the following major effect options:

items: Your weapon damage increases by an amount equal to half your level (rounded up), and does so again whenever you fail to hit with a weapon attack. This effect ends and the bonus damage is lost the next time you score a major success with a weapon attack, after that attack deals damage.

You may move up one space in the initiative tracker. Then, you gain a bonus to weapon damage against creatures behind you in the initiative tracker for the remainder of combat equal to half your level (rounded up).

subclassFeature.name: Dread Ambusher
entries: At tier 2, you master the art of the ambush. During your first turn of each combat, your Speed is increased by 10 feet. If you take the Attack action on that turn, your attack gains one instance of the Empowered Attack feature and deals {@dice 1d8} additional damage of the weapon's base type.

subclassFeature.name: Shadowbane
entries: At tier 2, you are adept at evading and hunting creatures that rely on darkvision. While in darkness, you are invisible to any creature that relies on darkvision to see you in that darkness.
Additionally, whenever you Expose a creature with darkvision, you can modify the final roll on the weakness table by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Precision in Darkness
entries: At tier 3, whenever you score a critical failure or minor success with a weapon attack against a creature in darkness, the attack deals damage as if it were a standard success.

subclassFeature.name: Umbral Veil
entries: At tier 4, your reflexes sharpen when battle begins. During the first round of combat, you have 50% resistance to damage and add half your Wisdom modifier to your armour. Additionally, whenever you take damage during that round, you may immediately use your reaction to shift up to half your Speed.

subclassFeature.name: Eclipsed Deflection
entries: At tier 5, shadows bend instinctively to shield you from harm. Whenever you take damage from an attack made by a creature that is in darkness, you reduce the damage by an amount equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your level. Additionally, you also impose disadvantage on the next attack roll the attacker makes.

subclassFeature.name: Scaffolding
entries: From tier 1, you can use a bonus action to assemble a {@vehicle scaffold} that is up to 10 feet tall in your space or in an unoccupied space adjacent to you, climbing to its top as part of the same bonus action (this movement provokes opportunity attacks as normal). This space must be on solid ground within your reach. Alternatively, while already atop a scaffold, you can increase its height by up to 10 feet. You are considered to be perched while atop a scaffold you have created. Instead of 10 feet, you may instead build a scaffold that is 5 feet tall, or extend the height of a scaffold by 5 feet.

As these scaffolds are not fully solid, they only provide half cover. You can use a bonus action to repair a scaffold within your reach, fully restoring its hit points. When a scaffold is destroyed or disassembled, any creatures atop the scaffold fall, landing {@condition prone} and potentially taking fall damage.

You can have a total height (in feet) of scaffolds assembled at a time equal to 10 times your Wisdom modifier. You cannot build or extend the height of a scaffold if it would cause the total height to exceed this limit. You may use a bonus action to disassemble a scaffold within your reach.
Additionally, you have proficiency in {@skill Magiscience}, and may use {@skill Magiscience} with Dexterity when working with scaffolding or any other improvised platform.

subclassFeature.name: Practised Climber
entries: From tier 1, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking Speed, and may end your turn and make attacks without disadvantage from positions that require squeezing. Additionally, whenever you take fall damage, the damage is calculated as if you had fallen 10 feet fewer.

subclassFeature.name: Perchwright Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Perchwright Rangers have access to the following major effect options:

items: If the triggering attack was made as part of a maneuver, refund its exertion cost by an amount equal to a tenth of the total height of your active scaffolds that you can see. This cannot refund more than a maneuver's total exertion cost. Otherwise, recover one point of exertion.
For the remainder of combat, attacks made while perched deal additional damage equal to half your level (rounded up).

subclassFeature.name: Prepared Positions
entries: From tier 2, whenever you roll for initiative, you can immediately assemble a scaffold with your {@subclassFeature Scaffolding|Ranger|sns|Perchwright|sns|1} feature and climb to its top. If you were already perched, make your initiative check with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Flying Fox
entries: At tier 2, you string lines between heights, grappling and swinging between them with ease. While perched or atop the edge of a raised platform, such as a clifftop, you can move directly to the top of another such platform within 30 feet of you, so long as that platform is of equal or lower elevation than the one you are currently on. This movement costs half as much movement as walking the same distance.

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Vantage
entries: At tier 3, attacking from above lets you find the gaps in a target's guard. The critical success range of your damaging ranged attacks increases by 1 while you have height advantage against your target, and by an additional 1 while you are also perched at least 10 feet above your target.

subclassFeature.name: Unassailable Perch
entries: From tier 4, whenever you take damage from an attack made by a creature that you have height advantage against, you reduce the damage by an amount equal to your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: King of the Hill
entries: At tier 4, whenever a creature starts climbing a scaffold you are perched on, you can use your reaction to make a ranged weapon attack against it.
