Additionally, whenever a creature damages a scaffold you are perched on, you may use a reaction to {@classFeature Expose Weakness|Ranger|sns|1|sns|Expose} that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Apex Strike
entries: From tier 5, while perched, your critical damage bonus is increased by your proficiency bonus for each 10 feet of scaffolding you are on.

subclassFeature.name: Commanding Vantage
entries: At tier 5, any height becomes yours to command. While you are perched, you have height advantage against a creature whenever any part of your body is vertically higher than any part of that creature's body.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you select this subclass at tier 1, you gain proficiency with medium armour and the scimitar.
If you're proficient with a melee weapon, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

subclassFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At tier 1, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

items.name: Dueling
entries: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

items.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
entries: You add your choice of either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage of any weapon you are wielding in your offhand.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Flourish
entries: At tier 1, you learn to conduct impressive displays of martial prowess and speed.

Whenever you take the Attack action on your turn, your Speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn, and if your action's attack hits a creature, you can use one of the following Blade Flourish options of your choice. You can use only one Blade Flourish option per turn.

entries.name: Defensive Flourish
entries: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra base damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You also add the number rolled to your armour until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Slashing Flourish
entries: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra base damage to the target you hit, and cause the same amount of harm of the same type to any other creature adjacent to you of your choice. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die.

entries.name: Mobile Flourish
entries: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to cause the weapon to deal extra base damage to the target you hit. The damage equals the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die. You can also push the target up to 5 feet away from you, plus a number of feet equal to the number you roll on that die. You can then immediately use your reaction to move up to your Speed to an unoccupied space adjacent to the target.

subclassFeature.name: Swords Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Swords Bards have access to the following major effect options:

items: If the triggering attack uses a Blade Flourish, add half your level (rounded up) to the Bardic Inspiration die roll. Otherwise, you can use a Blade Flourish with the attack by rolling a {@dice d4} instead of expending a use of your Bardic Inspiration.

The target loses resistance to the triggering attack's base damage type equal to half your level (rounded up) for 1 round. If the triggering attack killed the target, you can choose another creature within 60 feet of you to be the target of this effect instead, ignoring line of sight.

subclassFeature.name: Ringing Blade
entries: At tier 2, whenever you take the Attack action, you can expend a spell slot to add a number of {@dice d6}s of thunder damage equal to the spell slot's level to your attack's damage.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Attack
entries: From tier 3, when you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Riposte
entries: At tier 4, you learn to turn an enemy's aggression into a burst of controlled arcane power. When a creature fails to hit you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. This attack deals additional base damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die, without consuming the die. Additionally, you can choose one of the following additional effects.

items.name: Disrupting Pulse
entries: The target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

items.name: Arc Step
entries: You can immediately shift up to 10 feet.

items.name: Resonant Echo
entries: Until the end of your next turn, add a {@dice d4} to the die roll for the next Blade Flourish you use.
You can use this feature a number of times between long rests equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Master's Flourish
entries: Starting at tier 5, whenever you use a Blade Flourish option, you can roll a {@dice d6} and use it instead of expending a Bardic Inspiration die.

subclassFeature.name: Guiding Whispers
entries: At tier 1, you can reach out to spirits to guide you and others. You learn the Guidance cantrip, which doesn’t count against the number of bard cantrips you know. For you, it has a range of 60 feet when you cast it.

subclassFeature.name: Spiritual Focus
entries: At tier 1, you employ tools that aid you in channeling spirits, be they historical figures or fictional archetypes. You can use the following objects as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells: a candle, crystal ball, skull, spirit board, or tarokka deck.

subclassFeature.name: Tales From Beyond
entries: At tier 1, you reach out to spirits who tell their tales through you. While you are holding your Spiritual Focus, you can use a bonus action to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and roll on the Spirit Tales table using your Bardic Inspiration die to determine the tale the spirits direct you to tell. You retain the tale in mind until you bestow the tale’s effect or you finish a short or long rest.

You can use an action to choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you (this can be you) to be the target of the tale’s effect. Once you do so, you can’t bestow the tale’s effect again until you roll it again.
You can retain only one of these tales in mind at a time, and rolling on the Spirit Tales table immediately ends the effect of the previous tale.
If the tale requires a saving throw, the DC equals your spell save DC.

entries.caption: Spirit Tales
rows: Tale of the Clever Animal. For the next 10 minutes, whenever the target makes an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma check, the target can roll an extra die immediately after rolling the d20 and add the extra die's number to the check. The extra die is the same type as your Bardic Inspiration die.
Tale of the Renowned Duelist. You make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes force damage equal to two rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier.

Tale of the Beloved Friends. The target and another creature of its choice it can see within 5 feet of it gains temporary hit points equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier.

Tale of the Runaway. The target can immediately use its reaction to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. When the target teleports, it can choose a number of creatures it can see within 30 feet of it up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 0) to immediately use the same reaction.
Tale of the Avenger. For 1 minute, any creature that hits the target with a melee attack takes force damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die.

Tale of the Traveler. The target gains temporary hit points equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die + your bard level. While it has these temporary hit points, the target’s walking speed increases by 10 feet and it gains a +1 bonus to its AC.

Tale of the Beguiler. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or take psychic damage equal to two rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die, and the target is incapacitated until the end of its next turn.

Tale of the Phantom. The target becomes invisible until the end of its next turn or until it hits a creature with an attack. If it hits a creature with an attack during this invisibility, the creature it hits takes necrotic damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die and is frightened of the target until the end of the frightened creature's next turn.

Tale of the Brute. Each creature of the target’s choice it can see within 30 feet of it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes thunder damage equal to three rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die and is knocked prone. A creature that succeeds on its saving throw takes half as much damage and isn’t knocked prone.

Tale of the Dragon. The target spews fire from the mouth in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage equal to four rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Tale of the Angel. The target regains hit points equal to two rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier, and you end one condition from the following list affecting the target: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, petrified, or poisoned.

Tale of the Mind-Bender You envoke an incomprehensible fable from an otherworldly being. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or take psychic damage equal to three rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die and be stunned until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Spirits Major Effect #1
entries: For 1 round your spirits inflict vulnerability to the next instance of damage from a type of your choosing.

subclassFeature.name: Spiritual Potency
entries: At tier 2, when you cast a bard spell that deals damage or restores hit points through the Spiritual Focus, roll a d6, and you gain a bonus to the damage or healing of the spell equal to the number rolled.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Session
entries: At tier 3, spirits provide you with supernatural insights. You can conduct an hour-long ritual channeling spirits (which can be done during a short or long rest) using your Spiritual Focus. You can conduct the ritual with a number of willing creatures equal to your proficiency bonus (including yourself). At the end of the ritual, you temporarily learn one spell of your choice from any class.

The spell you choose must be of a level equal to the number of creatures that conducted the ritual or less, the spell must be of a level you can cast, and it must be in the school of Divination or Necromancy. The chosen spell counts as a bard spell for you but doesn't count against the number of bard spells you know.
Once you perform the ritual, you can't do so again until you start a long rest, and you know the chosen spell until you start a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Spirits Major Effect #2
entries: Your spirits manifest to aid or hinder. Choose an ally or enemy in 120' of you to be the target. If an ally is the target your spirit heals the target for 1d8 + your level. If an enemy they receive 1d10 + your level in psychic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Spiritual Guidance
entries: At tier 4, during a short or long rest you may consult your spiritual companions for guidance, you may choose a “Tale from beyond” to recall. Tales recalled this way do not count towards your limit of retained tales and you may still use your bonus action to recall other tales.
When spending your action to use a tale you may choose to use this 'banked' tale.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Connection
entries: At tier 5, you now have the ability to nudge the spirits of Tales from Beyond toward certain tales. Whenever you roll on the Spirit Tales table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to bestow. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

subclassFeature.name: Sound Sculptor's Tools
entries: When you select this subclass at tier 1, you gain proficiency with the {@skill Persuasion} skill. If you are already proficient in {@skill Persuasion}, you gain an expertise die instead. You also learn the {@spell message}, {@spell minor illusion}, and {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrips, and they do not count against your maximum cantrips known.

subclassFeature.name: Eyes of the Ears
entries: At tier 1, you learn to use sound to locate objects and creatures around you. As a bonus action, you can sing, stomp, or play a musical instrument, concentrating on your music as if it were a spell. Until the start of your next turn, any creature within 300 feet of you that can hear you automatically detects your presence, but you also gain blindsight with a range of 30 feet. If you already have blindsight, its range increases by 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Sound Sculptor Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Sound Sculptor Bards have access to the following major effect options:

items: You immediately gain an additional concentration slot that lasts until the end of the current combat. You can only gain one concentration slot from this effect. If you have already gained this effect's additional concentration slot, you instead gain a bonus to your resistance equal to half your level (minimum +1) for 1 round.
For 1 round, you count as having line of sight to all creatures you can sense within 30 feet of you and your areas only affect creatures of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Vital Reverberations
entries: At tier 2, you learn how to manipulate reverberations to help or hinder vital functions, such as beats of the heart or pulses through the nervous system. While concentrating on your Eyes of the Ears feature, creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you are unable recover hit points, except through resting. Additionally, whenever an enemy creature within that area becomes bloodied, you may choose another creature within that area to heal a number of hit points equal to two rolls of your bardic inspiration die.

subclassFeature.name: Deafening Presence
entries: At tier 3, you can weaponise the sound you create into a deafening cacophany. While concentrating on your Eyes of the Ears feature, all creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you are {@condition deafened} to all sounds except those from you, and distracted, granting reflex attacks made against them advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Discordance
entries: At tier 4, you learn to use your mastery over physical sound to control the battlefield. As a reaction to any creature within 30 feet of you moving into a space adjacent to another creature while you are concentrating on your Eyes of the Ears feature, you may push the moving creature 20 feet in a horizontal direction of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Dome of Sound
entries: At tier 5, you gain the most potent sound sculptor ability: the creation of a dome of sound. As an action while concentrating on your Eyes of the Ears feature, you can create a 30-foot radius sphere of physical sound centred on you. The dome lasts for as long as you maintain concentration on your Eyes of the Ears feature. The area is difficult terrain, and reverberations in the air grant disadvantage to {@skill Perception} checks made inside it. Ranged armour attacks fired within the area are also made at disadvantage. The area is a persistent zone which forces creatures to make a save. On a failed save, a creature loses concentration on any effect they are concentrating on. When you first create the dome, you can choose any creatures you can sense to be unaffected by the dome's effects for its duration. You can use this feature once between long rests.

subclassFeature.name: Talented Tactician
entries: The most brilliant of tactician marshals are those who can sit far away from the fronstlines and yet picture every move their enemy might take. For each move, they plan an appropriate response to drive their foe into desperation and surrender without ever firing a shot or lifting a sword.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Edge
entries: Beginning at tier 1 when you choose this archetype, whenever you roll initiative, you gain a tactics die, which is a {@dice d4}. While you have this die, any creature friendly to you within 60 feet of you that can hear or see you can roll the tactics die as a bonus die when making an armour attack roll. Once rolled, the tactics die is lost until you regain it at the start of your next turn. The tactics die disappears when the combat ends.

If you start your turn and no one has rolled the tactics die, you can trade out the die for a die of the next larger size, such that a {@dice d4} becomes a {@dice d6}, a {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}, and so on up to a maximum of {@dice d12}. After the tactics die is rolled, when regained it reverts to a {@dice d4}.

subclassFeature.name: Talented Tactician Major Effect #1
entries: Your tactics dice immediately increase by 2 sizes. If this would increase either of them beyond a {@dice d12}, they instead gain a +1 bonus for each size increase overflow.

subclassFeature.name: Student of War
entries: At tier 2, choose either {@skill History} or {@skill Nature}. You gain proficiency with the chosen skill and an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.

subclassFeature.name: Eye for Strategy
entries: Also at tier 2, you can quickly identify how a creature might fight. Whenever you roll for initiative, choose a hostile creature you can see within 60 feet of you and make either a {@skill History}, {@skill Magiscience}, or {@skill Nature} check (chosen by the DM) with a DC of 15. If you succeed, you learn a random one of the creature's passive traits. If the creature doesn't have enough for you to learn, you then learn random non-standard actions. If the creature again doesn't have enough for you to learn, you then learn random remaining actions. For every 2 you beat the DC by, you learn one additional passive trait or action.

subclassFeature.name: Operations Leader
entries: Starting at tier 3, you devise a set of communication cues. Any allies who have received this training - usually taking 10 minutes of discussion and practise - do not need to share a language with you to benefit from this feature.

Additionally on your turn, you can use a bonus action and spend 5 exertion points to make it easier for you and your allies to help each other with certain skills. While this coordination is in place, you and your chosen allies can take the Help action as a bonus action to help each other with skill checks. This coordination requires you to maintain concentration, and both allies - the one taking the Help action and the one making the skill check - to be within 30 feet of you at the time of the skill check.

You can use your bonus action to change which allies benefit from the coordination each turn and you are able to concentrate on it for up to 10 minutes. The skills that can benefit from this are {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Brawn}, {@skill Deception}, {@skill Finesse}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Stealth}, and {@skill Survival}. This might be used to help the team navigate difficult terrain, maintain a facade while undercover, or perform an ambush.

If you have proficiency in {@skill Stealth} this coordination can happen silently via surreptitious signals. If you have proficiency in {@skill Deception} this coordination can happen as a series of code words interspersed with other conversation. An onlooker needs to succeed on an {@skill Insight} check contested by your ability check - whether {@skill Stealth} or {@skill Deception} - to realise the coordination is taking place.

subclassFeature.name: Talented Tactician Major Effect #2
entries: You learn a passive trait or action about the attack's target as per your Eye for Strategy feature. If you already know all of this information about the target, the success tiers of your attacks against them are increased by 1 for 1 round.
If the target was killed by the attack, you instead choose another creature within 60 feet of you as the target of this major effect. You do not require line of sight to this other creature.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Tactics
entries: At tier 4, choose one of the following.

entries.name: More Tactical
entries: Whenever you gain a tactics die, you gain an additional tactics die to a maximum of two dice. The second tactics die can be used like the first and the tactics die increases size in the manner described under the Tactical Edge class feature.

entries.name: Tactical Efficacy
entries: Your tactics die begins at {@dice d6}. When you reach 20th level, your tactics die begins at {@dice d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Hidden Resources
entries: At tier 5, choose one of the following.

entries.name: Dig Deeper
entries: You can take a bonus action to allow a creature to regain the use of a class feature that would normally be regained by finishing a short rest. You can use this feature twice and you regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Reach Further
entries: While you are conscious and when an ally within range of your Commanding Presence would drop to 0 hit points, your ally can use their reaction to spend the tactics die and roll it to gain temporary hit points equal to the result plus your Charisma modifier plus your proficiency bonus. Once a creature has benefitted from the tactics die in this way, it cannot do so again until it finishes a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Incorporeal Arms
entries: Beginning at tier 1 when you choose this subclass, you can use a bonus action and spend 3 exertion to turn your arms partially incorporeal for a number of rounds equal to your Wisdom modifier. You can make your arms corporeal again by using a bonus action or your reaction.

While you arms are partially incorporeal, you add a second martial arts die to damage dealt by your unarmed strikes as your blows disrupt your target internally. You can also use this partial incorporeality to use an action to reach through an object and interact with what is just on the other side, such as a door handle, window latch, or item. You cannot use this ability to pass through metal objects or surfaces. However, when you do this, you suffer 5 ({@dice 1d10}) force harm every round you remain in the object. If this feature's duration ends while you are inside an object, you suffer 10 ({@dice 2d10}) force harm as your arms are ejected.

subclassFeature.name: Ghostly Soul Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Ghostly Soul Adepts have access to the following major effect options:

items: You recover exertion equal to your proficiency bonus and hit points equal to your level.
The target suffers {@dice 1d10} force harm and your next attack against the target has advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Armour
entries: From tier 2, while any part of your body is incorporeal, you gain a +1 bonus to your armour and creatures you target with attacks suffer force harm equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Ghostly Strikes
entries: At tier 3, you learn how to land a blow while manipulating your corporeality. As part of your Attack action, you can spend 1 exertion to convert all damage dealt by your unarmed strikes and melee weapon armour attacks to force damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Spectral Step
entries: Starting at tier 4, you learn how to make your entire body as insubstantial as a spirit's. You can use an action and spend 5 exertion to become incorporeal for up to a number of rounds equal to your Wisdom modifier. You can also end this effect by using a bonus action or your reaction. While incorporeal, you can still be seen as a faded version of yourself, but you gain advantage on all {@skill Stealth} checks, 50% resistance to all types of damage except force, and may pass through objects and creatures as if they were difficult terrain.

You suffer 10 ({@dice 2d10}) force harm if you end your turn inside an object. If you end your turn in a space occupied by a creature, you both suffer force harm equal to your martial arts die times half your Wisdom modifier. This feature's duration ends while you are inside either an object or a creature, you suffer 10 ({@dice 2d10}) force harm, are shunted to the nearest open space, and suffer an additional 5 ({@dice 1d10}) force harm for every space shunted.

subclassFeature.name: Ghostly Stride
entries: At tier 5, you are able to move through the world as lightly as the ghosts you emulate. You gain a fly speed equal to half your Speed and the ability to hover, and also gain an expertise die on all {@skill Finesse} and {@skill Stealth} checks. You are also immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition.

subclassFeature.name: The Genie
entries: You have made a pact with one of the rarest kinds of genie, a noble genie. Such entities rule vast fiefs on the Elemental Planes and have great influence over lesser genies and elemental creatures. Noble genies are varied in their motivations, but most are arrogant and wield power that rivals that of lesser deities. They delight in turning the table on mortals, who often bind genies into servitude, and readily enter into pacts that expand their reach.
You choose your patron's kind or determine it randomly, using the Genie Kind table.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: {@i 1st-level Genie feature}

The Genie lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The Genie Expanded Spells table shows the genie spells that are added to the warlock spell list for you, along with the spells associated in the table with your patron's kind: dao, djinni, efreeti, or marid.

subclassFeature.name: Genie's Vessel
entries: At tier 1, your patron gifts you a magical vessel that grants you a measure of the genie's power. The vessel is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. You decide what the object is, or you can determine what it is randomly by rolling on the Genie's Vessel table.
While you are touching the vessel, you can use it in the following ways:

entries.name: Bottled Respite
entries: As an action, you can magically vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space you left. The interior of the vessel is an extradimensional space in the shape of a 20-foot-radius cylinder, 20 feet high, and resembles your vessel. The interior is appointed with cushions and low tables and is a comfortable temperature. While inside, you can hear the area around your vessel as if you were in its space. You can remain inside the vessel up to a number of hours equal to twice your proficiency bonus. You exit the vessel early if you use a bonus action to leave, if you die, or if the vessel is destroyed. When you exit the vessel, you appear in an unoccupied space closest to it. Any objects left in the vessel remain there until carried out, and if the vessel is destroyed, every object stored there harmlessly appears in the unoccupied spaces closest to the vessel's former space. Once you enter the vessel, you can't enter again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Genie's Wrath
entries: Once during each of your turns when you hit with an armour attack, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your proficiency bonus. The type of this damage is determined by your patron: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).
The vessel's armour equals your spellcasting ability modifier. Its hit points equal double your warlock level, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage.

If the vessel is destroyed or you lose it, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest, and the previous vessel is destroyed if it still exists. The vessel vanishes in a flare of elemental power when you die.

subclassFeature.name: Genie Major Effect #1
entries: For 1 round, the target of this attack takes additional damage equal to your proficiency bonus, of a type determined by your patron’s kind, whenever it is hit by an attack. If the target dies from this attack, creatures of your choice that you can see immediately take this damage instead.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Gift
entries: From tier 2, you begin to take on characteristics of your patron's kind. You now have 50% resistance to a damage type determined by your patron's kind: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).

In addition, as a bonus action, you can give yourself a flying speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes, during which you can hover. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Assisted Arcana
entries: You learn each of the spells specific to your patron’s kind available on your expanded spell list. Additionally, when learning warlock spells, you may learn any spell that can deal damage matching your patron’s kind. Whenever you cast either such spells, their spell point cost is reduced by 50%.

subclassFeature.name: Genie Major Effect #2
entries: For 1 round, if you cast a spell that would have its spell point cost reduced by your Assisted Arcana feature, its spell point cost is 0 instead.

subclassFeature.name: Sanctuary Vessel
entries: From tier 4, when you enter your Genie's Vessel via the Bottled Respite feature, you can now choose up to five willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you, and the chosen creatures are drawn into the vessel with you.
As a bonus action, you can eject any number of creatures from the vessel, and everyone is ejected if you leave or die or if the vessel is destroyed.

In addition, anyone (including you) who remains within the vessel for at least 10 minutes gains the benefit of finishing a short rest, and anyone can add your level to the number of hit points they regain if they spend any Hit Dice as part of a short rest there.

subclassFeature.name: Limited Wish
entries: From tier 5, you can entreat your patron to grant you a small wish. As an action, you can speak your desire to your Genie's Vessel, requesting the effect of one spell that is 6th level or lower and has a casting time of 1 action. The spell can be from any class's spell list, and you don't need to meet the requirements of that spell, including components; the spell simply takes effect as part of this action.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish {@dice 1d4} long rests.

subclassFeature.name: Sired
entries: Sired savants have been infected, or blessed, with vampirism by a more senior vampire. This process is known as being sired by that vampire.

subclassFeature.name: Vampiric Transformation
entries: At tier 1 when you first take this subclass, you gain a bite natural weapon, which you can use an action. The weapon cannot be used in any other way, including as part of the Attack action, as a reaction, or when triggered by a maneuver. The bite weapon deals {@dice 1d6} + Strength piercing damage, and you recover hit points equal to the damage it deals on a hit.
Additionally, you gain 30 feet of darkvision.

subclassFeature.name: Sired Major Effect #1
entries: For 1 round, you ignore the effects of sunlight on you and gain +3 armour against attackers who do not have darkvision.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: From tier 2, your bite natural weapon has advantage on attack rolls against targets with fewer hit points than their maximum.
Additionally, your bite natural weapon recovers additional hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier on a hit.

subclassFeature.name: Supernatural Senses
entries: At tier 3, you gain 60 feet of blindsight.

subclassFeature.name: Sired Major Effect #2
entries: For 1 round, you gain a +2 bonus to armour attack rolls against targets with fewer hit points than their maximum.

subclassFeature.name: Emerging Immortality
entries: At tier 4, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have 50% resistance to necrotic damage.
Whenever you would become {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, make a save, ignoring the condition on a success.
Whenever you would drop to 0 hit points, make a disadvantaged save, remaining at 1 hit point instead on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Perfect Senses
entries: Also at tier 4, you gain 30 feet of true sight.

subclassFeature.name: Eternal Form
entries: From tier 5, as a bonus action, you can cast {@spell Gaseous Form} on yourself at will, as if it were cast with a 5th level spell slot.
Additionally, you recover hit points equal to your Strength modifier whenever you start your turn while conscious and not in sunlight.

subclassFeature.name: Chronal Shift
entries: At tier 1, you can magically exert limited control over the flow of time around a creature. As a reaction, after you or a creature within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a save, you can force the creature to reroll. You make this decision after the DM conveys the results of the roll. The trget must use the result of the second roll.
You can use this feature twice, and you regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Temporal Awareness
entries: Starting at tier 1, you can add your Intelligence modifier to your initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Chronurgy Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Chronurgy Wizards have access to the following major effect options:

items: The initiative order is immediately revealed to you. Then, you may move an ally on the initiative tracker to any position on the tracker.

You warp time around the target, causing them to become {@condition rattled} and suffer {@dice 1d8} ongoing force harm, stacking until they pass a passive save. This harm increases to {@dice 2d8} at 5th level, {@dice 3d8} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d8} at 17th level.

subclassFeature.name: Momentary Stasis
entries: When you reach tier 3, as an action, you can magically make a fortitude attack against a Large or smaller creature within 60 feet of you, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target is encased in a field of magical energy until the end of your next turn or until it takes any damage. While encased in this way, the creature is {@condition slowed}.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but the creature is {@condition unconscious} while encased instead of {@condition slowed}.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also {@condition slowed} for 1 round after it is released from the field.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Altered Fate
entries: From tier 3, after you influence an attack with your Chronal Shift feature, you may choose to also either increase or decrease the attack's success tier by one.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Abeyance
entries: At tier 4, when you cast a spell using a spell slot of 4th level or lower, you can condense the spell's magic into a mote. The spell is frozen in time at the moment of casting and held within a grey bead for 1 hour. This bead is a Tiny object with 2 armour and 2 hit points, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage and harm. When the duration ends, or if the bead is destroyed, it vanishes in a flash of light, and the spell is lost.

A creature holding the bead can use its action to release the spell within, whereupon the bead disappears. The spell uses your spellcasting ability, but otherwise treats the creature who released it as the caster for all other purposes.
Once you create a bead with this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Convergent Future
entries: Starting at tier 5, you can peer through possible futures and magically pull one of them into events around you, ensuring a particular outcome. When you or a creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a save, you can use your reaction to ignore the die roll and immediately decide whether it succeeds or fails (or, in the case of an attack, whether it scores a standard or minor success).
When you use this feature, you gain one level of {@condition strife}. Strife gained in this way are lost whenever you take a long rest, but cannot be removed in any other way.

subclassFeature.name: Sculpt Spells
entries: Beginning at tier 1, you can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocation spells. When you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Evoker Major Effect #1
entries: Your next spell counts as upcasted 1 level.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Cantrip
entries: Starting at tier 2, your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, the creature takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Evocation
entries: Beginning at tier 3, you can add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to one damage roll of any wizard evocation spell that you cast.

subclassFeature.name: Evoker Major Effect #2
entries: Your spell echoes in the area again at the start of your next turn. Echoes are less powerful, dealing 1d4 + your level in damage to all enemies in the area.

subclassFeature.name: Overchannel
entries: Starting at tier 4, you can increase the power of your simpler spells. When you cast a wizard spell of 1st through 5th level that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell.

The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a long rest, you take 2d12 necrotic damage for each level of the spell, immediately after you cast it. Each time you use this feature again before finishing a long rest, the necrotic damage per spell level increases by 1d12. This damage ignores resistance and immunity.

subclassFeature.name: Master Evocation
entries: Starting at tier 5, you no longer make major effect choices, instead you apply all your major effects.

subclassFeature.name: Sun Priest
entries: At each indicated level, add the listed spells to your spells prepared. They do not count towards your limit.

entries.name: Favoured Illumination
entries: Starting at tier 1 when you choose this subclass, you learn the {@spell dancing lights}, {@spell light}, or {@spell produce flame} cantrip. This does not count against your number of known cantrips.

entries.name: Flash Fight
entries: Also at tier 1, you learn that light is everything on the battlefield. You can use an action to touch a hostile creature within your reach, gently illuminating it with your divine will and granting advantage on armour attacks made against it before the start of your next turn.

Alternatively, you can use an action to touch an ally within reach, surrounding them with a coruscating shimmer of bright light. Until the start of your next turn, creatures have disadvantage on armour attacks against the touched ally.
Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Sun Priest Major Effect #1
entries: The target makes a saving throw, becoming blinded for 1 round on a failed save. On a successful save, or if they were already blinded, their attacks have -1AB for 1 round. If the target dies from this attack, your next blinding effect during the combat has advantaged offence.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Radiant Burst
entries: Starting at tier 2, you can use an action to clutch your holy symbol and emit a bright blast. In addition to dispelling any magical darkness within 30 feet, make a fortitude attack against all enemy creatures within 30 feet, with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: The target has disadvantage with armour attacks for 1 round.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition blinded} by the light for 1 minute, or until it succeeds on a passive save.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but the target is also {@condition debilitated}.
Alternatively, you can choose to make the light more harmful. Instead of any of the above outcomes of the fortitude attack, you can have it deal radiant damage equal to {@dice 2d12} + your level.

subclassFeature.name: Honed Searing
entries: Also from tier 2, no more than once per turn when you roll 1 on a damage die for fire or radiant damage, you may treat that roll as if it was the maximum value on that die.

subclassFeature.name: Fierce Flash
entries: Beginning at tier 3, you can use Flash Fight on creatures within 30 feet of you in addition to creatures you can touch.

subclassFeature.name: Sun Priest Major Effect #2
entries: Any number of creatures of your choice that you can see and are in bright light gain +3 to saving throws for 1 round.

subclassFeature.name: Harness the Flame
entries: Starting at tier 4, when you make a damaging attack with a cleric cantrip or a weapon, add fire or radiant damage equal to your Wisdom modifier. You must choose the damage type at the beginning of each long rest, and may not change it until you have completed another long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Summon the Stars
entries: At tier 5, you are able to manipulate the burning brilliance of the stars themselves. As an action, choose an object or creature within 60 feet. For 1 minute the target emits bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. The target emits a 30-foot persistent zone around it that forces creatures to make a save or become {@condition blinded} for 1 minute, or until they pass a passive save.

The first time this feature is used per long rest, the persistent zone also causes {@dice 8d6} radiant harm. Magical auras within the bright light become visible for 1 hour, shedding dim light in a 5-foot radius for 1 hour. An {@condition invisible} creature that takes radiant damage from the light is also revealed and it sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius for 1 hour.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Sea Spells
entries: When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Sea Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Circle of the Sea Spells
rows: {@spell fog cloud}, {@spell ray of frost}, {@spell thunderwave}

subclassFeature.name: Wrath of the Sea
entries: From tier 1, as a bonus action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to emit a 5-foot radius of ocean spray centred on you for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), manifest it again, or have the {@condition unconscious} condition.

When you manifest the spray and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns, you can choose another creature in the spray. The target suffers cold harm equal to a number of d6s equal to half your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one die), and then makes a save if it is Large or smaller. On a fail, the target is pushed 15 feet away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Sea Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Sea Druids have access to the following major effect options:

items: If you are in combat, the number of d6s of harm your Wrath of the Sea causes is increased by one for the remainder of combat.

You regain one use of your Wild Shape feature and recover hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your level. If you have not expended any uses of your Wild Shape feature, you instead recover hit points equal to {@dice 2d10} plus your level.

subclassFeature.name: Aquatic Affinity
entries: At tier 2, the radius of the ocean spray created by your Wrath of the Sea increases to 10 feet.
In addition, you gain a swim speed equal to your Speed.

subclassFeature.name: Rising Swell
entries: From tier 3, your Wrath of the Sea causes additional cold harm equal to three times the number of rounds that have started this combat.

subclassFeature.name: Stormborn
entries: From tier 4, your Wrath of the Sea confers two more benefits while active, as detailed below.

items.name: Flight
entries: You gain a fly speed equal to your Speed.

items.name: Resistance
entries: You have 50% resistance to cold, lightning, and fire damage.

subclassFeature.name: Oceanic Gift
entries: At tier 5, instead of manifesting the ocean spray of Wrath of the Sea around yourself, you can manifest it around one willing creature within 60 feet of yourself. That creature gains all the benefits of the spray and uses your Wisdom modifier for it.
In addition, you can manifest the spray around both the other creature and yourself if you expend two uses of your Wild Shape feature instead of one when manifesting it.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Wild Shape
entries: When you choose this subclass at tier 1, you gain the ability to use Wild Shape on your turn as a bonus action, rather than as an action.
Additionally, while you are shapeshifted with Wild Shape, you can use a bonus action to expend one spell slot to regain {@dice 2d4} hit points per level of spell slot expended.

subclassFeature.name: Moon Major Effect #1
entries: If you are shapeshifted, you gain temporary hit points equal to half the triggering attack's damage, which stack with any existing temporary hit points.
If you are not shapeshifted, you gain half as many temporary hit points, which stack with any existing temporary hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Circle Forms
entries: The rites of your circle grant you the ability to transform into more dangerous animal forms. Starting at tier 2, the bestial strike action on your animal forms gains 1 additional instance of the Empowered Attack feature, multiplying the damage 1 additional time.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Strike
entries: Starting at tier 2, your weapon attacks in beast form ignore resistance and immunities.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Fortitude
entries: Starting at tier 3, the initial temporary hit points granted by wild shape is increased by an amount equal to double your level.

subclassFeature.name: Moon Major Effect #2
entries: You immediately shift to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you that is adjacent to an ally. Each creature of your choice that you passed through during this movement must make a save. On a fail, a creature suffers bludgeoning harm equal to your proficiency bonus and is {@condition rattled} until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Wild Shape
entries: At 10th level, you can expend two uses of Wild Shape at the same time to shapeshift into an {@creature Elemental Form} of one of the following types: Air, Earth, Fire, or Water.

subclassFeature.name: Thousand Forms
entries: By tier 5, you have learned to use magic to alter your physical form in more subtle ways. You can cast the {@spell Alter Self} spell at will.

subclassFeature.name: Synaptic Surgeon
entries: At its core, all mental activity stems from jolts of electricity leaping between synapses in the mind. The synaptic surgeon uses inventions that harness these surges of energy, manipulating them in ways that boost allies and hinder foes.

subclassFeature.name: Synaptic Surgeon Spells
entries: At tier 1, when you prepare your artificer spells you can prepare certain spells and create spell inventions for them in addition to your normal number of prepared spells. These spells count as artificer spells for you, even if they aren't on the artificer spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Synaptic Gadget
entries: Also at tier 1, you create a unique device capable of manipulating the electric impulses in a living creature: a synaptic gadget. This device can take many forms, such as a pair of gloves that generate a manipulative static charge or a clockwork invention that generates a mind-altering frequency. Regardless of its form, only you know how to manipulate it effectively, and the device can only affect living creatures with an Intelligence score of at least 3 unless otherwise stated. You can't use your synaptic gadget on yourself unless a feature allows it.

Your synaptic gadget has a number of uses equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you complete a short or long rest by taking the time to recharge it. You cannot regain expended uses this way if you don't have access to your tools of artifice.
You can spend a use of your synaptic gadget to manipulate the mind in a variety of ways.

entries.name: Adrenal Surge
entries: As a bonus action, you apply your synaptic gadget to a willing creature within reach, including yourself, to suppress pain or push muscles to their limit. Choose Strength or Dexterity. The target gains advantage on ability checks for the chosen ability score, and either fortitude or reflex attacks have disadvantage against it respectively, until the end of its next turn. Its weapon attacks governed by the chosen ability deal an additional {@dice 1d4} base damage for the duration. This damage increases by 1 die at 5th level, 11th level, and 17th level (maximum {@dice 4d4}).

At the end of the duration, the creature suffers a level of {@condition fatigue} that persists until cured or the combat ends. The creature can't benefit from this feature again until this level of {@condition fatigue} is removed. {@condition Fatigue} suffered from other sources does not affect this limitation.

entries.name: Danger Sense
entries: As a bonus action, you apply your synaptic gadget to a willing creature within reach, heightening its awareness. For a number of rounds equal to your Intelligence modifier, the target gains advantage on {@skill Insight} and {@skill Perception} checks and its armour increases by 2 as long as it is not {@condition staggered} and its Speed is greater than 0. This bonus increases by 1 at 5th level, 11th level, and 17th level (maximum 5).

entries.name: Disruptive Burst
entries: As an action, you can weaponise your synaptic gadget to disrupt the target's nervous system. This feature has a range of 15 feet. Make a ranged spell armour attack that deals {@dice 1d10} lightning damage. On a major hit, the target is also {@condition slowed} until the end of its next turn. This damage increases by {@dice 1d10} at 5th level, 11th level, and 17th level (maximum {@dice 4d10}).

subclassFeature.name: Synaptic Surgeon Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Synaptic Surgeon Artificers have access to the following major effect options:

items: You immediately use your Synaptic Gadget feature against a creature within its range. This does not require or expend a use of the gadget.
You repair a fizzled spell invention that contained a spell from your Synaptic Surgeon Spells.

subclassFeature.name: Surgeon's Learnings
entries: At tier 2, you gain an amount of additional uses of your synaptic gadget between rests equal to your number of cantrips known.

subclassFeature.name: Triggered Surge
entries: At tier 3, you can spend a use of your synaptic gadget to create a beneficial effect that only triggers under conditions you determine. Activating this feature requires you to touch the gadget to a target for one minute, after which you make an advantaged melee will attack with the following outcomes against an unwilling creature, or automatically use the standard success result for a willing one:

items.name: Minor success
entries: Designate the condition that will cause the surge to trigger, much like setting a posthypnotic suggestion. Only one condition can be designated, and once established it can't be changed for the duration of this feature. Likewise, allies must be designated (or described, if the creature has never seen them, though this occasionally leads to misidentification and mishaps) for the purposes of this feature when the condition is designated and can't be changed for the duration. The creature must be conscious for the effect to trigger.

Example conditions include: the creature is the target of a spell not cast by an ally; the creature is the target of a weapon attack; the creature falls from a height of more than 10 feet; the creature hears a designated phrase spoken (the creature must be able to hear the phrase to trigger the effect, but does not need to understand it); the creature casts a damaging spell; the creature makes a melee weapon attack against an ally.
When the condition is triggered, all attacks against the target have advantage for one round.

The effect ends early if the creature becomes {@condition unconscious}, is reduced to 0 hit points, or you spend 1 minute with your synaptic gadget removing the suggestion. This does not require an additional use of your gadget. The target can't be subjected to more than one triggered surge at a time and the trigger wears off if the conditions are not met for 24 hours. Once a condition is triggered, the creature realises it was affected by magic if it was not otherwise aware.

items.name: Standard success
entries: As minor success, but choose from the effect options below.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, and the target is also vulnerable to all damage for the effect's duration.
Additional effect options:

entries.name: Triggered Vigour
entries: Choose Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. When the condition is met, the creature gains the effects of the {@spell enhance ability} spell for the chosen ability. This effect does not require concentration.

entries.name: Triggered Rebuke
entries: Choose {@spell blindness/deafness} or {@spell hold person}. When the condition is met, the creature is affected by the standard success result of the chosen spell. This effect does not require concentration.

subclassFeature.name: Advanced Synaptic Gadget
entries: At tier 4, you've plumbed the depths of the mind. You can now use the following effects.

entries.name: Disorienting Frequency
entries: Your synaptic gadget can now emit a sound that muddles the mind. As an action, you can spend a use of your synaptic gadget to make a 30-foot cone of sound. Make an area spell fortitude attack against each creature within the area that deals {@dice 7d6} thunder and psychic damage. On a hit, a target is also {@condition slowed} until the end of its next turn. On a minor success, the target is {@condition rattled} for the same duration. This feature affects all living creatures, regardless of Intelligence.

entries.name: Disruptive Overload
entries: When using a Disruptive Burst, you can expend an infusion use as part of the action to supercharge your synaptic gadget and enable it to affect undead creatures and constructs made of conductive materials.

entries.name: Improved Triggered Vigour
entries: When using your Triggered Vigour on a willing creature, you can spend an infusion use as part of the process to boost two ability scores rather than one. However, the creature can't benefit from your Triggered Vigour again for 24 hours once its has been affected in this way.

entries.name: Rapid Triggered Surge
entries: When using your Triggered Surge feature, you can spend an infusion use to implant the trigger over the course of 1 round. During this time you can't take actions, bonus actions, or reactions or spend any movement. If the creature is unwilling, your attack against it does not have advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Restore the Mind
entries: Starting at tier 5, you can spend a use of your synaptic gadget to restore brain activity to its normal state or even temporarily revive someone whose mind has stopped working. By using your synaptic gadget on a living creature, you can remove either a level of {@condition strife}, the {@condition charmed} condition, or a reduction to a single mental ability score. A creature can only benefit from this feature once a week.

If you use your synaptic gadget on a corpse, you can reactivate its brain activity for a short time. If the target died within the last week, you can ask it questions as though you had cast {@spell speak with dead} without components. If the target died within the last 24 hours, you can also pass the electric impulses into its muscles, animating it to complete one task that takes 10 minutes or less. The temporarily revived target functions as a {@creature zombie}. It can act on information it knew in life but cannot process new information or make decisions on its own.

subclassFeature.name: Stitcher
entries: With the careful grafting of flesh and a dash of lightning, what once was dead may live again! Stitchers are artificers who delve into the marvelous mysteries of life, death, and reanimated flesh. Many stitchers begin in medical fields but once they see that dead flesh move for the first time it's hard to ignore the potential of magiscience. Stitchers may continue to work as doctors and mend the sick, though their true passion now requires corpses flesh as raw material for their experiments.

subclassFeature.name: Stitcher Spells
entries: At tier 1, when you prepare your artificer spells you can prepare certain spells and create spell inventions for them (or forgo them for modifications) in addition to your normal number of prepared spells. These spells count as artificer spells for you, even if they aren't on the artificer spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Monstrous Minion
entries: Also at tier 1 you learn the secrets of giving life to dead flesh and become able to create a monstrous minion.

You can create this monstrous minion by spending a long rest working on a humanoid corpse that has been dead no more than 24 hours, or the corpse of a previously-killed monstrous minion. This monstrous minion is unique and its exact appearance is up to you. It is a minion and requires your concentration to maintain, but you cannot lose this concentration against your will. Your monstrous minion makes death saves and tracks wounds upon reaching 0 hit points.
Your monstrous minion is a {@creature zombie} with the following improvements.

items: Your monstrous minion's type changes to construct.
Whenever your monstrous minion rolls initiative, it gains a number of temporary hit points equal to your artificer level squared.
Your monstrous minion's armour increases by an amount equal to half your proficiency bonus.
Your monstrous minion adds your proficiency bonus to its damage rolls.
Your monstrous minion regains all lost hit points at the end of a long rest so long as you tend to it with your tools of artifice.

entries: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can graft modifications onto your monstrous minion at the cost of infusion uses. These modifications only remain functional due to your infusions, and you can maintain these modifications as if they were infused items. Any modifications you fail to maintain are removed whenever you finish a long rest. You can install any of the following modifications at the cost of 1 infusion each.

entries.name: Exploding Bile
entries: Your monstrous minion does not make death saves or track wounds, and the first time it would make a death save it instead explodes and is destroyed. The minion makes a reflex attack against creatures within 10 feet of it when it explodes, dealing {@dice 3d4} poison damage. This damage increases to {@dice 6d4} when you reach 11th level.

entries.name: Extra Arms
entries: Your monstrous minion gains a climb speed of 15 feet. In addition, creatures attempting to escape from a grapple by your monstrous minion have a penalty equal to your proficiency bonus on the save.

entries.name: Extra Legs
entries: Your monstrous minion's Speed increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Fine Stitchwork
entries: Your stitchwork is barely noticeable and with a heavy coat or similar accoutrements your monstrous minion can pass for a living creature. When you graft this modificaiton, make a {@skill Magiscience} check. Creatures must make a {@skill Perception} check with a DC equal to your check result to visually determine that your minion is a construct.

entries.name: Grafted Spell Invention
entries: When you prepare your spell inventions and modifications to your monstrous minion, you may choose to graft a spell invention into the monstrous minion. You may order your monstrous minion to cast a spell using its grafted spell invention, governed by your Intelligence instead of its own. You must roll a fizzle die as normal. Any grafted spell inventions are destroyed if the monstrous minion is destroyed.

entries.name: Grafted Weapon
entries: When you prepare your spell inventions and modifications to your monstrous minion, you may choose to graft a melee weapon onto one of its arms in place of a hand. The melee weapon must be able to be wielded with one hand. Your monstrous minion gains an attack with the grafted weapon, which it becomes proficient with. You may retrieve weapons grafted in this way the next time you prepare modifications for this monstrous minion by removing it, or from its remains if it is destroyed.

entries.name: Infectious Bite
entries: Your monstrous minion's Bite attack causes an additional {@dice 1d4} ongoing necrotic harm on a hit that persists, but does not stack, until the target regains hit points or is medically administered to with a {@dc 15} {@skill Medicine} check. This harm increases to {@dice 2d4} when you reach 11th level.

entries.name: Shocking Coils
entries: Your monstrous minion has spearking coils protruding from its back. A creature suffers {@dice 1d6} lightning harm when it hits your monstrous minion with a melee weapon attack. This damage increases to {@dice 2d6} when you reach 11th level.

entries.name: Thicker Skin
entries: Your monstrous minion's armour increases by 2.

entries.name: Vigor Mortis
entries: Your monstrous minion can take the Dash action as a bonus action. It can't do so again until it moves 0 feet on its turn.

subclassFeature.name: Stitcher Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Stitcher Artificers have access to the following major effect options:

items: If the triggering attack dealt damage, your monstrous minion's next attack before your next turn deals additional damage of your attack's base type equal to your level. Otherwise, your monstrous minion gains +1 to all defences for 1 round.
Instead of you gaining this major effect option, your Monstrous Minion does instead: Your temporary hit points increase by an amount equal to double your master's level.

subclassFeature.name: Exuding Gas
entries: Starting at tier 2, you can have your monstrous minion release foul gases from its body that can be detonated by your {@spell corpse explosion} spell. This allows you to target your monstrous minion with that spell as if it were a corpse, but when you cast the spell in this way it cannot {@condition poisoned|poison} creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Dedicated Supplies
entries: At tier 3, you devise special spell inventions that provide dedicated support for your monstrous minion. You gain an additional spell invention each long rest, but the spells cast from it only target and affect your monstrous minion. Additionally, spells cast from this invention with a range of Self affect your monstrous minion instead of you, provided you are adjacent to the minion when the spell is cast.

subclassFeature.name: Advanced Grafting
entries: At tier 4 you've grown tired of wasting so many perfectly good monster parts and have learned how to integrate them into your creations. The following modifications are added to your list of modification options using your Monster Minion feture at the cost of two infusion uses instead of one.

entries.name: Dragon Gullet
entries: Your monstrous minion gains the following breath weapon attack.

items.name: Stolen Breath (Recharge 5-6)
entries: The monster exhales the putrid and infected gullet of a fallen dragon in a 30-foot cone. It makes a reflex attack against each creature in the area, dealing {@dice 1d10} necrotic damage and {@dice 2d10} damage of the type the dragon breathed in life.

entries.name: Giant Muscles
entries: Your monstrous minion's Strength increases by 4. Note that its weapon attacks are governed by Strength.

entries.name: Grim Duplicate
entries: Your monstrous minion has the face and features of the humanoid used to make this modification. When you graft this modification, make a {@skill Magiscience} check. Creatures must make a {@skill Perception} check with a DC equal to the result of your check to visually determine that your minion is not the humanoid used to create it, realising it is a construct on a substantial success.

entries.name: Monstrous Part
entries: Your monstrous minion gains a barbed tail that it can use to fling spikes at enemies as an action. This is a ranged weapon armour attack that targets a creature within 100/200 ft., dealing {@damage 1d4 + 3} piercing damage.

entries.name: Bestial Instincts
entries: Your monstrous minion has advantage on armour attack rolls against creatures adjacent to an ally of the minion that is not {@condition incapacitated}.

subclassFeature.name: Harness Death
entries: At tier 5 you've harnessed death - mostly. You gain the {@spell Inescapable Malady} spell and when you prepare your artificer spells you can prepare it and create a spell invention for it (or forgo it for a modification) in addition to your normal number of prepared spells. This spell counts as a 5th-level artificer spell for you (including for extending its effects) and you can cast it without the usual material components, but a creature you target with it cannot be targeted by this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Artillerist
entries: An artillerist specialises in using magic to hurl energy, projectiles, and explosions on a battlefield. This destructive power is valued by armies in the wars on many worlds. And when war passes, some members of this specialisation seek to build a more peaceful world by using their powers to fight the resurgence of strife.

subclassFeature.name: Artillerist Spells
entries: At tier 1, when you prepare your artificer spells you can prepare certain spells and create spell inventions for them in addition to your normal number of prepared spells. These spells count as artificer spells for you, even if they aren't on the artificer spell list.

entries.caption: Artillerist Spells
rows: {@spell cone of cold}, {@spell wall of force}

subclassFeature.name: Eldritch Cannon
entries: Also at tier 1, you've learned how to create a magical cannon. Using your tools of artifice, you can take an action to magically create an {@creature eldritch cannon} in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of you. Once you create a cannon, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest or until you expend a spell invention to create one. You can have only one cannon at a time and can't create one while your cannon is present.

The cannon is a minion and requires your concentration to maintain, though you cannot lose concentration on it against your will, even if you are {@condition incapacitated}. It disappears if it is reduced to 0 hit points or after 1 hour. You can dismiss it early as an action.

When you create the cannon, you determine its appearance. You also decide which type it is, choosing from Flamethrower, Force Ballista, or Protector. The cannon can only act while you are within 60 feet of it. If you are not, it does nothing on its turns.

subclassFeature.name: Artillerist Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Artillerist Artificers have access to the following major effect options:

items: Your eldritch cannon's armour is increased by 3 for 1 round. If you do not have an active eldritch cannon, you gain the bonus instead.

For 1 round, your eldritch cannon's Flamethrower variant only affects creatures of your choice, its Force Ballista variant ignores half and three-quarters cover, and its Protector variant's range increases to 20 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Resilient Construction
entries: From tier 2, your eldritch cannons are reinforced to withstand physical assaults. They gain 50% resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Conduit
entries: At tier 3, you know how to channel your destructive spell inventions through your eldritch cannon. Whenever you cast a spell with an instantaneous duration that deals damage, you may cast it from your eldritch cannon instead of yourself. When you do so, it counts as if the cannon cast the spell instead of you, and the first damage roll made for it is increased by {@dice 1d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Explosive Cannon
entries: Starting at tier 4, every eldritch cannon you create is more destructive:

items: Damage the cannon deals with its own actions and the amount of temporary hit points it grants are both increased by {@dice 1d8}.
Whenever the cannon reaches 0 hit points it detonates, making an area spell reflex attack against all creatures of your choice within 20 feet of it, dealing {@dice 4d6} force damage.

subclassFeature.name: Fortified Position
entries: By tier 5, you're a master at forming well-defended emplacements using your eldritch cannon:

items: You and your allies have half cover while within 10 feet of your eldritch cannon, as a result of a shimmering field of magical protection that the cannon emits.

Whenever you create your eldritch cannon, you may instead create an {@creature eldritch megacannon}. If you need to expend a spell invention to create the megacannon, you must expend two inventions instead. Any effect that refers to your eldritch cannon also applies to your megacannon.

subclassFeature.name: Annihilator Spells
entries: You gain the following spells at the levels listed. These spells always count as prepared for you, and don't count towards your total number of spells prepared.

entries.caption: Annihilator Spells
rows: {@spell bane}, {@spell corpse explosion}, {@spell false life}, {@spell inflict wounds}
{@spell deadweight}, {@spell lemure transformation}, {@spell ray of enfeeblement}, {@spell shatter}
{@spell bestow curse}, {@spell dispel magic}, {@spell slow}, {@spell vampiric touch}
{@spell blight}, {@spell confusion}, {@spell control water}, {@spell ice storm}
{@spell cloudkill}, {@spell cone of cold}, {@spell contagion}, {@spell insect plague}

subclassFeature.name: Things Fall Apart
entries: At tier 1, your mere touch can cause objects to decay as if with a great passage of time, or simply shatter in an instance. You can spend an action to destroy a small object you are touching, and that no one else is touching. Most magic items, and some particularly resilient materials like adamantine are immune to this. The manner of the destruction varies - rot, erosion, fractures, rust - but generally cannot cause more damage than a person with a sledgehammer spending a round smashing away. This doesn't let you break things faster than usual, it just avoids the need to have a tool or exert yourself.

subclassFeature.name: The Churn
entries: Additionally, starting at tier 1, destruction and death in your presence cascades and feeds on itself.

At the end of each of your turns, if any creature was reduced to 0 hit points within 30 feet of you since the end of your last turn, you can choose another creature within that range, and cause {@dice 1d10} force harm to it. The force harm increases to {@dice 2d10} at 5th level, {@dice 3d10} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d10} at 17th level.

subclassFeature.name: Annihilator Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Annihilator Sorcerers have access to the following major effect options:

items: Immediately trigger the effects of your The Churn feature.
Immediately recover sorcery points equal to your proficiency bonus. Additionally, the next time you cast a spell this combat, you may choose an additional Metamagic option to modify the spell with.

subclassFeature.name: Overwhelm
entries: From tier 3, your cantrips deal full damage on a minor success.

Additionally, you can focus your Things Fall Apart feature on distant massive objects by spending a sorcery point. If you spend a minute concentrating on an object you can see, you can cause it to fall apart in some way that would naturally be possible. The object must be something that has not been attended to by any sapient being for at least a year - something truly abandoned or wild, like a derelict house, a cliff edge upon which no one camps, or a wild drifting glacier - but can be as large as a hundred feet across. The destruction manifests over the course of a minute. This is seldom of use offensively, but serves as a terrifying display of the power that fuels you.

subclassFeature.name: Consuming Vortex
entries: At tier 3, you can manifest the consuming power of the Gyre to draw creatures towards you or towards your magic.

When you cast a spell, you can choose either yourself, or a space within the area of the spell you are casting. Before your spell takes effect, creatures of your choice within 120 feet of that spot must make a save, being pushed 20 feet towards that spot on a failure. After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Unboil an Egg
entries: At tier 4, your understanding of the nature of destruction gives you the ability to undo its damage.
You can spend 1 sorcery point to cast {@spell cure wounds}, heightened to the highest level spell you can cast.

Alternatively, you can spend an action and concentrate on a damaged or destroyed object, then spend 1 sorcery point for every 30 feet across that object is. Over the course of a minute, you reverse the damage to the object, potentially restoring it to its prime. This requires concentration, and if your concentration fails, the restoration fails as well.
This cannot restore things that were reshaped into new items. You can rebuild a shattered boat, but cannot cause the metal in a suit of armour to turn back into an ingot or into raw ore.

subclassFeature.name: The Teeth of the Gyre
entries: At tier 5, you can manifest the heart of the Gyre, which can destroy anything. You can spend a bonus action to create a sphere of annihilation within 5 feet of you, which lasts for one minute. This floating sphere of darkness is a 2-foot-diameter hole in reality that eliminates all matter that it comes into contact with.

Anything that the sphere touches or that touches it that is not instantly obliterated suffers {@dice 2d10} force harm. This harm increases to {@dice 3d10} at 11th level, and {@dice 4d10} at 17th level.
The default state of the sphere is stationary, but should a creature be in the way of a moving sphere it suffer's half the sphere's usual force harm.

While within 60 feet of an uncontrolled sphere of annihilation, you can use an action to make a {@dc 25} Charisma ({@skill Magiscience}) check and take control of it. On a success, the sphere moves in the direction of your choice a number of 5-foot spaces equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). On a failure, the sphere moves 10 feet towards you instead. If a sphere is controlled by another creature, you may wrest away control by using an action and making a Charisma ({@skill Magiscience}) check contested by the controlling creature's. On a success, you take control of the sphere and can utilise it as normal. Other creatures attempting to influence it make {@skill Magiscience} checks and the distance they can move it is based on their Intelligence modifier.

When you create the sphere, you can immediately move it as if you had successfully controlled it, but afterwards the sphere hovers in place until you or another creature spend an action to try to control it.

A unique effect happens when a sphere comes into contact with a planar portal or extradimensional space (such as a portable hole). In the case of such an event, the DM rolls {@dice d100} to determine what happens next: on 1-50 the sphere is destroyed, on 51-85 the sphere simply moves through the portal or into the space, and on 86-100 a rift forms that pulls in all creatures and objects within 180 feet. Each object and creature, including the sphere, reappears in a random plane of existence.
After you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity
entries: At tier 1, you learn the following Channel Divinity options:

items.name: Emissary of Peace
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your presence with divine power. As a bonus action, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to {@skill Persuasion} checks for the next 10 minutes.

items.name: Rebuke the Violent
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to rebuke those who use violence. Immediately after an attacker within 30 feet of you hits a creature other than you with an armour attack, you can use your reaction to make a ranged spell will attack against the attacker that deals radiant damage equal to the damage it just dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Redemption Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Redemption Heralds have access to the following major effect options:

items: Heal a creature within your reach a number of hit points equal to your level. If you are the only creature within your reach and you are on full hit points, increase your temporary hit points by an amount equal to your level instead.
Choose a creature within 10 feet of you. The next time it is targeted by a damaging attack, after you see the result of the attack, you can take that attack instead.

subclassFeature.name: Thou Shalt Not Kill
entries: From tier 1, any damage you deal and any harm you cause can always be non-lethal, even if it usually couldn't.

subclassFeature.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the herald levels listed.

subclassFeature.name: Reprimand
entries: From tier 2, you can punish those who would strike you. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee armour attack, you may spend 1 exertion to cause radiant harm to it equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of the Guardian
entries: Starting at tier 3, you can shield your allies from harm at the cost of your own health. You can use the Intercept reaction for any creature within 10 feet of you, instead of just adjacent creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Protective Spirit
entries: Starting at tier 4, a holy presence mends your wounds in combat. You regain hit points equal to 1d6 + your paladin level if you end your turn in combat with fewer than half of your hit points remaining and you aren't {@condition incapacitated}.
Additionally, the range of your Aura of the Guardian increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Emissary of Redemption
entries: At tier 5, you become an avatar of peace, which gives you two benefits:

items: You gain 50% resistance to damage from other creatures.
Whenever a creature hits you with an armour attack, it suffers radiant harm equal to half the damage you take from the attack.

entries: If you attack a creature, cast a spell on it, deal damage to it by any means, or cause harm to it by any means but features from this subclass, neither benefit works against that creature until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter
