entries: When you select this subclass at tier 1, you learn how to gruesomely take the power from a slain foe to become all the mightier. When you or an ally slays a living creature of a CR equal to half your level or higher, if it has died within the last minute you can use an action to strike into its body, rip out the heart, and take a massive bite. For a number of hours equal to half your level you gain an expertise die on ability checks.

Whether or not you are able to consume the heart of a creature is determined by the DM, and finding the heart of some creatures requires an {@skill Investigation} or {@skill Medicine} check (DC 10 + half the creature's CR).

subclassFeature.name: Heart-Eater Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Heart-Eater Berserkers have access to the following major effect options:

items: The triggering attack deals additional base damage equal to your level, and you recover the same amount of hit points. If you are raging, the added damage and healing is granted to all of your attacks for the remainder of your rage.

The triggering attack deals additional damage equal to triple your level, and you lose a number of hit points equal to your level. If you are raging, the added damage and hit point loss is granted to all of your attacks for the remainder of your rage.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodstriker
entries: At tier 2, you can tap into your fury to control the sanguine flow of a bloody combat. While you are raging, after you hit a bloodied living creature with a weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to do one of the following:

entries.name: Bloodtrack
entries: For the next hour you know the direction and distance to the creature as long as you are both on the same plane of existence.

entries.name: Crimson Deflect
entries: You capture the creature's blood in mid-air, forming a floating shield around you. Until the start of your next turn, your armour increases by an amount equal to half your proficiency bonus.
Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 2, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on weapon damage rolls for its duration.

subclassFeature.name: Bloody Work
entries: At tier 3, you become bloodied at 75% of your maximum hit points instead of 50%. Additionally, while you are bloodied, your attacks deal additional base damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Strike
entries: Starting at tier 4, you twist a strike at just the right moment to turn it into a devastating blow. When you hit with a melee weapon attack against a living creature, you can use your reaction to turn the attack into a critical hit. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. Alternatively, when you use your Heart Hunter feature, instead of gaining its usual benefits, you can choose to regain the use of this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 4, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on saves.

subclassFeature.name: Hard Dying
entries: At tier 5, you grow far beyond the blood that flows through your veins. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while raging, you can use your reaction to remain conscious for up to 1 minute so long as you are not dead. You continue to resolve the effects of dying as usual and begin to track your negative hit points. You immediately die when your negative hit points reach half your hit point maximum. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.
If you die during this effect your body fades away, and {@dice 1d4} hours later you reappear bloodied in the last place you took a long rest, and you cannot use this feature again for a week.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 5, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on armour attack rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Frenzy
entries: Starting when you choose this subclass at tier 1, you can go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do so, for the duration of your rage you can use a bonus action to add your weapon's base damage to the next melee weapon armour attack you make before the end of your turn. When your frenzied rage ends, you suffer one level of {@condition fatigue}.

subclassFeature.name: Berserker Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Berserker Berserkers have access to the following major effect options:

items: You gain a number of rage hit points equal to double your level.
Expend any number of rage hit points you currently have. If the triggering attack is a damaging attack, it deals additional damage equal to the number of rage hit points spent.

subclassFeature.name: Mindless Rage
entries: Beginning at tier 2, you can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} while raging. If you are {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened} when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

subclassFeature.name: Revenger
entries: From tier 3, while you or an ally within 30 feet of you is bloodied, you have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and your damaging weapon attacks deal additional base damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Intimidating Presence
entries: Beginning at tier 4, you can use your action to frighten someone with your menacing presence. When you do so, choose one creature within 30 feet of you. If the creature can sense you, make a will attack against it with the following outcomes:

items.name: Minor success
entries: You gain an expertise die on {@skill Intimidation} checks against the target for 1 round.

items.name: Standard success
entries: The target is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to extend the duration of this effect on the {@condition frightened} creature until the end of your next turn. This effect ends if the creatures ends its turn out of line of sight or more than 60 feet away from you.

items.name: Critical success
entries: As standard success, but you do not need to expend your action to maintain the effect for the first minute of its duration.
If the attack scored a minor success or worse, you can't use this feature on the target again for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Retaliation
entries: Starting at tier 5, when you are hit by an adjacent creature, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Mastermind
entries: Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this subclass, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favours are some of your favourite treasures.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Intrigue
entries: When you choose this subclass at tier 1, you gain proficiency with the Deception, Finesse, and History skills. You also learn two languages of your choice.

Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, enabling you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Tactics
entries: Starting at tier 1, you can use the Help action as a bonus action. Additionally, when you use the action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than adjacent to you, provided the target can sense you.

subclassFeature.name: Mastermind Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Mastermind Rogues have access to the following major effect options:

items: You immediately make a Perception check with DC equal to 8 + the target's CR. On a success, the target is Exposed as per the Ranger's {@classFeature Expose Weakness|Ranger|sns|1} feature for 1 round. If the triggering attack killed the target, you may choose another creature within 60 feet of you to be the target of this effect instead, ignoring line of sight.

If you share a language with the target, the target is {@condition charmed} for 1 round. Otherwise, they are {@condition frightened} for 1 round instead. If the triggering attack killed the target, you may choose another creature within 60 feet of you to be the target of this effect instead, ignoring line of sight.

subclassFeature.name: Silver Tongue
entries: Starting at tier 2, you develop your conversational skills during battle to better deceive others. As an action, you can attempt to deceive a creature within 30 feet of you that can sense you. Make a Deception check contested by the target's Insight check. If you succeed, for 1 round or until you use this feature on a different creature, your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks from the target and any attacks against it aided by your Help action have their success tier increased by 1. If you fail the contest, the target becomes immune to this effect for 1 hour.

subclassFeature.name: Soul of Deceit
entries: Starting at tier 3, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means, unless you allow it. You can present false thoughts by succeeding on a Deception check contested by the mind reader's Insight check.
Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates you are being truthful if you so choose, and you can't be compelled to tell the truth by magic.

subclassFeature.name: Insightful Manipulator
entries: Starting at tier 4, if you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice:

items: Intelligence score
Wisdom score
Charisma score
CR (compared to your level)

entries: At the DM's discretion, you might also realise you know a piece of the creature's history or one of its personality traits, if it has any.

subclassFeature.name: Stifling Distraction
entries: Beginning at tier 5, your tactical assistance in battle improves defence as well as offence. Whenever a creature makes an attack aided by your Help action, the attack's target has disadvantage with their own attacks against the attacker for 1 round.
